During lunchtime, the restaurant buzzes with local office workers, making reservations highly recommended. The interior is spread across two floors, with the first floor featuring counter seating ideal for evening visits, while the second floor offers larger tables perfect for group gatherings and company dinners.

properties.name: The Charcuteria
properties.description: Nestled in Hannam-dong, The Charcuteria offers an authentic European charcuterie (샤퀴테리) experience, a concept that translates to cured and processed meats. This cozy spot, featured on the popular TV show 'Sui Meok Hoe' (수요미식회), is perfect for a relaxed wine gathering with friends. They boast a wide selection of European cured meats like jamón (하몽), prosciutto (프로슈토), and salami (살라미), alongside a curated collection of cheeses and accompaniments, making it a haven for wine and whiskey pairings.

properties.name: The Xef
properties.description: Transport yourself to Spain without leaving Seoul at The Xef, a hidden gem in the Hyochang Park area. Helmed by a Spanish chef, this restaurant offers an authentic taste of Spain, with a menu that hasn't been overly localized. Start with their homemade bread and refreshing Spanish Sangria (샹그리아). Don't miss the incredible Lobster Croquettes (랍스터 크로켓), praised as one of the most perfect fried dishes, and the deeply flavorful Paella Ibérica (아롯 네그로 이비자), which rivals the best found in Spain. The food is seasoned boldly, reflecting true Spanish flavors, with fresh seafood and perfectly cooked rice absorbing rich, complex tastes.

properties.name: 대성갈비
properties.description: Daesung Galbi has relocated to a spacious and bright new location near Ttukseom Station, offering a modern and comfortable dining experience compared to its previous, more rustic atmosphere. The restaurant is well-equipped with excellent ventilation, making it a pleasant spot to enjoy their signature dishes. While the prices are slightly higher than average, the generous side dishes, including a must-try seasoned raw crab (yangnyeom gejang, 양념게장), justify the cost. It's a popular spot, especially with older patrons, and the efficient service, including frequent grill changes, ensures a smooth and enjoyable meal.

properties.name: 단비
properties.description: Experience the refreshing arrival of new housing opportunities in Seoul with 'Danbi' (단비), a name that signifies a welcome shower after a drought. This development brings a significant number of units to the market, addressing the city's ongoing housing supply challenges. With a focus on providing substantial 'general sale' (일반분양) units, Danbi offers a rare chance for residents to secure a home in prime Seoul locations, making it a highly anticipated project for many.

properties.name: The Bakers Table
properties.description: A beloved German bakery with 13 consecutive Blue Ribbon awards, The Bakers Table offers an authentic European experience right in Noksapyeong. This spot boasts an impressive range of breads, from hearty baguettes (바게트) and pretzels (프레즐) perfect for sandwiches, to sweet treats like croissants (크루아상) and eclairs (에클레어). Beyond baked goods, they also serve substantial brunch options, including a must-try tomato soup (토마토 스프) and the namesake 'The Bakers Table' plate, featuring German-style sausages and potatoes.

properties.name: 대장장이화덕피자집
properties.description:
Nestled in the charming Bukchon Hanok Village, this pizzeria offers a unique dining experience blending traditional Korean architecture with a modern, cozy ambiance. The open kitchen allows you to witness the chefs craft authentic pizzas in a high-temperature oven, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere perfect for dates. While the portions might be on the smaller side, the quality and flavor are undeniable.

The star of the show is their traditional pizza, fired at over 480°C for under 90 seconds, resulting in a thin, crispy crust with a delightful chew. The Margherita Extra is a must-try, featuring a generous amount of creamy buffalo mozzarella and fresh basil, elevated by a drizzle of fragrant olive oil. For pasta lovers, the Scallop Cream Tripoline is a standout, with its uniquely textured pasta coated in a surprisingly light, subtly spicy cream sauce packed with fresh seafood.

properties.name: Namsan winery
properties.description: Nestled at the entrance of Gyeongnidan-gil, Namsan Winery is a unique wine bar that has infused a Portuguese spirit into a charming brick house. This long-standing establishment, now a decade strong, stands out by specializing in Portuguese cuisine and wines. It's a place with a distinct identity, offering an authentic taste of Portugal right here in Seoul. The interior, with its subtle pink mood lighting and walls lined with an intriguing selection of Portuguese wines, creates an intimate atmosphere perfect for dates or gatherings.

properties.name: 꺼거
properties.description: Step into Kkeogeo (꺼거) and be transported to a Hong Kong-inspired haven in Yongridan-gil. This popular spot, featured on "Julseoneun Sikdang" (줄서는식당), is known for its lively atmosphere and delicious Cantonese-style dishes. Be prepared for a wait, as they use Catch Table for on-site queuing, but the vibrant ambiance and unique flavors are well worth it. The interior is cozy, with 2-4 person tables, creating an intimate dining experience perfect for sharing a meal.

properties.name: osteria juyeon
properties.description: Osteria Juyeon is a culinary haven that recently hosted the 3rd Seoul Charity Dinner, a heartwarming event organized by Tasty Korea and led by renowned food director Angela Kim. This special occasion saw the participation of national chef Kim Gi-dong, who helms Osteria Juyeon, and was sponsored by Kooksoondang. The dinner successfully raised funds for the Dongbang Social Welfare organization, highlighting the restaurant's commitment to giving back to the community.

properties.name: 꽃,밥에피다
properties.description: Nestled in the charming Bukchon Hanok Village, Kkot, Bap-e Pida is a serene oasis dedicated to healthy, eco-friendly Korean cuisine. This Michelin-recognized gem prioritizes ingredients grown through sustainable farming, offering a refined dining experience that feels both nourishing and luxurious. The ambiance blends traditional Korean aesthetics with modern comfort, making it an ideal spot for a special meal, especially when impressing parents or celebrating significant occasions.

properties.name: 능라밥상
properties.description: This newly opened spot in Sinwol offers a clean and inviting atmosphere, perfect for both a meal and drinks. While the menu features some familiar favorites like LA galbi, the true stars are the North Korean (이북) specialties. The signature Eobok-jaengban (어복쟁반) is a hearty, slow-cooked beef and vegetable stew served in a large platter, ideal for sharing. Don't miss the substantial, meat-filled North Korean-style glutinous rice sundae (이북식 찹쌀순대) and the subtly spicy bibim naengmyeon (비빔냉면), which provides a nice contrast to the milder dishes.

properties.name: 높은집종가산꼼장어
properties.description: Step into a Seoul institution with a 28-year legacy, recently featured on 'The Delicious Guys.' This spot offers a unique 곰장어 (gomjangeo) omakase experience, showcasing the delicacy in various forms from a refreshing jelly to a hearty stew. The star of the show is the grilled 곰장어 (gomjangeo), served in distinct parts like skin, body, and innards, allowing you to savor each texture. Don't miss the accompanying fresh sea cucumber innards (wada) for dipping, a true delicacy that elevates the grilled eel.

properties.name: 나리의집
properties.description: Nestled between Itaewon and Hangangjin Station, Narui Jip is a bustling hotspot famous for its chilled, thinly sliced pork belly, or naengsam (냉삼). Expect a line, especially if you arrive close to its 2 PM opening, as this place is a popular choice for locals and visitors alike. The interior is surprisingly spacious, accommodating many eager diners ready to enjoy the star of the show: perfectly chilled naengsam that's expertly sliced before it melts.

properties.name: GROWERS
properties.description: Nestled in a charming residential area, GROWERS is a beautifully renovated house cafe perfect for dates or catching up with friends. Its Instagram-worthy ambiance, especially during the winter season with festive decorations, makes it a popular spot. The brunch here is a standout, with the "GROWERS Breakfast" being a highly recommended signature dish. This hearty meal offers a generous spread of ingredients, allowing you to customize your own delicious creations, and is served with four different jams to complement the freshly baked bread.

properties.name: GIMISA
properties.description: Tucked away in a quiet alley near Seongsu Station, GIMISA SEOUNGSU (기미사 성수) offers a stylish and urban escape. The cafe boasts a modern, yet calm interior, complemented by a curated playlist and a unique menu that sets it apart from typical coffee shops. Their signature drinks, like the Valrhona Cafe Mocha (발로나 카페모카), are crafted with evident passion and skill by their experienced baristas. It's a perfect spot for a moment of quiet reflection, especially on a rainy day.

properties.name: 경양카츠
properties.description: Nestled near Kyung Hee University, Gyeongyangkats is a popular spot for various kats (카츠) dishes, especially among students. The interior is clean and well-organized, offering a pleasant dining atmosphere. While it can get crowded during the academic year, visiting during breaks means you can often enjoy a meal without a wait. The menu offers several kats options, with the tender pork tenderloin kats (안심카츠) and the classic pork loin kats (등심카츠) being popular choices. Each set comes with a satisfying array of sides, including cabbage salad, noodles, and rice, ensuring a complete and hearty meal.

properties.name: 광화문국밥
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet alley, Gwanghwamun Gukbap offers a refreshing take on traditional Korean comfort food. This Michelin Bib Gourmand recipient is known for redefining the classic gukbap (국밥) with a clear, clean broth and thinly sliced pork, focusing on delicate flavors and textures rather than a heavy, boiling stew. The atmosphere is modern and efficient, with a focus on quick turnover, making it ideal for solo diners at the bar or lively group meals. Expect a vibrant buzz during peak hours, with the clinking of bowls and lively conversation forming the backdrop to your meal.

properties.name: GUSTO TACO
properties.description: Gusto Taco, a multi-award-winning Mexican eatery near Sangsu Station, is a beloved spot for authentic flavors. While the set menus can be a bit overwhelming, the star of the show is undoubtedly their handmade tortillas, so delicious they're practically a dish on their own. Whether you opt for their hearty burritos (which are incredibly tasty but can be quite filling) or their perfectly balanced tacos, the quality shines through. It's a popular spot with a mixed crowd of locals and foreigners, often bustling but without long waits.

properties.name: 군산아구산꽃게
properties.description: This spot is a legendary institution in the Majang-dong crab district, known for its generous portions and fresh, flavorful seafood. The signature mixed stew (seokkeo-jjim, 섞어찜) is a must-try, brimming with succulent crab, tender anglerfish, and plump seafood like octopus and sea squirts. The crab is incredibly sweet and packed with meat, complemented by a clean, spicy broth that's perfect for pairing with drinks. Don't forget to save room for the classic Korean finish: fried rice (bokkeumbap, 볶음밥) made with the leftover broth and topped with seaweed flakes.

properties.name: 계열사
properties.description: Nestled in the charming Buam-dong area, Gyyeolsa is a renowned spot celebrated for its exceptional fried chicken (후라이드 치킨). Despite its popularity and potential for long waits, the crispy, satisfying texture of the chicken is a major draw. The restaurant boasts a classic, cozy ambiance with exposed brick and vintage decor, offering a unique dining experience, especially in its subterranean seating area. It's a must-visit for chicken enthusiasts seeking a taste of a 'Master of Living' (생활의 달인) recognized dish.

properties.name: TXT Coffee
properties.description: Nestled along the picturesque stone walls of Changdeokgung Palace, TXT Coffee is a serene specialty coffee haven in the heart of Bukchon Hanok Village. The cafe's exterior, a charming blend of green and brown, perfectly complements the traditional surroundings. Inside, the intimate space, inspired by the colors of traditional Korean architecture, invites you to focus on the art of coffee. A unique ordering system where you check your menu choices with a pencil adds a touch of playful nostalgia.

properties.name: glt gelato
properties.description: Tucked away in a charming alley near Hongik University Station, GLT Gelato is a hidden gem that locals rave about. This cozy, pink-hued shop, often recognized by its distinctive color and the enigmatic owner, offers a unique gelato experience. While the menu rotates, the pistachio flavor is legendary, described as the benchmark for all other pistachios. Expect a rich, dense texture that defines true gelato, a welcome contrast to many places that misrepresent their offerings.

Escape the biting cold at 17℃, a cozy haven perfect for warming up during Seoul's frigid winter. This spot is ideal for shaking off the chill and enjoying a comforting meal. While specific signature dishes aren't highlighted, the reviews suggest a welcoming atmosphere that makes you feel right at home, offering a much-needed respite from the harsh weather outside.

properties.name: 가봉루
properties.description: Nestled behind the Sejong Center for the Performing Arts, Gabongru is a bustling Chinese restaurant popular with office workers looking for a lively spot for after-work drinks and meals. The spacious interior, complete with private rooms, makes it an ideal venue for company dinners. While the prices are generally reasonable, especially for the lunch sets, be prepared for a slightly higher price point on alcoholic beverages.

properties.name: 갯마을
properties.description: Nestled in the bustling streets near Nonhyeon Station, 갯마을조개터 (Gaetmaeul Jogae-teo) is a lively seafood spot that truly comes alive, especially on weekend evenings. While known for its impressive 조개전골 (jogae jeongol - assorted clam hot pot), the star of the show for many is the generous portion of plump, fresh 굴찜 (guljjim - steamed oysters). The restaurant offers a vibrant atmosphere with ample seating and a well-stocked self-serve bar featuring a variety of banchan (side dishes) and condiments, perfect for customizing your seafood feast.

properties.name: 겐로쿠우동(홍대본점)
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of the bustling Hongdae district, Genroku Udon (겐로쿠우동) is a highly acclaimed udon specialist with a legacy dating back to 2017, consistently recognized in the Blue Ribbon Survey. This establishment has graced numerous television programs, including "The Master of Living" (생활의 달인), for its signature dishes like the grilled leek udon. The ambiance is warm and inviting, with a traditional Japanese exterior and a cozy, wood-toned interior offering views of the vibrant street below. It's also a great spot for solo diners, with ample single seating and a convenient kiosk ordering system.

properties.name: Deux Amis
properties.description: Step into Deux Amis, a charming Garosu-gil cafe named "Two Friends" in French, reflecting its origins from two chefs graduated from world-renowned pastry schools. This establishment, a 14-time recipient of the Blue Ribbon award, offers a delightful European-inspired ambiance, with sun-drenched seating on the second floor providing a warm and inviting atmosphere. While the menu features a good selection of teas and coffee with bean choices, the true stars are the exquisite, artfully crafted desserts displayed enticingly at the counter.

properties.name: Restaurant ON
properties.description: Nestled in the vibrant Sharosu-gil area, Citron Office is a chic cafe and dessert haven perfect for a relaxed afternoon. The ambiance is cozy and inviting, with ample seating options including group tables and intimate spots for couples or solo diners. It's a great spot to unwind and enjoy a sweet treat.

properties.name: DRESDEN GREEN
properties.description: Dresden Green is a fine-dining establishment helmed by Chef Park Ga-ram, known from the TV show "Black & White Chef 2." This hidden gem was already a favorite among fine-dining enthusiasts before the chef's television debut. The ambiance is immediately striking, with a quiet elegance upon entering. Guests are often treated to private rooms, offering an intimate dining experience. Chef Park Ga-ram himself may present dishes, sharing insights into the ingredients with a calming narration that enhances the appreciation of each course. The service is consistently praised for its attentiveness and professionalism, making diners feel they are receiving exceptional value.

properties.name: MONGONE
properties.description: Step into Mongone (몽고네) and be transported to a charming European escape right in the heart of Apgujeong. This long-standing Italian gem, helmed by Head Chef Umberto and curated wine selections from Sommelier Kim Sung-jun, offers an intimate dining experience. With cozy private rooms perfect for gatherings and a sophisticated ambiance, it's an ideal spot for those seeking authentic Italian cuisine paired with exceptional wines.

properties.name: 프로간장게장
properties.description: This popular spot, a favorite among international visitors, is conveniently located just a five-minute walk from Sinsa Station. While on the pricier side, the signature ganjang gejang (간장게장) is lauded for its clean, pure flavor, free from any fishy notes. The brine is perfectly balanced, not too salty, and infused with a subtle bay leaf aroma, highlighting the rich roe and innards. The yangnyeom gejang (양념게장) offers a bolder, spicier profile with prominent garlic, chili, and onion notes.

properties.name: 김영모과자점
properties.description: Step into a haven for bread lovers at Kim Young-mo Patisserie (김영모과자점), a renowned bakery established by Korea's first master patissier, Kim Young-mo. With over 40 years of tradition, this spot offers an overwhelming selection of baked goods, from delicate pastries and crusty baguettes to rich pound cakes and celebratory cakes. The inviting aroma of butter greets you at the door, hinting at the high-quality treats inside. Whether you crave sweet indulgences or savory European-style breads, you'll find a delightful array to choose from, making it a perfect stop for a quick lunch or a leisurely coffee break.

properties.name: 정대
properties.description: Chef Lee Jung-dae's eponymous restaurant, Jeongdae, offers an exquisite omakase experience that celebrates the finest seasonal ingredients. The meal begins with a refreshing starter of seasonal sashimi, featuring plump squid and sweet shrimp with a tangy dressing that awakens the palate. Highlights include the tender braised octopus (nitako, 煮蛸), a delicate sea bass steamed in sake (sakamushi, 酒蒸し) with a rich broth, and perfectly grilled cutlassfish (tachiuo yaki, 太刀魚焼き) from Jeju. The sushi course is a masterclass in texture and flavor, with each piece, from the aged sea bream (madai, 真鯛) to the fatty tuna (chutoro, 中トロ and otoro, 大トロ), showcasing meticulous preparation and balanced seasoning.

properties.name: 모노로그
properties.description: Monologue offers a premium omakase experience in Seoul, perfect for special occasions. The winter menu highlights king crab (대게), prepared with minimal adornment to showcase its natural sweetness and delicate texture. The sashimi and sushi courses are equally impressive, emphasizing the pristine quality of the ingredients with precise cuts, ideal temperature, and expert aging. The minimalist plating allows the food to take center stage, creating a focused and elegant dining narrative.

properties.name: 버드나무집
properties.description: Escape the city bustle with a scenic drive to Bird Tree House (버드나무집), a renowned eel specialty restaurant with 30 years of tradition. Boasting breathtaking Han River (한강) views from its private rooms, this spot is perfect for a romantic date or a relaxing getaway. The spacious interior, coupled with the stunning panorama, creates an unforgettable dining experience. It's a well-established eatery, frequently featured on television, and the ample parking makes it a convenient choice for those arriving by car.

properties.name: 송강
properties.description: This well-known spot is a favorite among celebrities, celebrated for its ability to soothe a weary stomach after a night of indulgence. While the restaurant offers both pufferfish (복어) and tidal flat eel (갯벌장어), the star of the show is undoubtedly the clear pufferfish soup, bokguk (복지리). Its refreshingly crisp broth is perfect for a revitalizing meal, offering a comforting warmth that's hard to resist. The pufferfish tangsuyuk (복탕수육) is also a delightful crispy treat, served with a dipping sauce that complements its satisfying crunch.

properties.name: 한일관
properties.description: Hanilkwan, a long-standing establishment in Apgujeong, offers a refined dining experience perfect for family gatherings and special occasions. The star of the show is their Hanu Bulgogi Jeongol (한우 불고기 전골), praised for its tender, high-quality beef and subtly seasoned broth that isn't overly sweet or salty, making it a healthy and sophisticated choice. Complementing the bulgogi are their Seoul Naengmyeon (서울냉면), a Pyongyang-style cold noodle dish with a clean, savory broth and a soft noodle texture that's gentle on the palate. The attentive service from experienced Korean staff further enhances the welcoming and elegant atmosphere.

properties.name: 필경재
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Seoul, Pilgyeongjae (필경재) is a historic Hanok (한옥) estate, recognized as Korea's first designated traditional building. It offers a serene escape, allowing visitors to experience the beauty of traditional Korean architecture amidst a meticulously maintained garden. This venue is a sought-after destination for special occasions like Doljanchi (돌잔치) and Gohee-yeon (고희연), providing a picturesque backdrop for memorable celebrations.

properties.name: 서관면옥
properties.description: Seogwan Myeonok offers a refined dining experience centered around its signature Pyeongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면). The star of the show is the 'han-sang' (한상) set, which pairs the delicate cold noodles with succulent galbi-gui (갈비구이), a traditional Korean grilled beef short rib. This set also includes a variety of seasonal side dishes and jeon (전), making it a complete and elegant meal perfect for special occasions or business gatherings.

properties.name: 미우
properties.description: The Hyundai Seoul is a shopper's paradise, with luxury brands like Prada, Dior, Miu Miu, and Gucci conveniently located on the first floor. Visitors can expect a seamless shopping experience, with helpful staff offering personalized recommendations and allowing ample time to try on various styles. While the allure of iconic pieces like the Lady Dior is undeniable, the Gucci CG Marmont Small Top Handle Bag ultimately captured the heart of one shopper, praised for its elegant design and surprisingly practical size, making it a perfect first luxury purchase.

properties.name: Buvette
properties.description: Step into Buvette Seoul and be transported to a charming European cafe, complete with a signature red awning and outdoor seating. This globally recognized brand, with roots in New York, offers a vibrant atmosphere buzzing with a diverse crowd, from families with young children to elders. The interior boasts a striking, colorful design with a particularly eye-catching bar area. While the lively ambiance can be a bit boisterous, it creates an undeniably exciting mood perfect for a delightful meal.

properties.name: 김인복의광평(삼성본점)
properties.description:
This upscale eatery near Samseong Station, a frequent feature on popular Korean food shows, offers a sophisticated ambiance reminiscent of Jeju Island. It's a must-visit for its premium Jeju black pork, specifically the Nanchukmatdon (난축맛돈), and its exquisite Pyeongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면). The menu also boasts delightful options like yukhoe uni (육회 우니) and charcoal-grilled galbi (숯불갈비), ensuring a memorable culinary journey for all.

With a stylish interior featuring various seating options from bar counters to private rooms, it's perfect for both intimate dates and lively company dinners. The restaurant also proudly serves premium Yebisu draft beer, a rare find in Korea. Its consistent recognition in the Blue Ribbon Survey highlights the chef's 30 years of expertise, making it a standout destination for discerning diners.

properties.name: BLUE by. Filleter
properties.description:
Step into BLUE by.Filleter (블루 바이 필레터), a charming restaurant housed in a renovated detached house, evoking a European ambiance. Chef Jeong Se-wook, with experience from renowned establishments, helms this unique charcoal-grilled seafood concept, a fusion of two previous ventures. The interior subtly nods to its partnership with Johnnie Walker Blue Label, creating a sophisticated space with a dedicated bar area perfect for intimate gatherings.

The menu is a celebration of the sea, with French-inspired dishes and a standout grilled seafood platter. Start your culinary journey with their exquisite scallop tartare (가리비 타르타르), a harmonious blend of textures and complex flavors featuring anchovy sauce, pecorino cheese, and herbs, perfectly complemented by crispy rusks. It's an ideal pairing for a glass of wine, setting the stage for an unforgettable dining experience.

properties.name: GUCCI OSTERIA SEOUL
properties.description: This Italian fine-dining establishment, Gucci Osteria Seoul, has garnered praise for exceeding expectations, with some even suggesting it surpasses its Michelin-starred counterpart in Florence. The ambiance is sophisticated, making it a memorable destination for a special meal. The highlight of the menu appears to be the Abalone (전복) linguine, a simple yet masterfully executed pasta dish featuring a sublime abalone liver sauce and perfectly cooked noodles, elevated by a subtle juniper berry finish. Other notable dishes include the "In Fondo al Mar" with sea urchin and caviar, and the "Carbonada alla Valdostana" featuring Korean beef tenderloin with polenta and Taleggio cheese.

properties.name: 아루히니와
properties.description: Aruhi Niwa is a highly sought-after sushi omakase spot in Yeouido, known for its incredible value and lively atmosphere. Securing a reservation can be a challenge, often requiring quick reflexes and the 'empty seat alarm' feature on Catch Table. The experience is elevated by the incredibly friendly and engaging chefs, who encourage photos and add to the overall fun vibe. While the meal is reasonably priced, be prepared to order a bottle of sake, as it's a mandatory part of the dining experience here.

properties.name: ELPIC
properties.description: ELPIC, helmed by Chef Kim Soo-hong, is a premier destination for meat lovers, specializing in expertly grilled dishes. The restaurant boasts a sophisticated ambiance, perfect for indulging in a serious carnivorous feast. Standout dishes include the grilled abalone (전복) with smoked roe sauce and brown butter, offering a delightful interplay of textures and rich, smoky flavors balanced by fresh accompaniments. For a truly memorable experience, the dry-aged Hanwoo T-bone steak (한우 티본 스테이크) is a must-try, showcasing the exceptional quality and depth of flavor from meticulous aging and grilling techniques.

properties.name: CRAB52
properties.description: Perched on the 52nd floor, CRAB52 offers a luxurious seafood buffet experience with breathtaking city views. The highlight is the sequential presentation of premium dishes, including succulent king crab and lobster prepared in various styles, and a delightful snow crab (대게찜) that guests can order for seconds. The experience is further elevated by the attentive service, with staff bringing dishes directly to your table, and the option to enjoy high-quality Korean beef sirloin (한우로스구이) grilled to perfection.

properties.name: HANSANG THE TABLE
properties.description:
Experience a refined Korean fine dining journey at HANSANG THE TABLE, perfect for special occasions. The ambiance is elegant, with intimate bar seating and private rooms available for groups. Start your culinary adventure with delightful appetizers like crispy dried shrimp and pollack, followed by artfully presented dishes such as seasoned mung bean jelly (cheongpomuk, 청포묵), dried pollock (meoktae, 먹태), and savory pancakes (bindaetteok, 빈대떡). Don't miss their exceptional highball, praised for its sophisticated and clean taste.

The main courses showcase the best of Korean ingredients, from the incredibly fresh catch of the day (nalsaengseon, 날생선) served with a unique beet puree, to tender Korean beef sirloin (hanu chaekkeut, 한우채끝) paired with house-made yak gochujang (약고추장). A standout dish is the zucchini noodle pasta (hobaknamul pasta, 호박나물파스타), a surprisingly simple yet incredibly flavorful creation that reviewers rave about. Conclude your meal with a refreshing Goheung yuzu (Gohung yuja, 고흥유자) dessert, leaving you with a perfectly balanced and memorable dining experience.

properties.name: Restaurant O Y
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Cheongdam-dong, Restaurant O Y offers a sophisticated French dining experience, recognized by the Michelin Guide Seoul & Busan 2025. The ambiance is chic and modern, with warm lighting creating an intimate setting perfect for dates, anniversaries, or business gatherings. While they offer a single, meticulously crafted course menu that changes seasonally, highlights like the earthy mushroom tart (버섯 타르트) and the reimagined Tarte Tatin revisité (타르트 타탱 리비지테) dessert are standout choices.

properties.name: 스시마츠모토
properties.description: Nestled in the backstreets of Cheongdam-dong's luxury shopping district, Sushi Matsumoto offers an exclusive and intimate dining experience with only eight counter seats. The ambiance is sophisticated yet minimalist, evoking a sense of stepping into Japan. Chef Matsumoto, formerly of Josun Hotel's Sushi Cho, meticulously recreates authentic Edomae-style sushi, focusing on traditional techniques and the finest seasonal ingredients. The omakase menu, especially the dinner course, is a journey of exquisite flavors, featuring delicate preparations like steamed abalone (mushiawabi, 무시아와비) with a rich sea urchin sauce (geu sauce, 게우소스) that has a surprisingly creamy, almost risotto-like quality.

properties.name: OSTERIA COTTO
properties.description: Osteria Cotto in Apgujeong offers a sophisticated urban Italian escape, perfect for special occasions or a delightful date night. The ambiance is chic with soft lighting, making it ideal for memorable moments. Start with their warm, crusty ciabatta and move on to the visually stunning burrata cheese salad (부라타 치즈 샐러드) bursting with freshness. The margherita pizza (마르게리타 피자) boasts a uniquely crispy, pastry-like crust, while the truffle pasta (트러플 파스타) delivers rich, creamy flavors with perfectly cooked noodles.

properties.name: 명인밥상
properties.description: Nestled near Exit 4 of Gyeongchong Hospital Station, Myeongin Bapsang is a long-standing favorite in Songpa, recognized for its five consecutive years of Blue Ribbon selections. This Han-sik (Korean cuisine) master's restaurant offers a welcoming atmosphere with ample space, including private rooms perfect for gatherings and a spacious main hall ideal for family celebrations. The accessible design with ramps and an elevator ensures comfort for all guests, from families with strollers to seniors.

properties.name: 영동장어
properties.description: This popular Gangnam spot is renowned for its premium tidal flat eel (gaetbal jangeo, 갯벌장어). While the atmosphere can get lively and is perfect for year-end gatherings, be prepared for a potentially hefty bill, especially if you indulge in premium ingredients like king crab or sea urchin. Reservations are highly recommended, particularly during peak seasons, as walk-ins are rarely accommodated.

properties.name: coffee montage
properties.description: Coffee Montage offers a sophisticated and comfortable atmosphere, perfect for a relaxed coffee break. The U-shaped interior design provides ample space between tables, creating a refined yet stable ambiance. It's a highly recommended spot, praised for its excellent coffee quality and friendly service, making it a standout cafe in the Gangdong area.

properties.name: SMALL BATCH COFFEE
properties.description: This cozy cafe, with a branch in Seoul, is a popular spot known more for its delicious baked goods than its coffee. While the coffee is decent, the pastries are the real stars here, offering a sweet treat for those looking for a break from exploring the city. It's a great place to grab a bite and recharge.

properties.name: The Margaux Grill
properties.description: Step into The Margaux Grill (더 마고 그릴), a sophisticated dining destination on the 7th floor of JW Marriott Seoul. This restaurant offers a culinary experience beyond a simple meal, focusing on premium ingredients and dishes prepared on a state-of-the-art wood-fire grill. The ambiance is enhanced by natural light streaming through floor-to-ceiling windows, offering serene views of the garden, and the unique display of artisan knives adds a memorable touch to the elegant interior.

properties.name: WOLFGANG’S STEAKHOUSE
properties.description: Step into Wolfgang's Steakhouse, a renowned New York institution, and experience the epitome of classic American fine dining. This upscale establishment is celebrated globally for its authentic dry-aged steaks, offering a taste of New York's legendary culinary scene right here in Seoul. The ambiance is sophisticated and elegant, with a luxurious interior featuring mosaic ceilings and walnut accents, creating a timeless atmosphere perfect for special occasions. While the dress code is smart casual, the refined setting invites you to dress up for an unforgettable meal.

properties.name: GOO STK 528
properties.description: GOO STK 528 is a pioneering steakhouse in Apgujeong, renowned for popularizing dry-aging in Korea. They boast an in-house dry-aging chamber, ensuring top-quality beef. The classic, intimate ambiance makes it an ideal spot for special occasions and romantic dates. Don't miss their signature dry-aged bone-in strip steak, cooked to perfection and served on a sizzling hot plate for an extra sear.

properties.name: yanchul seoul
properties.description: yanchul seoul

properties.name: 고도식
properties.description: This popular spot near Songnidangil is a must-visit for pork lovers. Expect a lively atmosphere, especially on weekends, so consider using Catch Table to snag a spot. The star of the show is the signature pork back-rib (돼지 생갈비), expertly grilled by the staff to juicy perfection. Don't miss out on their legendary wood-fired pork rind (장작 껍데기), which takes a bit longer to prepare but is well worth the wait. The array of house-made sauces and banchan (side dishes), like the addictive garlic sesame oil and various jangajji (pickled vegetables), elevate the already delicious meat.

properties.name: WEILOU
properties.description:
Perched on the 34th floor of the Grand InterContinental Seoul Parnas, WEILOU offers a modern Chinese dining experience with breathtaking city views. While parking can be a challenge, especially on weekends, the restaurant boasts ample seating and private rooms perfect for family gatherings and special occasions. The ambiance is sophisticated yet comfortable, making it an ideal spot for a refined meal.

The star of the show is undoubtedly the Peking duck (북경오리), a must-try for any visitor. Other highly recommended dishes include the Szechuan-style lobster with endive wraps (사천식 바닷가재 엔다이브쌈), praised for its delicious flavor that even children enjoy. The menu focuses on clean, well-balanced tastes, ensuring a pleasant dining experience for all.

properties.name: Le pain
properties.description: Le Pain, a beloved bakery from Songridan-gil, has arrived at The Hyundai Seoul, bringing its renowned sweet treats to the bustling department store. While the Yeouido location focuses on exquisite cakes and tarts perfect for gifting, don't forget their original claim to fame: the delicious *bamshikppang* (밤식빵), or chestnut bread. Their strawberry tart is a masterpiece, featuring a crisp, sturdy crust that holds up beautifully to the sweet cream cheese and fresh strawberries, even after a long journey. For a taste of classic French pastry, the vanilla mille-feuille is a must-try, with its impossibly flaky layers and rich, aromatic vanilla cream.

properties.name: 이수부
properties.description: Experience the minimalist elegance of Isubu, a hidden gem that eschews traditional signage for a subtle, artistic entrance. This one-table kitchen offers an intimate dining experience, perfect for special occasions. From the perfectly paired rosé wine to the delightful bread basket featuring ciabatta and rye with cashew pesto, every detail is curated for a memorable meal. Don't miss the exquisite combination of heirloom tomatoes with burrata, the surprisingly delightful cilantro in the abalone salad, and the perfectly cooked cod with apricot sauce. The grilled shrimp are a sweet revelation, and the sea bream with makgeolli (막걸리) sauce is a savory delight. The meal culminates in an incredibly tender sirloin steak, melting in your mouth, followed by refreshing fruit and tea.

properties.name: 기꾸
properties.description: Nestled just a minute's walk from Shinnonhyeon Station, Gikku Tuna offers a refined dining experience perfect for both intimate meals and larger gatherings. The interior boasts a calm yet luxurious ambiance, with seating options ranging from a solo bar to private rooms ideal for business dinners or celebrations. Their extensive menu features a variety of tuna courses, single dishes, and lunch specials, ensuring something for every palate.

properties.name: 포노부오노
properties.description: Nestled in the trendy Dosan Park area of Sinsa-dong, Pono Buono offers a warm and intimate dining experience, reminiscent of an Italian cave with its cozy wood-toned interior. This popular wine dining bar, helmed by the "Hidden Genius" Chef Kim Tae-sung, is known for its vibrant open kitchen where guests can witness culinary artistry firsthand. The menu focuses on à la carte dishes that showcase the chef's creative flair and deep understanding of Italian cuisine, with a strong emphasis on pasta textures and dishes inspired by Italian travels. Be sure to try the signature Pono Bread (포노 브레드), freshly baked in their central wood-fired oven, and the highly acclaimed Aglio e Olio (알리오 올리오) pasta, praised for its perfect al dente texture and aromatic garlic.

properties.name: 윤서울
properties.description: Yun Seoul offers an intimate and refined Korean fine dining experience, recognized with a three-year consecutive Michelin star. The ambiance is private and personalized, with thoughtful touches like reserved name cards and attentive service that adapts to guests' needs, such as adjusting cutlery for left-handed diners. The lunch menu, in particular, showcases a deep appreciation for noodles, featuring unique creations like 'Baekhwamyeon' (백화면) made with wheat and fermented soybeans, dressed in camellia oil and topped with fresh vegetables, and 'Pyeongyeon' (평면), a dish inspired by ancient noodle preparation methods, served with a blend of aged soy sauces and carefully sourced mountain vegetables.

properties.name: Restaurant EVETT
properties.description: Chef Joseph Lidgerwood, an Australian with a background in renowned international kitchens like The Ledbury and The French Laundry, has established Restaurant Evett in Seoul. Initially earning a Michelin star in 2019, the restaurant has since relocated and reopened. Lidgerwood's passion for Korean ingredients shines through, as he actively explores local produce, ferments, and traditional pastes like jang (장) and kimchi (김치). The tasting menu offers a creative journey, starting with intriguing amuse-bouches like a perilla leaf (깻잎) juice and a "harvest"-themed creation featuring acorn flour crackers and smoked mackerel. Dishes like grilled wild asparagus with dried pollack (hwangtae, 황태) and a "garlic and mugwort" (maneul-ssuk, 마늘 쑥) inspired by Korean mythology showcase the chef's innovative approach to local flavors.

properties.name: Perigee
properties.description:
Perigee, a highly-regarded pasta restaurant in Samseong-dong, is known for its exceptional handmade fresh pasta. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with an open kitchen adding to the lively, albeit sometimes noisy, dining experience. The restaurant offers a single-course tasting menu, allowing guests to explore a variety of creative dishes.

Signature dishes include the unique chicken liver eclair (닭간 에끌레어), a surprising blend of savory and sweet, and the Pici pasta with abalone (전복 봉골레 피치면), featuring thick, chewy noodles that perfectly capture the rich sauce. Another standout is the Andarinos pasta with baby octopus (아기 낙지 안다리노스), a rare pasta shape in Korea, offering a delightful texture that complements the tender octopus.

properties.name: 산로
properties.description: Sanro offers an elevated dining experience, recognized by its inclusion in the 2025 Michelin Guide. Helmed by Chef Yoo Sung-yeop, who trained at the three-Michelin-starred Kiku-no-i in Kyoto, the restaurant specializes in exquisite kaiseki (가이세키). The ambiance evokes a serene Kyoto garden, with a refined interior featuring a hinoki (히노끼) counter and a central hearth where chefs meticulously prepare dishes. Reservations are highly sought after, reflecting the restaurant's prestige and the exceptional quality of its cuisine.

properties.name: PAO PAO RESTAURANT
properties.description: Tucked away in a quiet residential area, PAO PAO RESTAURANT (보보식당) offers an authentic taste of Chinese cuisine, steering clear of typical Chinese-Korean fusion. The atmosphere is lively and fun, with upbeat music and a wide selection of baijiu (바이주) to complement the meal. Signature dishes include the renowned Dongpo Pork (동파육), a flavorful and tender braised pork belly, and the refreshing Mala Cucumber Salad (마라한 오이 샐러드) with its zesty, numbing kick. Don't miss the Lion's Head Meatballs (사자두 완자), served in a rich, savory broth, and the T-Smoked Chicken (티스모크 치킨) with its crispy skin.

properties.name: The Italian Club
properties.description: Nestled on the 10th floor of Hyundai Department Store's Trade Center branch, The Italian Club offers a delightful dining experience with a great ambiance, making it a go-to spot for pasta lovers. While weekend evenings can mean a wait, the cozy private rooms offer a tranquil escape from the bustling atmosphere. Start with their warm, crusty bread served with balsamic oil, and don't miss the Caesar salad featuring incredibly tender, sous-vide chicken breast. The classic Margherita pizza is a solid choice, boasting a thin crust generously topped with mozzarella and fresh basil.

properties.name: Kojacha
properties.description: KOJACHA offers a sophisticated Asian fusion experience, blending Korean (한국), Japanese (일본), and Chinese (중국) culinary influences as its name suggests. The ambiance is perfect for a leisurely meal in private rooms, making it an ideal spot for intimate gatherings. A standout dish is the steamed shark's fin (샥스핀찜), and the experience is elevated by the unique touch of hand-ground drip coffee.

properties.name: 이치에
properties.description: Step into Ichie (이치에), a renowned izakaya helmed by Chef Kim Geon, celebrated for his appearance on Netflix's 'The Chef's Table'. The vibrant atmosphere, centered around an open kitchen, buzzes with energy. The menu is a lavish affair, spanning traditional Japanese home-style dishes and izakaya classics, from delicate sashimi to crispy tempura. Don't miss the signature Sashimi Moriawase (사시미 모리아와세) and the exquisite Managatsuo Yuanyaki (마나가츠오 유안야키), a grilled fish marinated in yuzu soy sauce.

properties.name: Imok SMOKE DINING
properties.description:
Step into Imok Smoke Dining (이목스모크다이닝) for a sophisticated yet relaxed atmosphere, perfect for a special occasion. The ambiance is chic and inviting, setting the stage for an elevated dining experience. While specific dishes aren't detailed, the restaurant is known for its expertly prepared barbecue, promising a memorable culinary journey.

This establishment offers a refined setting with attentive service, including professional sommeliers and a welcoming kids' zone, making it suitable for various gatherings. With options for private rooms and bar seating, Imok Smoke Dining provides a versatile and upscale environment for enjoying their signature smoked dishes.

properties.name: Super PAN
properties.description: Super PAN offers a refined and comforting dining experience, perfect for family gatherings or casual meetups. The ambiance is warm and inviting, complemented by exceptionally friendly service that makes every guest feel well-cared for. They focus on healthy, seasonal ingredients, transforming familiar flavors into unique culinary creations that are both delicious and approachable, even for those with specific ingredient preferences.

properties.name: 미가키
properties.description: Step into Migaki (미가키), a refined Kaiseki Omakase experience helmed by Chef Choi Kyu-deok, formerly of Walkerhill Hotel. This intimate Japanese restaurant, whose name means 'to polish and shine,' offers a meticulously crafted multi-course meal that changes daily with the freshest seasonal ingredients. The ambiance is elevated by the chef's evident passion for beautiful tableware, with each delicate dish presented on unique, artful ceramics. Expect a journey through exquisite flavors and textures, where every bite is a testament to culinary artistry.

properties.name: 키이로
properties.description: Securing a reservation at Tempura Kiiro (덴푸라 키이로) is a challenge, but the intimate, 8-seat counter experience is well worth the effort. The staff are exceptionally friendly, guiding you through the process and ensuring a comfortable evening. The omakase (오마카세) menu features a rotating selection of seasonal ingredients, expertly fried to perfection with a light, crispy batter that defies expectations of greasiness. Don't miss the highly recommended Hanwoo beef and shiso roll (한우 안심 시소 말이) as an add-on, a tender and flavorful delight.

properties.name: 당옥
properties.description: This pop-up event at Starfield Suwon brings together talented chefs from the "Black & White Chef 2" competition. Among the highlights is "Dangok" (당옥), helmed by the "Culinary Scientist." While the overall vibe is a bustling food hall experience, "Dangok" offers a delightful snack option. Their "Dashimaki Sando" (다시마끼산도) is a perfect choice for a light bite to satisfy any lingering hunger after enjoying other culinary creations.

properties.name: KAPPO AKII
properties.description: Nestled within the Maison Glad Hotel, KAPPO AKII offers a sophisticated izakaya (일식 주점) experience, blending trendy kappo-style Japanese cuisine with the unique flavors of Jeju. This isn't just a place for drinks; the menu boasts high-quality dishes that are equally satisfying as a full meal. Regulars rave about the consistently delicious and special offerings, making it a must-visit for those seeking a memorable dining experience on the island.

properties.name: 육통령(교대직영점)
properties.description: This spot near Gyodae Station is a must-visit for anyone craving top-tier Korean barbecue. The vibe is lively and perfect for gatherings, with spacious seating and excellent ventilation ensuring a comfortable dining experience. They pride themselves on transparently aged meats, showcasing a dedication to quality that's evident from the moment you see the prime cuts. The extensive selection of banchan (side dishes) and sauces, including unique options like omija sauce and galchi sokjeot (cutlassfish innards sauce), elevates the meal into a personalized culinary journey.

properties.name: Yeong Dong Pochana
properties.description: Step into Yeong Dong Pochana, a stylish eatery in Gangnam that boldly deviates from the typical Thai restaurant formula. Instead of the usual pho and pad Thai, this spot, run by seasoned restaurateurs, offers a curated selection of more exotic and authentic Thai dishes. The ambiance is intentionally modern and hip, reflecting current trends in Thailand, making it a unique destination for those seeking a different culinary adventure.

properties.name: 무니
properties.description: Step into a festive wonderland at PANI D'EM MUNIE, an Apgujeong Rodeo gem that truly shines during the holiday season. The cafe is renowned for its stunning, large-scale Christmas tree that greets visitors, creating a magical ambiance. With a chic rooftop terrace offering city views and stylish interiors, it's a prime spot for capturing memorable photos. While prices are on the higher side, the visually delightful experience and the array of beautifully crafted desserts, including their signature ribbon-shaped pastries, make it a worthwhile destination.

properties.name: MATER
properties.description:
MATER is a culinary gem helmed by Chef Kim Young-bin, whose impressive resume includes stints at Sydney's 'Master,' Denmark's '108,' and the renowned 'Noma.' This restaurant, recognized by the Michelin Guide Seoul 2024, masterfully blends Asian and Nordic influences, with a focus on fermentation techniques. The ambiance, located in a bright, subterranean space, offers a sophisticated yet welcoming atmosphere, perfect for an intimate dining experience.

Signature dishes showcase Chef Kim's innovative approach. Expect artfully presented creations like the 'Beet' (비트), a visually stunning dish featuring fermented mushroom sauce, and the 'Abalone' (전복) with its delicate seaweed and savory oil. The 'Hae-Nam Cabbage' (해남 양배추) is a revelation, transformed into a delightful roll with butter and fish sauce, offering an explosion of umami. Complement your meal with their curated wine selection, including natural wines.

properties.name: 스시사이토쥬욘
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet, upscale residential area, Sushi Saito Juyon offers an exclusive dining experience reminiscent of its Tokyo namesake. The intimate space, designed for deep focus on the chef's craft, features elegant tableware and a serene ambiance, transporting guests to Japan. Reservations are notoriously difficult to secure, with waits extending over a year, underscoring its coveted status. The dinner-only omakase, served in two seatings, is a testament to meticulous preparation and high-quality ingredients.

properties.name: 월화고기(보라매점)
properties.description: This spacious, multi-level eatery near Boramae Hospital Station is a popular spot, often hailed as one of Seoul's top three for samgyeopsal (삼겹살). While the signature pork belly was sold out during this visit, the menu still offered a delicious array of alternatives like moksal (목살 - pork neck), and various grilled ribs and cuts. The restaurant boasts a hip, clean atmosphere, making it ideal for gatherings, and features a convenient self-serve bar for fresh sides and condiments. They even offer complimentary corkage for most bottles, adding to the celebratory vibe.

properties.name: SICHUAN HOUSE
properties.description: Sichuan House offers a sophisticated dining experience near Samsung Station, with a chic interior featuring dark tones and unique chili-themed decor. It's an ideal spot for dates or special occasions, with a menu boasting winter specials like braised pork belly and stir-fried crab meat, alongside popular dishes such as mala tang and guo bao rou. The "White" course menu provides a delightful sampling of their offerings, including a creamy crab soup, sweet mango shrimp, and tender Dongpo pork belly served with buns.

properties.name: MODERN NÜLANG
properties.description:
Step into Modern Nülang and be transported to a chic Shanghai of the 1930s. This stylish Chinese restaurant offers a refined take on classic dishes, blending traditional flavors with Western ingredients for a unique fusion experience. The ambiance is sophisticated yet inviting, making it a popular spot for gatherings and special occasions. 

The menu features creative interpretations of Chinese cuisine. Highlights include the Garlic Pepper Shrimp (갈릭 페퍼 슈림프), perfectly crispy and coated in a savory sauce, and the unique Beef Nanzi Wans (소고기 난자 완스) with its surprisingly chewy texture and juicy interior. Don't miss the Truffle Hot Pot Rice (트러플 핫팟 라이스) for a fragrant twist on fried rice, or the addictive Spicy Dan Dan Noodles (비빔 탄탄면) with its bold, savory heat.

properties.name: 배나무골오리집
properties.description: This spacious restaurant in Yangjae is a fantastic spot for family gatherings, offering ample parking and valet service for convenience. The meal begins with an array of delicious banchan (반찬), including a standout sweet and tangy tomato jangajji (토마토 장아찌) that perfectly complements the main dishes. Appetizers like japchae (잡채) are generously refilled, setting the stage for a hearty feast. The main course features a variety of duck preparations, including a ham-like duck and a more assertively spiced option, alongside perfectly grilled duck barbecue. Don't miss the flavorful kimchi (김치) and the incredibly addictive salted baby shrimp (어리굴젓), which are a perfect pairing with the accompanying hot stone pot rice (솥밥). The meal concludes with delightful extras like crispy yukjeon (육전) and surprisingly delicious fried eggplant (가지 튀김), making it a truly memorable dining experience.

properties.name: 휴135다온반상
properties.description: Nestled within the Apgujeong Hyundai Department Store, this restaurant offers a sophisticated and clean dining experience, perfect for shoppers seeking a delicious meal. The ambiance, with its warm lighting and wood tones, creates a refined atmosphere ideal for dates, family gatherings, or meals with friends. With direct access from Apgujeong Station, it's a convenient and highly recommended spot for those craving authentic Korean flavors.

properties.name: 현우동
properties.description: Nestled in a cozy alley near Hongik University Station, Hyunwoo Dong is a celebrated udon specialist that has earned accolades from the Michelin Guide and Blue Ribbon Survey. This spot is known for its chewy noodles and deeply flavorful katsuobushi (가쓰오부시) broth, creating a comforting and satisfying meal. The ambiance is warm and inviting with a wood-toned interior, perfect for a solo meal or a casual get-together, and the prices are surprisingly reasonable for the quality offered.

properties.name: 홍명
properties.description:
Nestled in the bustling streets of Nonhyeon-dong, Hongmyeong is a highly sought-after Chinese restaurant, even drawing crowds eager to try dishes recommended by celebrity Sung Si-kyung. Be prepared for a potential wait, as the restaurant is popular, and parking is handled via valet service. The ambiance is intimate, with a few private rooms available, making it suitable for various gatherings. 

The signature dishes include the Gganjjajang (간짜장), a dry-style jajangmyeon served with a fried egg, and the Kkuobarou (꿔바로우), a sweet and sour crispy pork dish. While the Gganjjajang might surprise with its less saucy consistency, the Kkuobarou is often praised for its satisfying crunch and flavorful sauce.

properties.name: 흑산도홍어
properties.description: Hong-eo Hanmari, located between Mangwon and Hapjeong stations, is a must-visit for authentic Jeolla-style skate (hong-eo, 홍어). The restaurant offers a unique omakase-style experience, presenting a variety of skate dishes alongside an array of delicious side dishes like seasoned skate salad (hong-eo muchim, 홍어무침), skate pancake (hong-eo jeon, 홍어전), and even braised abalone (jeonbokjang, 전복장). With customizable fermentation levels for the skate, from mild to strong, it caters to all palates. The extensive makgeolli (막걸리) selection perfectly complements the rich flavors.

properties.name: 황소곱창
properties.description: This spot is a must-visit for anyone craving delicious Korean offal. The star of the show is the 'Modum Hanpan' (모둠한판), a platter featuring a variety of grilled intestines like beef tripe (곱창), large intestine (대창), diaphragm muscle (특양), and abomasum (막창), all served with a complimentary spicy beef tripe soup (소뼈해장국). For the truly hungry, an all-you-can-eat option is available for a small additional charge, allowing you to indulge for two hours. The staff are friendly and will guide you through the best way to enjoy each cut, even for first-timers.

properties.name: 현복집
properties.description: Hyunbokjip is a go-to spot in Gangnam for exquisite pufferfish (복어) dishes, especially their prized *chambok* (참복). The thinly sliced *chambok sashimi* (참복 사시미) offers a delightful chewy and crisp texture, while the *chambok skin튀김* (참복 껍질 튀김) is a crispy, chewy revelation, even surpassing chicken skin in its addictive quality. For a refreshing kick, the *minari bok껍질 muchim* (미나리 복껍질 무침) with its chewy pufferfish skin and aromatic minari is a must-try, and the *ponzu bok udon* (폰즈 복어 우동) provides a perfectly zesty and refreshing finish.

properties.name: près de moi
properties.description: Près de moi is a chic boulangerie and sandwich bar in Cheongdam-dong, launched by a renowned French-trained chef. The space boasts a modern French aesthetic with black and white tiled floors and matte gold accents, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere. It's the perfect spot to enjoy high-quality sandwiches and pastries, paired with a selection of champagne, wine, or beer, making it an ideal destination for both casual bites and sophisticated gatherings.

properties.name: Flavors
properties.description: Flavors offers a sophisticated dining experience, perfect for special occasions or corporate gatherings. Guests rave about the complimentary wine or red wine with their meal, and the attentive table-side drink service. The highlight for many is the incredibly fresh, hand-sliced sashimi, and the must-try lobster, praised for its delicious flavor and easy preparation. The grilled section is a standout, with the lamb and beef ribs receiving particular acclaim for their exceptional taste.

properties.name: 한성칼국수
properties.description: Nestled near Seoul Grand Park, Hansung Kalguksu offers a comforting escape, especially on a chilly day. This popular spot is known for its hearty and flavorful dishes, with a particular highlight being their unique, handmade dumplings (mandu, 만두). The restaurant boasts ample parking and a traditional, shoe-off dining area, creating a cozy atmosphere perfect for a satisfying meal after exploring the park.

properties.name: PATISSERIE CREMIEL
properties.description: Nestled near Yangjaecheon stream, PATISSERIE CREMIEL is a popular French-style patisserie run by a Korean-French couple. It's so well-loved that you might encounter queues during peak hours, and special orders for cakes and pastries require advance booking. While they don't offer baguettes, their croissants are a must-try, often described as the best in Korea, especially when enjoyed fresh from the oven. Other delightful options include the queen amand and chaussons aux pommes (apple pastries), evoking a small slice of Paris.

properties.name: 타마유라
properties.description: Tamayura, located within the JW Marriott Hotel Seoul, offers an exceptional sushi omakase experience that has captivated even the most discerning palates. The ambiance is perfect for special occasions, with personalized touches like name-engraved postcards adding a thoughtful element. From the expertly prepared tsumami (appetizers) like smoked Spanish mackerel with shiso and caviar, to the incredibly fresh sushi and a standout sukiyaki, every dish is a testament to the chef's skill and the quality of ingredients. The generous portions and attentive service make for a truly memorable and satisfying meal.

properties.name: Toc Toc
properties.description: Nestled near Exit 13 of Cheongdam Station, TOC TOC offers a sophisticated dining experience, recognized by the Michelin Guide Seoul 2024. The ambiance is immediately welcoming, setting the stage for an exquisite meal. Highlights include their house-baked mini bread and kouign-amann pastry, served with a unique kelp butter, and a refreshing octopus carpaccio. The modern Korean-inspired dishes shine, such as the premium 1++ sirloin yukhoe (육회) elevated with kelp, and a creative taco featuring a large grilled shrimp with harissa spice on a house-made corn tortilla.

properties.name: ZENZERO
properties.description: ZENZERO offers a serene escape with its minimalist Italian-inspired design and a focus on natural flavors. This gelato shop, recognized as a 'Taste of Seoul' spot, provides a tranquil ambiance perfect for enjoying a refreshing treat. The interior, adorned with plants and warm wooden tones, creates an intimate setting ideal for solo visits or quiet conversations.

properties.name: ZEST
properties.description: ZEST at Conrad Seoul offers a refined buffet experience, perfect for special occasions or a sophisticated night out. While not overwhelmingly vast, the selection is curated with high-quality dishes, appealing to those who prefer quality over quantity. The ambiance is enhanced by the spacious dining area and the option for window seats offering views of Yeouido, especially beautiful during sunset. Service is attentive and friendly, adding to the overall pleasant dining atmosphere.

properties.name: Exquisine
properties.description: Exquisine, a Michelin-starred gem in Cheongdam, offers a refined dining experience focused on celebrating the unique essence of Korean ingredients. Chef Jang Gyeong-won masterfully crafts innovative dishes, blending the concepts of 'Exquisite' and 'Cuisine' into every plate. The ambiance is intimate and serene, with only a few tables, making it perfect for a quiet, upscale meal. Service is attentive and thoughtful, with staff going the extra mile to ensure comfort, from guiding guests to their seats to providing blankets.

properties.name: 중앙해장
properties.description: This Yeouido (여의도) spot is a must-visit for those craving exceptional gopchang-jeongol (곱창전골). While the prices are a bit higher than average, the quality and flavor are said to be worth it, with many calling it a "life-changing" dish. The atmosphere is clean and modern, a departure from the original branch, and even offers solo dining options with individual cooking stations.

properties.name: 진미평양냉면
properties.description: Nestled between Hakdong and Gangnam-gu Office stations, Jinmi Pyeongyang Naengmyeon is a hidden gem for Pyongyang naengmyeon (평양냉면) enthusiasts. Despite its slightly tucked-away location, this Michelin Bib Gourmand recipient draws significant crowds, often with a substantial wait, especially on weekends. The interior is clean and modern, having undergone recent renovations. While the star is undoubtedly the Pyongyang naengmyeon, the menu also boasts highly recommended dishes like eobokjaengban (어복쟁반) and jeyuk (제육), with many patrons suggesting that nearly everything on the menu is worth trying.

properties.name: 정인면옥
properties.description: This popular Yeouido spot is a must-visit for Pyongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면). Even for those new to the dish, the broth is surprisingly flavorful, offering a savory depth without being too bland. The chewy noodles and simple yet satisfying toppings of meat and egg create a harmonious bowl, perfect for a hot summer day. It's a Michelin-recognized establishment, so expect a wait, especially during peak season.

properties.name: 쥬안
properties.description: 쥬안 (Ju-an) offers a refined kaiseki omakase experience in Cheongdam, perfect for special occasions and year-end gatherings. While not a sushi or beef omakase, it presents a delightful array of dishes designed to be enjoyed with drinks over several hours. The private room option enhances the exclusive atmosphere, making it a memorable dining destination. Expect generous portions and a sophisticated ambiance that justifies the fine dining price point.

properties.name: y'east
properties.description: Chef Jo Young-dong's contemporary restaurant, Y'east, offers a refined dining experience that beckons for repeat visits. The tasting menu is a meticulously crafted journey, showcasing innovative pairings and textures. Standout dishes include the Tofu (두부) with king crab and caviar, a delicate balance of sweet and savory, and the Herring & Galbi Stone (청어 & 갈비찜) featuring a blue cheese donut that masterfully blends richness with a hint of funk. The Sweet Shrimp (단새우) is another highlight, with its aged sweetness complemented by nutty and umami notes.

properties.name: 은화계
properties.description: Step into Eunhwagye (은화계), a trendy spot specializing in charcoal-grilled chicken and unique chicken cuts. The atmosphere is a blend of modern and casual, reminiscent of a hip butcher shop, with seating options ranging from intimate bar tables for two to larger tables perfect for group gatherings and company dinners. It's a popular choice for both locals and visitors, offering a vibrant dining experience that caters to various occasions, from casual dates to lively celebrations.

properties.name: hulotte
properties.description: Hulotte offers a sophisticated fine dining experience, utilizing exquisite Jean-Neron Laguiole cutlery from southwestern France. The restaurant's unique approach lies in its seasonally changing tasting menus, each crafted around a specific theme, often inspired by historical figures or narratives. Guests receive a booklet explaining the concept behind each dish, transforming the meal into an immersive storytelling journey. The cuisine itself is a masterful blend of French techniques with global influences, creating a deeply satisfying and memorable culinary adventure.

properties.name: Yi Ning
properties.description: Yi Ning offers a refined Chinese dining experience in Cheongdam, perfect for special occasions or a sophisticated night out. The restaurant, which underwent a stylish renovation, boasts a spacious interior with private rooms, creating an intimate atmosphere. It's a popular spot for regulars and even attracts Chinese-speaking groups, hinting at its authentic flavors. The "Tulip Course" is a highlight, featuring dishes like the refreshing "Teukseon Yangjangpi Japchae" (특선 양장피 잡채), a delightful appetizer that awakens the palate with fresh vegetables and a zesty mustard sauce.

properties.name: 우정
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of the Sindang-dong Tteokbokki Town, Ujeong is a beloved, long-standing eatery famous for its fiery chicken feet (dakbal). The vibe is lively and bustling, with locals and visitors alike packing the two-story space, especially late into the night. Be warned: the spicy dakbal (매운 닭발) lives up to its name and is not for the faint of heart, but its addictive, flavorful heat is what keeps people coming back for more.

properties.name: UOVO
properties.description: UOVO is a must-visit for pasta lovers, renowned as one of Seoul's top three fresh pasta destinations. The ambiance is warm and intimate, with dim lighting making it a popular spot for dates and special occasions. An open kitchen allows diners to witness the pasta-making magic firsthand. The complimentary focaccia is a delightful start, boasting a crispy exterior and chewy interior. For appetizers, the beef tartare (비프 타르타르) is highly recommended for its incredibly smooth texture and unique pairing with a subtly sweet milk chip.

properties.name: 왕스덕
properties.description: Step into Wangsduck (왕스덕), a renowned Chinese restaurant in Sinsa, celebrated for its exquisite Peking duck (북경오리). The ambiance is sophisticated, with spacious private rooms and a grand dining hall, making it ideal for special occasions. Witness the theatrical carving of the duck right at your table, a culinary performance that sets the stage for a memorable meal. Beyond the star attraction, the entire menu boasts delicious Chinese dishes, ensuring a satisfying experience for all your dining companions.

properties.name: Hotel Douce
properties.description: Step into Hotel Douce (오뗄두스) in the SFC Mall and be transported to a charming European-style patisserie. This gem, run by a chef recognized in France, boasts an impressive array of delicate pastries, from classic éclairs and mille-feuille to rich chocolates and macarons. It's the perfect spot for a sweet escape, offering a delightful selection of treats that promise a taste of Parisian elegance right in the heart of Gwanghwamun.

properties.name: 외고집설렁탕
properties.description: Once a humble spot that famously declined a TV appearance, Oegojip Seolleongtang (외고집설렁탕) has transformed into a sprawling, upscale establishment. The interior is surprisingly luxurious for a seolleongtang (설렁탕) house, adorned with Michelin Guide Bib Gourmand and Blue Ribbon accolades. While the broth is still praised for its clean, savory flavor and lack of gamey notes, some patrons feel it has become a bit lighter than in its earlier, more intimate days. The menu now prominently features the head meat seolleongtang (머리고기 설렁탕), offering a richer texture alongside the traditional cuts.

properties.name: ESSENCE
properties.description: Essence, a Michelin-recognized French bistro near the Seoul Arts Center, offers a lighter, more approachable take on fine dining compared to its predecessor. It's an ideal spot for a pre- or post-performance date, with a cozy interior perfect for intimate gatherings. The menu features classic French fare with unique twists, including options like escargots and chicken liver mousse (닭간 무스), alongside more familiar dishes like risotto and steak.

properties.name: Allen
properties.description: Allen offers an exquisite French fine dining experience in Seoul, with an omakase-style approach that elevates the traditional tasting menu. The chef's passion for champagne is evident, with the wine pairing featuring three distinct champagnes, and guests can even opt for premium selections like Dom Pérignon. The ambiance is sophisticated, making it a perfect spot for a special occasion, and the attentive service, including unexpected kitchen tours, adds a layer of exclusivity to the meal.

properties.name: Alice Bar
properties.description: Step into Alice Bar in Cheongdam-dong and be transported to a world of vintage charm and intimate ambiance. With its dim lighting, exposed brick, and subtle nods to "Alice in Wonderland," this spot is perfect for a romantic date night or a quiet, private conversation. The "Face to Vase" cocktail, a delightful blend of gin, eucalyptus, sake, mango, and coriander, offers a fresh, floral aroma that truly complements the enchanting atmosphere. The creative and beautifully presented cocktails are a highlight, with staff ready to offer friendly recommendations.

properties.name: Urban Knife
properties.description: Nestled near Apgujeong Station, Urban Knife Imbiss is a hidden gem specializing in authentic German sausages, lovingly crafted by a chef trained in Germany. This "somaek" (소맥) spot, a playful combination of sausage and beer, offers a taste of genuine German fare without breaking the bank. The cozy interior features ample bar seating, perfect for solo diners enjoying a "honmaek" (혼맥) – a solo beer session. Don't miss the Currywurst (커리부어스트), a flavorful sausage dish with a subtle curry aroma, perfectly complemented by crispy fries and tangy sauerkraut. The generous portions and high quality make it a standout in the upscale Apgujeong district.

properties.name: Stick with Me
properties.description: Step into Stick With Me, a handcrafted chocolate brand originating from New York's Soho, now a celebrated name in Korea after being famously gifted by actress Gong Hyo-jin. Each chocolate is a miniature work of art, boasting vibrant, jewel-like exteriors crafted with natural colorants. While not inexpensive, these chocolates offer a unique sensory experience, blending exquisite taste with stunning visual appeal, making them a perfect choice for special gifts or a delightful personal indulgence.

properties.name: SUSHI ARAMIZ
properties.description: SUSHI ARAMIZ

properties.name: 스시상현
properties.description: Experience an exquisite sushi omakase (스시 오마카세) with breathtaking Han River views at Sushi Sanghyun. Located in the Seoul Royal Marina, this branch of the Michelin-starred original offers a serene dining setting with private counter seats overlooking the water and docked yachts. The ambiance is elevated by the spacious interior and the stunning panorama, making it a perfect spot for special occasions.

properties.name: Adore
properties.description: Adore is a trendy spot celebrated for its exquisite fruit mochi (과일찹쌀떡) and artisanal chocolates. Reviewers rave about the fresh fruit fillings, particularly the strawberry chocolate mochi and the unique 'Dubai' flavor. The presentation is consistently described as luxurious, making it an ideal gift option. Beyond the mochi, their chocolates are equally praised for their smooth texture and delightful fillings, offering a sophisticated treat for any occasion.

properties.name: 스시시미즈
properties.description: Nestled in the upscale Dosan Park area, Sushi Shimizu offers an intimate omakase experience led by the charismatic Chef Lee Gwang-yeol. The ambiance is refined, with guests seated directly at the counter, observing the meticulous preparation of each dish. While sake is available, some patrons may opt for non-alcoholic pairings to fully appreciate the nuanced flavors. The sushi here is known for its distinctive, firm, and subtly seasoned rice (shari), which creates a unique contrast with the fresh toppings, particularly the white fish. This bold approach to the rice is a signature element that sets Sushi Shimizu apart.

properties.name: SUN THE BUD
properties.description: Sun the Bud is a bright and airy spot in Cheongdam, perfect for a guilt-free indulgence after a workout. Bathed in natural sunlight, the restaurant offers a serene ambiance ideal for a relaxing meal. A unique perk is the 10% discount for anyone wearing workout attire, making it a popular choice for those who prioritize clean eating and low-carb diets.

properties.name: stay
properties.description: Perched on the 81st floor of the Signiel Seoul, STAY offers a truly elevated dining experience with breathtaking panoramic views of the city. The ambiance is chic and modern, with a special touch of yellow decor that complements the stunning cityscape. While the exact dishes might be a delightful surprise, expect meticulously crafted courses that are as visually appealing as they are delicious. It's the perfect spot for a special occasion or a romantic date, especially if you snag one of the coveted window seats (though an additional fee may apply).

properties.name: 스시코우지
properties.description: Experience an elevated sushi journey at Sushi Kouji, helmed by a chef trained in Tokyo's Michelin 3-star scene. This high-end sushi restaurant offers an exquisite omakase experience, where each dish is a testament to meticulous preparation and premium ingredients. From the perfectly seared flounder fin sashimi (광어 지느러미) to the signature sweet shrimp (도하새우) with uni, every bite is designed to impress. The meal is artfully complemented by a curated selection of sake, chosen to enhance the delicate flavors of the seafood.

properties.name: XINGKAI
properties.description: Xingkai positions itself as a high-end Chinese fine dining experience, nestled in the basement of the Seoul Finance Center building. While the ambiance is pleasant, the restaurant can feel a bit sparse with fewer patrons. The menu offers a range of dishes, with the 'specialty' cold appetizers (xingkai teseong naengchae, 싱카이 특선 냉채) featuring a standout preserved egg (songhuidan, 송화단) that evokes memories of other esteemed establishments. The steamed shark's fin with matsutake mushrooms (sanghaesik jayeonsongyi shakseupinjjim, 상하이식 자연송이 샥스핀찜) highlights seasonal ingredients, though the shark's fin itself is considered decent rather than exceptional, and the sauce is mild. Other notable dishes include the sea cucumber and abalone with kailan (haesam jeonbok kairan, 해삼 전복 카이란), praised for its satisfying textures, and a spicy lobster (maeuun soseu badagajjae, 매운 소스 바닷가재) that offers a unique fried preparation. The meal concludes with a surprisingly grilled matsutake mushroom (jayeonsongi gui, 자연송이 구이) and satisfying, if not groundbreaking, staple dishes like jjampong (짬뽕) and fried rice (bokkeumbap, 볶음밥).

properties.name: Sweets EPI
properties.description: A beloved bakery since its early days, Sweets EPI (스위트 에삐) has maintained its reputation as a premier cake destination. This cozy spot, conveniently located near Hankt Station (한티역), has been a consistent recipient of Blue Ribbon Survey accolades for over a decade, a testament to its enduring quality. While the interior is compact, the focus is squarely on their exquisite cakes and baked goods, making it a perfect stop for a special occasion treat.

properties.name: 신가예촌
properties.description: Step into Shingayecheon (신가예촌), a hidden gem recognized for five consecutive years by Blue Ribbon. This restaurant offers a traditional Korean dining experience, with a cozy, rustic ambiance perfect for family gatherings. Their signature dish, boribyeolgi (보리굴비), is a must-try, praised for its fresh, non-fishy taste. The menu features hearty, home-style dishes like seoksoe bulgogi (석쇠 불고기) and a flavorful gyeojim (지짐) made with dried radish greens, all served with a comforting, unpretentious flair.

properties.name: 슈치쿠
properties.description: Perched on the 58th floor of the iconic 63 Building, Shuchiku offers a breathtaking dining experience with panoramic views of Seoul. This upscale Japanese omakase (오마카세) restaurant is an ideal choice for special occasions like birthdays or anniversaries. The counter seats, which require advance booking, provide an intimate setting, especially if you're lucky enough to have the space to yourselves, as one reviewer did. The attentive service, from coat check to parking assistance, adds to the luxurious feel.

properties.name: 스바루
properties.description: Nestled in Bangbae-dong, Subaru is a celebrated soba (소바) specialist that has earned a coveted spot in the Michelin Guide for nine consecutive years. This isn't just hype; the dedication to craft is evident in every bite. Chef Kang Young-cheol's philosophy of delivering the purest soba experience shines through, with noodles made fresh daily using a traditional 2:8 buckwheat-to-flour ratio. The star of the show is the expertly prepared soba, but don't overlook their comforting Nabe Udon (냄비우동) with its exceptionally smooth yet firm noodles, or the flavorful Duck Soba (오리메밀), where the rich duck broth perfectly complements the chilled noodles.

properties.name: 세야스시
properties.description: This intimate sushi spot offers a refined omakase experience, perfect for a special occasion or a quiet evening out. The chef's attention to detail shines through, from the perfectly seasoned rice to the unique preparations of each dish. Expect a delightful journey through fresh seafood, with standouts like the unique texture of the akami zuke (marinated lean tuna) and the surprisingly delicious smoked mackerel (samchi, 삼치).
