The chef's willingness to accommodate and personalize the experience makes for a truly memorable meal.

properties.name: 서초장어타운
properties.description: Nestled in the bustling eateries near Gyodae Station, Seocho Jang-eo Town is a long-standing gem specializing in freshwater eel. The vibe here is unpretentious and focused on quality, making it a perfect spot for a satisfying meal, especially for those who appreciate a good drink with their food. The restaurant prides itself on using domestically sourced ingredients, including their premium Japanese eel (minmul jang-eo, 민물장어), rice, and kimchi, ensuring a taste of authentic Korean flavors.

properties.name: 소호정
properties.description: This long-standing establishment, known for its traditional Andong-style noodles (Andong Guksu, 안동국시), offers a refined dining experience. The restaurant, which once supplied its noodle-making secrets to the Blue House, maintains a classic ambiance with private rooms suitable for important guests. While prices are on the higher side, the consistent quality and presentation, with dishes served in elegant ceramicware, justify the cost. The standout dishes are the premium Korean beef brisket (Suyeok, 수육) and the signature Guksu (국시), both praised for their deep flavors and perfect textures. Don't miss the addictive pickled perilla leaves (Kkaennip, 깻잎) served as a side dish.

properties.name: 새벽집
properties.description: This 24-hour, year-round Korean BBQ spot in Cheongdam is a go-to for high-quality Hanwoo (한우) beef, especially their famous yukhoe bibimbap (육회 비빔밥). The atmosphere is lively, attracting a diverse crowd from late-night revelers to families, and the maze-like interior with private rooms offers a unique dining experience. Valet parking adds to the convenience in this bustling district.

properties.name: Salon du Bouquet
properties.description: Salon du Bouquet offers an intimate and festive dining experience, especially during the holiday season. The restaurant has transformed into a course-menu-only establishment to showcase the culinary talents of Chef Kim Yu-shin before his departure for studies in Japan. Guests can expect a refined atmosphere, with special attention paid to presentation, including custom menus and elegant table settings. The sommeliers are known for their warm welcome and expert wine recommendations, enhancing the overall celebratory mood.

properties.name: VINHO
properties.description: Vinho, a contemporary dining bar, is the brainchild of Chef Sungbin (from Mingles) and Sommelier Jinho. The name itself, a blend of their names, also echoes the Italian word for wine. Despite a personal health setback, the reviewer found the experience exceptional, highlighting the sommelier's expert and friendly wine pairings. The signature oyster ice cream (굴 아이스크림) offered a surprisingly delightful, creamy taste, even for non-oyster lovers. The standout dish was the carrot sandwich (당근 샌드), praised for its soft texture and flavorful filling.

properties.name: BUTCHER'S CUT
properties.description: Step into Butcher's Cut in Gwanghwamun and discover a sophisticated ambiance, reminiscent of a classic whiskey bar, perfect for intimate dinners or special occasions. The dimly lit interior sets a mood that feels both exclusive and welcoming, making it an ideal spot for a date or a memorable meal. Prepare for a culinary journey featuring expertly grilled seafood and a selection of premium steaks.

properties.name: BICENA
properties.description: Perched on the 81st floor of Lotte World Tower, BICENA offers a breathtaking panoramic view of Seoul, setting the stage for an exquisite modern Korean dining experience. The Michelin-starred restaurant presents meticulously crafted tasting menus, like the 'Guhak' course, featuring a symphony of flavors and textures. Highlights include the crispy yet tender snow crab fritter rolls (daege bugak mari), the earthy truffle-infused sunchoke soup (dwaejigamja juk), and the incredibly tender abalone (jeonbok) served in a unique 'samhap' with octopus and beef. Each dish is a work of art, complemented by attentive service that explains the culinary journey.

properties.name: Piaf Artisan Chocolatier
properties.description: Piaf Artisan Chocolatier is a perpetually popular spot in Apgujeong, renowned for its exquisite handcrafted chocolates. The interior is charmingly intimate, filled with an impressive array of chocolate creations, from bar chocolates and spreads to mousses and macarons. Their signature 'Chocolat Chaud' (쇼콜라쇼) is made to order and offers a rich yet smooth drinking chocolate experience, perfect for a takeaway treat. While the prices and sizes might be considered 'wicked,' the unparalleled luxury and unique flavors, like the raspberry ganache or the sea salt praline, make it a worthwhile indulgence or a special gift.

properties.name: 봉피양(방이점)
properties.description: A Michelin Guide Bib Gourmand recipient, Bongpiyang (봉피양) in Bangi is renowned for its exceptional Pyeongyang naengmyeon (평양냉면). This dish features a rich broth made from beef, pork, and chicken, served with 100% buckwheat noodles that offer a satisfyingly chewy and slightly coarse texture. The toppings, including thinly sliced beef (pyeonyuk, 편육), boiled beef (suyuk, 수육), and pickled napa cabbage (eolgal-i jeolim, 얼갈이 절임), add layers of flavor and texture, making it a delightful experience even for those new to this classic Korean noodle soup.

properties.name: minu.c
properties.description: Minu.c is a chic and modern dining spot in the Yangjaecheon area, perfect for special occasions like birthday gatherings. The interior boasts a clean, black-themed aesthetic, with large central tables accommodating group dining. While offering a diverse menu that caters to various dietary preferences, including seafood and vegetable options, their small plates are particularly noteworthy, standing out as substantial dishes rather than mere garnishes. The restaurant is also recognized as a prime destination for wine enthusiasts, featuring an extensive wine list and offering corkage services.

properties.name: 미우야
properties.description: Nestled near Yangjaecheon Stream, Miuya is a popular spot that draws crowds even before opening. While the menu features many Japanese dishes, the lack of photos can make ordering a bit of a puzzle. However, the wait is often rewarded with delicious food, like the creamy mentaiko cream udon (명란 크림 우동) and the unique soba sushi (소바 스시) with its soft texture. The tempura is also a highlight, with perfectly crispy shrimp (새우튀김) and chewy udon noodles.

properties.name: mille gâteaux
properties.description: Mille Gateaux offers a delightful experience, especially for Earl Grey enthusiasts. Their signature Earl Grey mousse cake (얼그레이 무스 케이크) is a multi-layered masterpiece, featuring a rich milk cream, a lighter mousse, and a deeper Earl Grey flavor at the base. A subtle spread between layers ensures moisture and cohesion, creating a cake that's both decadent and refreshing. It's recommended to pair this exquisite mini cake with an Americano to cut through the richness.

properties.name: 봉우리(역삼본점)
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet alley near Yeoksam Station, Bong Uri (봉우리) is a distinguished Hanjeongsik (한정식) restaurant recognized in the 'Seoul Food 100' list. The restaurant boasts a spacious, traditional Korean architectural style with private rooms, making it an ideal setting for special occasions and family gatherings. Their signature 'Sikgaek Jeong Sik' (식객정식) is a meticulously curated menu inspired by the renowned comic 'Sikgaek', featuring a harmonious blend of meat, seafood, fish, and vegetables from across Korea, offering a delightful and accessible experience for all ages. They also offer special birthday services, including miyeokguk (미역국), perfect for celebrating milestones.

properties.name: 벽제갈비
properties.description: Step into a realm of unparalleled Korean beef at Byeokje Galbi (벽제갈비), a Michelin-recognized establishment that stands out even in the bustling Shinchon area. This isn't your typical all-you-can-eat joint; expect a refined dining experience with impeccable service and a sophisticated ambiance, perfect for discerning palates. The star of the show is undoubtedly their Hanwoo (한우) beef, particularly the 'Hanwoo Teuksang Saengdeungsim' (한우특상생등심), praised for its exquisite marbling, melt-in-your-mouth texture, and a satisfying chew that elevates the premium cut. Don't miss the unique 'Chilmi Sogeum' (칠미소금), a special seven-ingredient salt blend that perfectly complements the rich flavors of the beef.

properties.name: 백리향
properties.description: Perched on the 57th floor of the 63 Building, Baekrihyang (백리향) offers a sophisticated setting with stunning Han River views, perfect for intimate gatherings. This Chinese restaurant was chosen for its delicious cuisine, making it an ideal spot to treat family. The staff are noted for their exceptional kindness and helpfulness, ensuring a comfortable and memorable experience, especially for special events like a baby's first birthday (doljanchi, 돌잔치).

properties.name: 명보당
properties.description: 명보당 (Myeongbodang) offers an intimate fine-dining experience, with its name honoring the owner-chef's parents' former jewelry shop. This Michelin-recognized gem is known for its exclusive dinner-only tasting menu, making reservations a challenge, especially with its growing popularity among celebrities. The chef, initially perceived as reserved, reveals a commanding presence in the kitchen, orchestrating a symphony of flavors.

properties.name: MOTT 32 SEOUL
properties.description: Mott 32 Seoul offers a sophisticated take on modern Chinese cuisine, blending classic Cantonese flavors with Western influences. Located conveniently via an elevator from Famille Station, this upscale restaurant is perfect for special occasions and group gatherings. Be sure to pre-order their signature Peking Duck (북경오리), a must-try for its crispy skin and tender meat, often carved tableside. Other highlights include the succulent Honey-Glazed Iberico Pork Belly (꿀소스 이베리코 차슈) that melts in your mouth and the innovative Mango Cream Prawns (망고크림새우) with a delightful sweet and savory profile.

properties.name: MAISON M'O
properties.description:
Step into MAISON M'O and be greeted by a dazzling display of exquisite pastries, from delicate madeleines to vibrant petit gateaux. The atmosphere is bright and welcoming, with friendly staff eager to guide you through their tempting selection. While the millefeuille is a crowd favorite, don't miss the chance to try their renowned madeleines, especially the salted milk chocolate variety, which are a testament to the bakery's dedication to quality ingredients and masterful technique. 

For a truly unique experience, the Pain Perdu is a must-try. This elevated take on French toast features a brioche base soaked in a rich custard, pan-fried to perfection, and topped with a delightful combination of brûléed lemon mousse and a savory sesame paste. The interplay of sweet and salty, creamy and crisp, makes this a dessert that lingers in memory long after the last bite. MAISON M'O is a delightful destination for anyone seeking artisanal sweets crafted with passion and precision.

properties.name: 만발
properties.description: Nestled near the Gangseo-gu Office, Sonkkeut-eseo Manbalhada (손끝에서 만발하다) is a charming floral shop with a warm, wooden exterior. This 24-hour establishment stands out with its unique fresh flower vending machine, perfect for those last-minute floral needs. Inside, you'll find a vibrant display, especially during peak seasons like Parents' Day, with a focus on beautiful carnations (카네이션) alongside a diverse selection of other fresh blooms kept in a refrigerated display. Customers can easily choose designs from a QR code catalog and have custom arrangements, like a stunning pink flower basket, created on the spot in about 20 minutes.

properties.name: Malta Tuna
properties.description: Nestled in Daechi-dong, Malta Tuna is renowned as one of Seoul's top three tuna destinations. The restaurant offers a serene dining experience with private rooms, making it ideal for quiet meals and special occasions. Reviewers rave about the impeccable quality and freshness of the tuna, with expert staff guiding diners on the best ways to enjoy each cut. Beyond the star attraction, expect delightful surprises like a standout 간장게장 (ganjang gejang - soy-marinated raw crab) that could be a meal in itself, and a satisfying conclusion with bibimbap and maeuntang (spicy fish stew).

properties.name: 무등산
properties.description: Escape the city and embark on an adventure to Mudeungsan National Park, easily accessible via public transport from Seoul. The trail from Jeungsimsa Temple entrance offers a moderate hike, shaded by lush trees, making it a refreshing escape even on hot summer days. Along the way, you'll encounter the ancient 'Mind Guardian Tree' (당산나무) and reach viewpoints like Jungmeorijae (중머리재) and Jangbuljae (장불재), offering breathtaking panoramic views. Don't miss the unique columnar jointing formations at Ipseokdae (입석대), a testament to ancient volcanic activity.

properties.name: 만두집
properties.description: This popular spot, originally famous in China, offers an authentic dumpling experience right before your eyes. The handmade dumplings (손만두) are a must-try, with the plump 'Tongsaeu Samsun Mandu' (통새우삼선만두) featuring a delightful mix of savory flavors and shrimp. The clean and spacious interior makes it a great choice for a date, and with a variety of side dishes and drinks, it's also a perfect place to unwind with a drink.

properties.name: Trattoria Rialto
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Cheongdam, Trattoria Rialto offers an unexpectedly delightful Italian dining experience with incredible value. The ambiance is chic yet cozy, making it a perfect spot for special occasions or a relaxed meal. Their signature pasta course, especially at the attractive price point, is a must-try, featuring a delightful antipasti with burrata cheese, arugula, and prosciutto, followed by a choice of five distinct pasta dishes. Don't miss the chance to try their Tiramisu or the gelato for a sweet finish.

properties.name: MAMALEE DINING
properties.description: MAMALEE DINING offers a sophisticated Korean dining experience, blending the renowned expertise of Mingles and Mamalee Market. This upscale establishment in IFC Mall provides a spacious and comfortable setting, perfect for business lunches or special gatherings. They elevate familiar Korean dishes like bulgogi (불고기) and yukhoe bibimbap (육회비빔밥) by incorporating premium ingredients, such as high-grade Hanwoo beef and artisanal sausages from Sesk Mensel. Don't miss their unique take on pasta, featuring ingredients like low-sodium pollack roe from Sokcho or the flavorful Mamapolitan pasta with Sesk Mensel sausage.

properties.name: MAILLET
properties.description: MAILLET is a renowned French patisserie in the chic Seocho-gu district, famous for its exquisite macarons and traditional French pastries. Even on a rainy weekend, expect a short wait for a table, a testament to its popularity. The interior boasts a charming, salon-like ambiance with comfortable seating and a lovely garden terrace, perfect for enjoying a sweet escape. Their signature Millefeuille Vanille (밀풰이바니) offers a delightful contrast of crispy layers and a refreshing vanilla cream, while the Paris-Brest (파리브레스트) impresses with its rich hazelnut cream and soft choux pastry.

properties.name: Lawry's the prime rib
properties.description: Step into a grand, antique-filled dining room at Lawry's The Prime Rib, a renowned Beverly Hills steakhouse. The unique "L" shaped seating arrangement adds to the classic ambiance. Reservations are highly recommended due to its popularity. The signature Lawry's Classic Prime Rib Dinner offers a set experience, including a tableside-prepared salad with a chilled fork, followed by a prime rib cut fresh from a gleaming cart. The prime rib itself is incredibly tender, served with classic sides like mashed potatoes and Yorkshire pudding.

properties.name: SE CHAMBER
properties.description: SE CHAMBER

properties.name: 마루심
properties.description: Marushim is a refined establishment specializing in Nagoya-style unagi donburi (eel rice bowl), known as hitsumabushi (ひつまぶし). The restaurant boasts a clean and comfortable ambiance, making it an excellent choice for family gatherings or a pleasant meal out. While they offer private rooms, the main dining area is also spacious and well-lit, providing a welcoming atmosphere for all guests. Their signature hitsumabushi (ひつまぶし) is a must-try, offering a delightful culinary experience that honors traditional Japanese flavors.

properties.name: Ryunique
properties.description: Ryunique

properties.name: Ristorante EO
properties.description: Experience refined Italian dining with a view at Ristorante EO, located in The Hyundai Seoul. This fine-dining establishment offers a sophisticated ambiance perfect for special occasions, with window seats providing stunning vistas of Yeouido. The menu features meticulously prepared courses, with highlights including the delicate croaker carpaccio (민어카르파치오), a rich ragu lasagna (라구 라자냐) made with premium Korean beef, and a succulent grilled prime beef tenderloin (한우 안심구이) served with Osetra caviar.

properties.name: SAN
properties.description: This spot offers a surprisingly authentic Korean dining experience, making you feel like you've stepped back into Seoul. The star of the show is the galbijjim (갈비찜), a hearty braised short rib dish that's perfect for sharing. While the prices might be a bit steep, the generous portions and comforting flavors make it a worthwhile indulgence.

properties.name: LÉGUME
properties.description: Chef Sung Si-woo's vegan restaurant, LÉGUME, has earned a Michelin star for its innovative approach to plant-based cuisine. The tasting menu is a symphony of textures and flavors, featuring dishes like a vibrant green pea (완두콩) espuma with surprising accompaniments and a zero-waste vegetable cookie (채소 쿠키) that offers a delightful contrast. Each course showcases a masterful balance of sweet, savory, and tart notes, creating a truly memorable dining experience.

properties.name: 따뚱
properties.description: Nestled within the Riverside Hotel near Sinsa Station, Ttudung is a renowned Chinese restaurant celebrated for its exquisite Peking duck (북경오리). This establishment offers a sophisticated dining experience, with all seating in private rooms, making it an ideal choice for intimate gatherings, business meetings, or special occasions. The Peking duck is a must-try, meticulously prepared using traditional methods and aged for over four days before being slow-roasted for two hours to achieve a tender, flavorful result without any gamey taste. Beyond the signature duck, Ttudung also boasts a diverse menu of high-quality Chinese dishes, including a highly-rated chili shrimp (칠리새우), and offers various course menus that provide excellent value, especially when booked through platforms like Naver or Catch Table.

properties.name: 뜨락
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet Cheongdam-dong alley, Tteurak (뜨락) offers a refined Hanwoo (한우) experience, a stark contrast to its unassuming exterior. Since the 1990s, this esteemed establishment has been a recipient of Blue Ribbon awards, and was famously highlighted on the TV show "Sui Misikhoe" (수요미식회) by comedian Shin Dong-yeop as the place for "life-changing Hanwoo tenderloin." Upon entering, you're greeted by an elegant ambiance, with softly lit tables and the subtle aroma of charcoal, setting the stage for an exceptional meal.

properties.name: La Saveur
properties.description:
La Saveur offers an exquisite French fine dining experience, perfect for special occasions like birthdays. The owner-chef, with 22 years of experience, personally prepares each course, ensuring a meal filled with heartfelt effort and exceptional taste. While the price point might initially seem high, the meticulous preparation, delicious flavors, and beautiful plating of each dish reveal the true charm of French cuisine, offering a distinct experience far beyond a buffet.

The signature dishes, though not explicitly named as single items, are presented as part of a curated course menu. Highlights mentioned include salmon carpaccio, grilled mullet, and choices for the main course such as lamb or Korean beef tenderloin. The experience is elevated by thoughtful touches like complimentary salads for those who opt-in for notifications and a personalized handwritten note, making it a memorable culinary journey.

properties.name: THE GREAT HONG YUAN
properties.description: Nestled within the luxurious Joseon Palace, The Great Hong Yuan offers an elevated Chinese dining experience. The meal begins with a comforting welcome dish of sweet corn congee (초당옥수수 콘지), followed by a trio of exquisite dim sum (딤섬) featuring truffle mushroom, chili crab, and lobster with parmesan. The croaker, olive, and delicacy of the sea soup (올리브 진금사 민어 수프) is a complex and harmonious broth, showcasing tender croaker, shark fin, and abalone.

properties.name: dexter
properties.description: KOTRA deXter, also known as the Cyber Trade Consultation Center, offers reservable meeting rooms perfect for group projects and business needs. The space is clean, professional, and equipped with computers and large screens, facilitating smooth collaboration and information sharing. While Wi-Fi is unavailable due to security reasons, the dedicated computers and ample space make it an ideal venue for teams needing to conduct in-person meetings, especially for tasks like ad registration.

properties.name: The Round
properties.description:
The Round offers a sophisticated and elegant dining experience, perfect for special occasions and year-end gatherings. The interior is impeccably clean and luxurious, creating an atmosphere that feels more like a fine dining Western restaurant than a traditional Chinese establishment, complete with a visible wine cellar. While the presentation is top-notch, with dishes like Peking duck (북경오리) being a visual highlight, the flavors may not appeal to everyone, with some finding the dishes too salty or not quite hitting the mark for the price point.

The standout dishes for many were the more familiar options like the beef tangsuyuk (소고기 탕수육), which was praised for its taste, and the jjajangmyeon (짜장면) and jjamppong (짬뽕) which were surprisingly delicious. While the restaurant excels in ambiance and service, making it a great option for business entertainment or when someone else is treating, diners should be prepared for a premium price for the overall experience.

properties.name: 대치정육식당
properties.description: This upscale Korean beef (hanwoo, 한우) restaurant offers a premium dining experience, blending a butcher shop with a restaurant. The establishment boasts a clean, luxurious interior, reminiscent of a high-end department store's meat counter, and has been recognized on popular food shows like 'Wednesday Food Talk' and 'Good Food Saturdays'. With a focus on top-tier 1++ grade beef, it's a go-to spot for quality meat in the Gangnam area, offering both intimate private rooms and a more communal basement dining space.

properties.name: 대려도
properties.description: Nestled near Seoul's wedding district, Daeryeodo offers a sophisticated dining experience perfect for special family gatherings. The restaurant boasts a private, room-only setting, ensuring an intimate atmosphere for celebrations. With convenient underground parking and an elegant exterior, it sets the stage for a memorable meal. The multi-course menu, featuring dishes like the flavorful 'O-pum Naengchae' (오품 냉채) with its variety of tastes and the highly praised 'Eohyang Tong Hae-sam' (어향 통 해삼) with its addictive texture, is designed to impress.

properties.name: collage
properties.description: Collage, helmed by Chef No Jin-seong, offers a refined French dining experience within the stylish Salon de Ananti. The interior, a blend of warm wood tones and calming blue, creates an inviting atmosphere, complemented by a dynamic open kitchen. Diners can expect creative and approachable dishes, with standouts including an intensely flavored onion amuse-bouche and a tender, flavorful beef tenderloin (ansim, 안심) paired with caviar. The tasting menu showcases a thoughtful progression of flavors and textures, making it an accessible yet sophisticated culinary journey.

properties.name: COMME MOA
properties.description: Nestled in the charming alleys of Haebangchon, COMME MOA offers a delightful escape to France with its inviting ambiance and Michelin-recognized excellence. The exterior, with its warm lighting and red awning, hints at the European charm within. Inside, expect a culinary journey featuring standout dishes like the unique Sea Urchin Crème Brûlée (성게알 크렘 브륄레), a surprising blend of oceanic flavors and sweet caramel, and the show-stopping Beef Wellington (비프 웰링턴), praised for its rich beefy aroma and perfectly crisp pastry. The restaurant's attention to detail extends to its impeccable service and thoughtful presentation, making for a truly memorable dining experience.

properties.name: CUISSON82
properties.description: Nestled along the picturesque Yangjaecheon Cafe Street, CUISSON82 is a French bistro with a decade-long streak of Blue Ribbon recognition. The restaurant offers two distinct vibes: the classic CUISSON82, featuring a charming terrace (pet-friendly!) and a slightly more traditional ambiance, and Le82, its modern sister brand, boasting a sleek interior with bar seating and large windows overlooking the greenery. Whether you choose the cozy private room or a seat by the window, the atmosphere is sophisticated yet relaxed, perfect for a leisurely meal.

properties.name: Guillaume
properties.description: Step into Guillaume (기욤) and be transported to a charming French patisserie right in the heart of Cheongdam. This beloved pink-hued cafe, run by its French owner since 2008, offers an authentic taste of France with its daily baked breads and exquisite pastries. The ambiance is sophisticated yet welcoming, making it a perfect spot for dates, casual meetups, or simply enjoying a moment of indulgence.

properties.name: Casa del Vino
properties.description: Step into La Casa del Vino and be transported by its bold Latin music and warm, inviting ambiance. The interior, adorned in rich reds and golds, offers a cozy yet sophisticated setting perfect for savoring a diverse selection of wines from around the globe. Expert sommeliers are on hand to guide you through their extensive list, ensuring a personalized experience, and their curated food pairings are designed to complement every sip.

properties.name: Gombawie
properties.description: Nestled near Bongeunsa Station, Gombawie is a Michelin-recognized gem that draws crowds, especially after work. While the highly praised beef tripe hot pot (한우곱창전골) can be pricey, the quality is undeniable. The star of the show, however, is the grilled beef entrails, with the grilled large intestine (대창구이) and grilled abomasum (특양구이) receiving rave reviews for their exceptional texture and flavor, expertly grilled by the staff. Don't miss the rich and savory tripe soup (곱창국밥) to round off your meal.

properties.name: 가담
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Apgujeong, Gadame is a renowned Chinese restaurant that has gained significant fame through appearances on popular Korean TV shows and YouTube channels, solidifying its status as a celebrity hotspot. While originally a beloved local eatery, its media exposure has made reservations essential to secure a table. The restaurant offers a refined take on classic Chinese dishes, with a focus on balanced flavors and high-quality ingredients, making it a perfect spot for gatherings with friends and loved ones.

properties.name: 강남진해장
properties.description: This bustling spot near Gangnam Station is a popular late-night destination, often packed even in the early hours. It's known for its hearty Yangseonji Haejangguk (양선지해장국), a rich beef tripe and blood sausage soup. The accompanying kimchi, especially the garlicky geotjeori (겉절이), is a standout, perfect for pairing with the soup or enjoying as a side dish.

properties.name: 갯바위
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet alley near Seoul National University Station, Gaetbawi offers a serene dining experience focused on high-quality ingredients. The restaurant is known for its premium fresh cod soup (daegu-tang, 대구탕), which commands a higher price point due to the use of domestic fresh cod. Reservations are recommended, especially for their signature dish, as they prepare the ingredients meticulously. The side dishes are simple yet flavorful, complementing the main course with a home-style touch.

properties.name: 아야진생태찌개
properties.description:
This bustling spot is a must-visit for serious fans of pollack stew, specifically the premium 'saengtae jjigae' (생태찌개). Reviewers rave about the incredibly tender fish and the superior texture and flavor of the internal organs, especially the roe and milt, which are considered a must-add. For those looking to start their meal, the fresh seafood platter (해물모듬) is highly recommended, offering a delicious selection of sea bounty like sea urchin and abalone, perfect for pairing with a drink before diving into the main event.

The atmosphere here is lively, with plenty of cars parked outside indicating its popularity. While the saengtae jjigae (생태찌개) is pricier than its pollock counterpart, the quality justifies the cost. The side dishes are simple yet satisfying, with a particular highlight being the dried seaweed (gim, 김) perfect for wrapping rice. Don't miss the chance to try the fermented flatfish (gajami sikhae, 가자미식혜) – a delightful appetizer that perfectly complements a drink, setting the stage for the flavorful stew to come.

properties.name: La Main de Chef
properties.description: La Main de Chef offers a sophisticated take on contemporary Italian cuisine, masterfully weaving in the essence of seasonal Korean ingredients. Chef ByungYong Lee, with his unique culinary perspective shaped by studies in Japan and a fusion of Asian and European techniques, crafts dishes that are both fresh and innovative. Since 2017, this restaurant has been a consistent highlight on the Blue Ribbon Survey, a testament to its enduring quality and creative approach.

properties.name: Lisian
properties.description: Lisian is a go-to spot, especially when the weather heats up, drawing crowds for its famed Chinese cold noodles (중국냉면). This upscale Chinese restaurant offers a refined take on classic dishes. The wasabi cream shrimp (와사비크림새우) is a must-try, with its generous coating of creamy, spicy sauce and crunchy walnuts. The sweet and sour pork (탕수육) is also a standout, with a perfectly crispy batter and a balanced sauce that doesn't overpower the tender pork.

properties.name: 우동명가기리야마본진
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Gangnam, this udon specialist offers a sophisticated Japanese dining experience with a unique, multi-level underground ambiance. It's an ideal spot for both solo diners and groups, with private rooms available for those seeking a more intimate meal. The menu boasts a wide variety of udon dishes, and the freshly made noodles are a highlight, with options like the Zaru Udon (자루 우동) being a popular choice. Don't miss out on their crispy Korokke (고로케) as a delightful side.

properties.name: 떡함지
properties.description: Tteokhamji is a highly-regarded tteok (떡) shop, recognized with Blue Ribbon awards for its quality and variety. Located conveniently within the Banpo Hanaro Mart, it offers a wide selection of traditional Korean rice cakes, perfect for gifts, gatherings, or a personal treat. Beyond tteok, they also operate a cafe serving drinks like royal milk tea and chocolate milk, ideal for pairing with your chosen sweets. With 24-hour operation and even an unmanned system after 7 PM, Tteokhamji makes delicious, freshly made tteok accessible anytime.

properties.name: 백년화편
properties.description: Baeknyeon Hwapyeon (백년화편) is a renowned Seoul rice cake shop specializing in fresh, same-day production and delivery. They offer a sophisticated solution for gift-giving, particularly for wedding favors (gyeor-hon dap-rye-pum, 결혼 답례품). Their "Baengnyeon Daprye 6-1" set, featuring rice ball glutinous rice cakes (bab-al chapssal-tteok, 밥알찹쌀떡), black sesame cloud glutinous rice cakes (heuk-im-ja gu-reum chal-tteok, 흑임자구름찰떡), and walnut pumpkin glutinous rice cakes (gyeon-gwa-ryu ho-bak chal-tteok, 견과류호박찰떡), is praised for its universal appeal, elegant individual packaging, and satisfyingly substantial size. The thoughtful presentation, complete with optional custom stickers and a convenient box with handles, ensures a memorable and appreciated gift for any recipient.

properties.name: 호원당
properties.description: Established in 1953, Ho Won Dang (호원당) is a venerable institution specializing in traditional Korean confectioneries, particularly their renowned yakgwa (약과). This spot is a go-to for those seeking authentic, beautifully packaged gifts, especially during holidays. While their yakgwa comes in various forms like mandugwa (만두과), jungmogwa (중모과), and maeyeopgwa (매엽과), they also offer other delights such as dutteok (두텁떡), a sticky rice cake filled with nuts and dates. Many customers praise the traditional flavors that evoke nostalgia, making it a perfect choice for impressing parents and elders.

properties.name: 조광201
properties.description: Step into a hidden gem in Munjeong-dong, where the ambiance transports you straight to a vintage Chinese alley. The dim lighting, subtle incense, and walls adorned with Chinese posters and comic books create an immersive, local vibe. This popular spot, helmed by Chef Jo Gwang-hyo, known as the 'Manhwa-ripped Man' (만찢남), offers an authentic Chinese experience that draws crowds even on weekday evenings. It's the perfect place to unwind with a diverse selection of Chinese liquors and beers.

properties.name: Le Boeuf
properties.description: Nestled in Yeouido, Le Boeuf offers a refined dining experience with distinct lunch and dinner services. Lunch features a delightful one-plate platter, perfect for a quick yet satisfying meal, while dinner transitions into an elegant course menu. The star of the show is undoubtedly the steak, expertly prepared and served with complementary spinach cream sauce and unique pasta. Don't miss the house-made fig jam, especially when paired with an added brioche – a true signature touch.

properties.name: RANJUKU
properties.description: Step into Ranjuku (란주쿠) for a refined izakaya experience, where the ambiance is as carefully crafted as the cuisine. This spot, often found by searching for 'sukiyaki' (스키야키), offers a tranquil, wooden interior perfect for intimate conversations. It's a place that truly shines when you want to savor each dish and enjoy a leisurely meal.

properties.name: Dessertist
properties.description: Nestled near Seokchon Lake, Dessertist is a highly-regarded patisserie recognized by Blue Ribbon and MangoPlate. The ambiance is warm and cozy, perfect for a relaxed afternoon. They are known for their siphon coffee (사이폰 커피), but the real stars are the exquisite, professionally crafted desserts. Don't miss the seasonal strawberry parfait (딸기 파르페), a delightful symphony of ice creams, fruit, and textures, or the Pistache (피스타슈), a perfect balance of pistachio cream and tart strawberry confiture.

properties.name: shoto
properties.description: Shoto is a renowned patisserie specializing in shortcakes, with a history of excellence recognized by Blue Ribbon awards. Founded by a husband-and-wife team with extensive pastry experience, including at Hotel Shilla, Shoto offers a delightful array of seasonal fruit cakes. Their signature Strawberry Shortcake (딸기 쇼토) is a standout, praised for its incredibly soft, animal-fat cream and moist sponge cake. The balance of sweetness is perfect, allowing the fresh fruit to shine, making it a sophisticated treat for any occasion.

properties.name: ALADDIN'S LAMB
properties.description: A true Jamsil Saenae institution, ALADDIN'S LAMB has been serving up delicious halal lamb for over a decade, a testament to its enduring quality in a competitive neighborhood. The restaurant offers a unique, exotic ambiance, often filled with international patrons, making it a distinctive dining experience. With spacious seating and private booth options, it's an ideal spot for gatherings and celebrations, even attracting baseball fans after games.

properties.name: 서궁
properties.description: A true Yeouido institution, Seogung is a beloved Chinese restaurant that forgoes traditional staples like jajangmyeon (짜장면) and jjamppong (짬뽕) to focus on its legendary trio: ohyangjangyuk (오향장육), tangsuyuk (탕수육), and gunmandu (군만두). While all are exceptional, the tangsuyuk (탕수육) is a must-try, especially when ordered 'bokk-meok' (볶먹) style – stir-fried with the sauce – for an unparalleled crispy-chewy texture. Their fried dishes, including the spicy kanpung saeu (깐풍새우), are consistently excellent, making it a perfect spot for sharing a variety of plates with friends.

properties.name: 강남면옥
properties.description:
Nestled in the bustling heart of Myeongdong, Gangnam Myeonok is a vibrant hotspot drawing both locals and international travelers. This eatery is renowned for its signature dishes: the traditional Hamheung-style naengmyeon (냉면), featuring perfectly chewy noodles, and the incredibly tender galbijjim (갈비찜), a braised short rib dish made with natural honey. The rich, savory broth served with the naengmyeon is a must-try, and the galbijjim's melt-in-your-mouth texture makes it a crowd-pleaser, even for children.

The atmosphere buzzes with energy, reflecting its popularity as a go-to destination for authentic Korean flavors. Diners rave about the harmonious pairing of the spicy, flavorful bibim naengmyeon (비빔냉면) with the sweet and savory galbijjim. The restaurant also offers other delights like suyuk (수육) and steamed dumplings (찐만두), all prepared with transparently sourced ingredients. It's an ideal spot to experience the essence of Korean cuisine in a lively setting.

properties.name: 서글렁탕
properties.description: Nestled near Saetgang Station, Seogeulleongtang is a popular spot for its signature chilled pork belly, or naengsam (냉삼). Despite its name suggesting a soup restaurant, this establishment is renowned for its unique take on grilled pork. The meat is marinated in a special sauce before hitting the charcoal grill, offering a flavorful and tender bite. Free soft drinks add to the value, making it a bustling destination with a consistent wait, especially during peak hours. The restaurant's clever seating with built-in coat storage is a thoughtful touch for diners enjoying the smoky aroma of grilled meats.

properties.name: 영동설렁탕
properties.description:
A true 신사동 (Sinsa-dong) institution, 영동설렁탕 (Yeongdong Seolleongtang) has been serving its signature dish since 1976. This beloved spot, made even more famous by the TV show 'Meok-eul Ttaen-de', is a 24-hour operation, making it a perfect late-night or early-morning destination. The atmosphere buzzes with a diverse crowd, testament to its enduring popularity. 

The menu is refreshingly simple, focusing on two stars: seolleongtang (설렁탕) and suyuk (수육). The seolleongtang is known for its distinct, slightly pungent yet deeply savory broth, a flavor profile that has garnered a loyal following. Each table comes equipped with generous portions of kimchi and kkakdugi (깍두기), and a unique pitcher of 'kkakguk' (깍국) – a sweet radish water – adding another layer to the authentic dining experience.

properties.name: BEunusual
properties.description: Step into a world where culinary art meets digital wonder at Beunusual. This unique dining experience transforms with every course, as the ambient media art shifts to complement the dishes. From the tender abalone (jeonbok, 전복) to the rich truffle ravioli and the choice between Iberico presa and premium Korean beef (hanu, 한우), each plate is a masterpiece. The meal concludes with a subtly sweet banana dessert and a selection of fine teas, leaving you eager to return and explore their seasonal offerings.

properties.name: 아선재
properties.description: Nestled near Samseong Station, Aseonjae (아선재) is a highly recommended hanjeongsik (한정식) restaurant, perfect for significant family gatherings like 'sanggyeonrye' (상견례), the formal meeting of prospective in-laws. The restaurant's quiet ambiance, with private rooms, ensures a comfortable and undisturbed dining experience, allowing families to focus on conversation and connection. Its convenient location makes it accessible for guests arriving from different parts of the country.

properties.name: 성민양꼬치
properties.description: Nestled in the vibrant Sharosu-gil area, Seongmin Yangkkochi is a popular spot for those craving perfectly grilled lamb skewers. The restaurant boasts a spacious, multi-level interior, buzzing with patrons enjoying their meals, especially late into the evening. It's the ideal place to unwind with friends over a classic pairing of flavorful yangkkochi (lamb skewers) and a refreshing beer, creating a lively and satisfying dining atmosphere.

properties.name: 청와옥
properties.description: Cheongwaok is a popular spot for friends looking for a hearty meal and a good time, especially for those seeking out Seoul's top-tier sundae-guk (순대국). The spacious interior and ample parking make it an ideal meeting place, even for larger groups. Enjoy a variety of dishes alongside your meal, with a convenient self-serve bar for banchan (반찬) and condiments, allowing you to customize your experience. It's the perfect place to unwind with friends over some makgeolli (막걸리) and delicious Korean fare.

properties.name: TAOYUEN STYLE
properties.description: Taoyuen (도원) is the go-to spot for delicious Chinese cuisine at Hotel The Plaza. Known for its authentic flavors, it's a popular choice for both hotel guests and locals, even boasting a branch called 'Taoyuen Style' at Hyundai Department Store. Whether you're enjoying a meal during a special event or as part of a hotel package, Taoyuen promises a satisfying culinary experience.

properties.name: Imperato
properties.description: Breaking away from the typical image of a restaurant with a view or in a bustling food alley, Imperato offers a surprisingly elegant dining experience. While the reviewer admits to usually preferring more casual eateries, they found Imperato to be a delightful occasional treat. The atmosphere is clean and inviting, with a visible wood-fired oven hinting at the authentic pizzas to come. Sharing a variety of dishes with friends, the table enjoyed a spread that included grilled octopus (문어구이), a rich seven-cheese pizza (치즈몬스터7), and tender Korean beef tenderloin (한우A+ 안심).

properties.name: Le Jiu
properties.description: Nestled in the charming Seocho-gu district, Le Jiu is a decade-old establishment renowned for its exquisite fresh pasta and romantic ambiance. With its soft lighting and wine-friendly dishes, it's the perfect spot for a date night or a leisurely brunch. The restaurant boasts Blue Ribbon Survey stickers for 2023, 2024, and 2025, a testament to its enduring quality. During the holiday season, the interior is adorned with festive decorations, creating a truly magical atmosphere that fills up quickly with patrons.

properties.name: Tombola
properties.description: Tombola is a beloved neighborhood institution in Seorae Village, offering a comfortable and cozy atmosphere perfect for a relaxed meal. While not overly flashy, the dishes consistently deliver above-average, honest flavors. The star of the show is undoubtedly the gnocchi (뇨끼), praised for its tender and chewy texture, with a particularly rich and flavorful sauce that's a hit for those who enjoy creamy, decadent dishes. The steak course is also noted for its good value, making it a solid choice for a satisfying lunch.

properties.name: 진주집
properties.description: A legendary spot in Yeouido, Jinjujip is renowned for its rich and creamy kongguksu (콩국수), a cold soybean noodle soup. The broth is thick and flavorful, made with domestic soybeans, and seasoned just right, eliminating the need for extra salt or sugar. While the price might seem steep, the quality and unique, nutty taste of the meju beans (메주콩) make it a must-try for noodle enthusiasts. The restaurant is spacious, with multiple sections, and the service is efficient, with dishes arriving remarkably fast.

properties.name: 원주추어탕
properties.description: This long-standing restaurant in Banghwa-dong is a highly recommended spot for its authentic Chueotang (추어탕). They pride themselves on using 100% domestic ingredients, including specially sourced radish greens (siraegi, 시래기) from Yanggu and domestic loaches (m بعد). The spacious and clean interior, complete with an open kitchen, offers a welcoming atmosphere for diners, and they even provide amenities like aprons and high chairs for families.

properties.name: 덕원
properties.description: This is a premier ballet academy focused on preparing students for prestigious arts high school (예고) admissions. The academy emphasizes a rigorous, personalized approach, with the director acting as a dedicated mentor. They offer a structured four-step curriculum, from foundational physical analysis to advanced technique refinement based on the Vaganova method, culminating in performance preparation. The academy boasts unique facilities, including Gyrotonic equipment, to enhance student stability and prevent injuries, creating an optimal environment for growth.

properties.name: 길풍식당
properties.description: Step into Gilpung Sikdang (길풍식당), a 40-year-old institution near Seonyudo Station, and feel the warmth of tradition. This unpretentious eatery, bustling with locals during lunch, offers a simple yet trustworthy menu focused on hearty soups and premium meats. Their signature Seolleongtang (설렁탕) boasts a rich, unseasoned broth that allows you to perfectly customize your flavor with salt, pepper, and a hint of their exceptional kimchi. The Suyuk (수육), or boiled beef, is a must-try, praised for its tender, clean-tasting meat that pairs wonderfully with their special soy sauce and fresh chives.

properties.name: 등나무집
properties.description: Stepping into Deungnamujip (등나무집) feels like a journey back in time, a true Seoul institution since 1975. This spacious, Blue Ribbon-winning establishment is renowned for its hearty soups, but the real stars are the succulent braised ox tail (소꼬리찜) and tender boiled beef slices (수육). The meticulous attention to detail, from dedicated kimchi aging rooms to separate cooking stations, hints at the passion poured into every dish. It’s a place that exudes authenticity and a deep commitment to quality, making it a must-visit for those seeking a genuine taste of Korean culinary heritage.

properties.name: 첨벙아구찜
properties.description: Located near Hankti Station, this bustling restaurant is a go-to spot for delicious seafood dishes, especially their signature agwi-jjim (아귀찜). Reviewers rave about the boneless version, praising its tender texture and flavorful sauce. It's a popular choice for both lunch and dinner, with many recommending the generous portions and fresh ingredients. Don't miss out on their crispy minari-jeon (미나리전) as a side, which is a perfect complement to the main dishes.

properties.name: 삼거리먼지막순대국
properties.description:
Step into a piece of Seoul's culinary history at Samgeori Meonjijib Sundaeguk (삼거리먼지막순대국), a beloved "Baengnyeon Gaje" (백년가게) or "100-Year Store." This establishment, recognized as a Seoul Future Heritage, has been serving its signature sundae-guk (순대국) since the 1950s. The rich, clean broth, generously filled with offal, is the star here, drawing crowds even during peak hours. Despite price increases over the decades, it remains a cherished spot for a hearty and affordable meal, often requiring a wait but always worth it.

This no-frills eatery, tucked away in a narrow alley, boasts a legacy recognized by the government and featured on "Korean Table" (한국인의 밥상). While parking is challenging, the authentic flavors and the sheer dedication to preserving traditional tastes make it a must-visit for those seeking a genuine taste of old Seoul. Whether you're dining solo or grabbing a take-out to share with family, the comforting and flavorful sundae-guk is a testament to its enduring popularity.

properties.name: 오복꼬리곰탕
properties.description: Step into a Seoul institution with over 50 years of history, renowned for its exceptional kkoritgomtang (꼬리곰탕). This spot boasts a rich, milky broth that perfectly complements the tender meat, offering a deeply satisfying experience. The simple yet effective side dishes, including perfectly seasoned kimchi and kkakdugi (깍두기), are designed to enhance the star of the show. It's a beloved haunt for local office workers and loyal patrons seeking authentic, hearty Korean comfort food.

properties.name: 대문점
properties.description: Daemunjeom has expanded significantly over the years, now occupying a much larger space than before. While the restaurant's atmosphere has shifted, it holds nostalgic memories for many, particularly the beloved gunmandu (군만두 - fried dumplings) and jangyuk (장육 - cold, sliced meat) that were once savored with soju. The current iteration offers the Munjeong Jeongshik (문정 정식), a lunch special featuring sliced meat, accompanied by various condiments and steamed buns (꽃빵).

properties.name: 소나무집
properties.description:
Step into Sonamujip (소나무집), a beloved institution since 1977, offering a unique twist on a Korean classic. This cozy, home-like eatery is renowned for its signature Cheonggukjang Dakdoritang (청국장 닭도리탕), a savory chicken stew infused with the earthy aroma of fermented soybeans. The rich, flavorful broth is perfectly balanced, complemented by tender chicken and hearty vegetables, making it a deeply satisfying meal.

Beyond the star dish, Sonamujip also impresses with its delightful banchan (밑반찬), especially the crispy, non-greasy potato pancakes (감자전). Conveniently located near popular attractions like the National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art, Gwacheon, it's a must-visit for those seeking authentic, comforting Korean flavors. Reservations are highly recommended due to its popularity and potential for ingredients to sell out.

properties.name: brandnewhimont
properties.description:
Step into Brandnew Himont, a beloved bakery with over 40 years of history near Cheonho Station. While the exterior might hint at its long legacy, the interior boasts a clean, remodeled design. This popular spot is known for its wide variety of breads, with the raspberry baguette (산딸기바게트) and cheese baguette (치즈바게트) being standout favorites. The bakery operates on the first floor, with a cozy cafe space on the second.

Don't miss their signature raspberry baguette, a delightful blend of nutty crunch from embedded nuts and crusts, complemented by a smooth cream filling. Another must-try is the castella tteok (카스테라떡), a soft bread filled with a sweet and creamy castella filling that's been a hit even with those back home. It's the perfect place to grab a delicious treat to enjoy on the go or share with loved ones.

properties.name: CONTE DE TULEAR
properties.description: Prepare for a wait at CONTE DE TULEAR, a popular spot in Apgujeong Rodeo known for its consistently long lines. The ambiance is cozy and inviting, with options to dine indoors or enjoy the open-air seating, complete with cooling air conditioning. Don't miss their signature dishes, including the surprisingly delightful Kimchi Fried Rice (김치볶음밥), elevated with ingredients like brisket and avocado, and the perfectly ripe Avocado Toast (아보카도 토스트) on hearty rye bread.

properties.name: GORDON RAMSAY burger
properties.description: Step into a world of elevated burgers at Gordon Ramsay Burger, where the ambiance is as sophisticated as the cuisine. With a sleek, modern interior featuring black and gold accents and subtle lighting, it offers a comfortable yet luxurious dining experience. The spacious layout and well-spaced tables ensure a private feel, making it perfect for intimate conversations or a refined meal. The open kitchen adds a touch of transparency and excitement, allowing you to witness the culinary artistry firsthand.

properties.name: 압구정진주
properties.description:
This stylish restaurant, rumored to be a favorite of celebrities like Kanye and Bianca, offers a sophisticated dining experience. The ambiance is modern and clean, with spacious seating and private rooms upstairs, making it ideal for dates or group gatherings. Don't miss their signature pork dishes, featuring high-quality samgyeopsal (삼겹살) and moksal (목살) with excellent marbling and texture.

The meal is elevated by an array of delightful banchan (밑반찬), including flavorful seasoned raw crab (꽃게무침), savory rolled omelets, and perfectly seasoned pickles. The attentive service and overall polished atmosphere contribute to a memorable dining experience, solidifying its reputation as a top-tier Korean BBQ destination.

properties.name: mememi GARDEN
properties.description: Tucked away in the stylish Cheongdam-dong district, mememi GARDEN is a delightful surprise, marked by its elegant exterior and a welcoming ambiance. Despite initial assumptions of a typical cafe, the presence of three Blue Ribbons and attentive service, including a staff member greeting guests at the door, hints at a more refined experience. The interior boasts floor-to-ceiling windows and a clean, comfortable atmosphere perfect for a relaxed afternoon, making the friendly service and pleasant environment the standout features.

properties.name: 압구정면옥
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Apgujeong, this spot is a go-to for those seeking authentic Pyeongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면). The broth is described as incredibly rich and savory, clinging to the noodles with every bite. While the prices reflect the upscale neighborhood, the quality and experience are well worth it. Don't miss out on their signature eobok-jangi (어복쟁반), a hearty dish perfect for sharing, and their delicious handmade dumplings (만두) are a must-try, packed with flavorful fillings.

properties.name: STONE+WATER
properties.description: STONE+WATER is a modern cafe in Bangi-dong, Songpa, boasting a sleek black and white interior. It's a perfect spot for a quiet escape, offering a calm atmosphere ideal for enjoying a coffee and some personal time. Notably, this cafe is also pet-friendly, allowing dog owners to relax with their furry companions, even offering a special 'puppuccino' (멍푸치노). Its proximity to Olympic Park makes it a popular stop for those enjoying a stroll.

properties.name: GRAY GRISTMILL
properties.description: Step into Gray Gristmill (그레이그리스트밀), a specialty coffee roastery in Sinsa-dong that elevates the coffee experience. Founded by a national barista champion, this cafe focuses on the 'essence of coffee' with a unique ordering system where you consult with a barista to select your preferred beans from over 10 daily micro-lot and specialty options. The ambiance feels like a sophisticated laboratory, with staff dressed in suits acting as 'coffee concierges,' creating a focused and intimate space perfect for solo enjoyment. They even offer a complimentary plum ade upon arrival, setting a high standard from the start.

properties.name: devastate
properties.description: Nestled in a Cheongdam-dong alley, DEVASTATE is a chic, black-and-white haven perfect for intimate conversations or a stylish date. This roastery cafe boasts an impressive coffee menu, with patrons raving about the smooth, flavorful lattes made with premium beans. Don't miss their signature chewy nut cookies (넛쿠키) – a perfect pairing for your coffee.

properties.name: FIBEVREWING
properties.description: Nestled near Naebang Station, Five Brewing is a local gem known for its expertly brewed drip coffee, though it also shines with its unique take on traditional Korean sweets. The interior offers a chic, slightly dim ambiance, perfect for a quiet escape, with a second floor featuring long tables ideal for working or studying. Don't miss their signature yanggaeng (양갱), a delightful jelly confection available in creative flavors like Udo peanut and Yuzu-Hallabong, alongside other tempting treats like madeleines.

properties.name: COFFEE FIEL
properties.description: Nestled near Sinsa Station, COFFEE FIEL is a spacious and stylish specialty roastery that has maintained its reputation as a top Seoul cafe for 12 consecutive years. The ambiance is sophisticated with a black and gray palette, featuring a large in-house roasting room visible through glass and rotating art installations from collaborations with contemporary artists, giving it a gallery-like feel. With ample seating, including a large communal table and smaller setups, it's perfect for solo visits, meetings, or focused work, complemented by a charming outdoor terrace ideal for enjoying a coffee amidst the city buzz.

properties.name: Within Coffee
properties.description:
Escape the city's hustle and bustle at Within Coffee, a serene haven inspired by Nordic design. This minimalist space offers a tranquil retreat, perfect for moments of quiet reflection. Their meticulous approach to coffee shines through, with the Aeropress filter coffee (에어로프레스 필터커피) delivering a remarkably clear and vibrant flavor, while the cappuccino (카푸치노) boasts a velvety smooth foam that feels like a warm embrace. It's a place where the craft of coffee meets a calming atmosphere, offering a true pause in your day.

Within Coffee is more than just a cafe; it's an experience designed to bring comfort and a sense of calm, especially on a dreary day. The dedication to quality in every cup, from the precise Aeropress extraction to the perfectly frothed milk, builds a deep sense of trust and relaxation. It's the ideal spot to focus on the pure essence of coffee and the understated beauty of the space, making it a valuable choice for a peaceful interlude.

properties.name: February Roasters
properties.description: February Roasters, a multi-year Blue Ribbon recipient, offers a comfortable, medium-sized space perfect for a midday break. While known for its sweet signature coffee drinks, the "4월 크림라떼" (April Cream Latte) offers a pleasant, milky sweetness with a subtle coffee undertone, though the cheese cream flavor is mild. It's a reliable spot for a straightforward coffee experience in the Songridan-gil area.

properties.name: VAULT82
properties.description:
VAULT82 in Cheongdam offers a cinematic and romantic ambiance, making it a perfect spot for year-end celebrations and dates. The restaurant boasts a visually stunning exterior, especially during the holiday season, and an interior filled with festive decorations and comfortable seating. Guests can enjoy a diverse menu featuring starters like abalone risotto (전복 리조또) and main courses such as beef steak (비프 스테이크), complemented by an extensive selection of wines and signature cocktails.

The true magic of VAULT82 unfolds in its B1 cocktail bar, a classic and intimate space that has even been featured on the reality show 'Transit Love 3'. This hidden gem offers a unique experience with creative cocktails, including the intriguing Crispy Bacon (크리스피 베이컨) cocktail, prepared with impressive ice carving and a touch of flair. The plush seating and sophisticated atmosphere make it an ideal destination for those seeking a memorable night out with expertly crafted drinks and a chic vibe.

properties.name: 가네끼스시
properties.description: This long-standing sushi institution, now under the helm of Chef Park Jun-ho, is a must-visit, even filling up on the eve of a major holiday. The omakase experience here is truly exceptional, with generous portions and surprising ingredients like a remarkably large abalone (무시아와비) that sets a high bar from the start. The natural flatfish (광어) sashimi, lightly aged to perfection, and the crispy fried oxtail (옥돔 가라아게) are standout dishes that showcase the kitchen's skill. The inclusion of seasonal hairy crab (세이코가니) with two types of dipping sauces, and the perfectly grilled black cod (은대구 구이), further elevate the value and quality of the meal.

properties.name: 진지아
properties.description:
Step into Jinjia (진지아), an elegant Chinese restaurant helmed by the acclaimed Chef Choi Hyunjin. The ambiance is sophisticated and antique, creating a memorable dining experience that draws patrons from afar. It's a place where culinary artistry is on full display, evident from the numerous accolades adorning the establishment. 

The star of the show is undoubtedly the Mala Gopchang Jeongol (마라 곱창 전골), a rich and deeply flavorful stew that masterfully blends the spicy kick of mala with the savory depth of traditional 곱창 전골. Other highlights include refreshing shrimp dishes with cucumber and crispy fried items, making Jinjia a must-visit for those seeking an elevated Chinese home-style meal.

properties.name: Jean Boulangerie
properties.description:
Jean Boulangerie is a charming bakery offering a delightful array of French pastries and local specialties. From the classic baguette tradition to innovative 'MZ' breads like cruffins, there's something for every palate. Don't miss their signature Niçoise specialties, including the round bread 'banya' (빵바냐) and the onion and olive flatbread 'pissaladière' (피살라디에르). For those with a sweet tooth, the Paris-Brest éclair (파리 브레스트 에클레어) is a must-try, featuring a light hazelnut cream and a surprising chocolate crunch.

This bakery is a treasure trove of baked goods, perfect for a quick breakfast, a satisfying lunch with their quiches and sandwiches, or a sweet afternoon treat. They also offer regional delights like the Tropezienne (트로페지엠), a cream-filled brioche, and the Tourte aux blettes (근대 파이), a traditional Niçoise tart. With its wide selection and delicious offerings, Jean Boulangerie promises a truly authentic French baking experience.

properties.name: Charmandre
properties.description: Charmandre in Songridan-gil offers a delightful escape into British cuisine, challenging any preconceived notions about its flavors. The star of the show is the Beef Wellington (비프웰링턴), a beautifully presented traditional British steak featuring tender beef tenderloin wrapped in mushroom duxelles and prosciutto, encased in flaky pastry. It's a dish that impresses with its visual appeal and harmonious blend of textures and tastes, especially when paired with the accompanying vegetables. For those seeking a lighter yet equally satisfying option, the Mediterranean Vongole Pasta (지중해식 봉골레 파스타) provides a perfectly balanced, slightly spicy counterpoint to the richness of the Wellington, boasting fresh seafood.

properties.name: chick peace
properties.description: Chick Peace in Seongsu offers a delightful Mediterranean and Middle Eastern-inspired dining experience, focusing on healthy and delicious options. This popular salad spot features an extensive self-serve 'mezze' (메제) bar, allowing you to customize your meal with a wide variety of toppings like falafel (팔라펠), hummus (허무스), and red cabbage slaw (레드 캐비지 슬로우). The spacious and clean interior provides a comfortable atmosphere for enjoying your personalized creations, with options ranging from hot dishes to vibrant salads and pita bowls.

properties.name: El Tapeo
properties.description:
Step into El Tapeo and be transported to Spain with its vibrant decor and Spanish tunes. Located near Seoul National University, this spot offers an authentic taste of Spain, perfect for a casual lunch or a night out. Dive into classic Spanish dishes like paella (빠에야), jamón (하몬), and a variety of tapas (따빠스).

The signature dish, 'Casuela Patatas al Lo Pobre' (까수엘라 빠따따스 알 로 뽀브레), is a must-try. This hearty tapa features tender jamón, potatoes, and a fried egg, creating a delightful harmony of savory and rich flavors. It's the perfect introduction to Spanish cuisine for newcomers and a delightful reminder of Spain for those who have visited.

properties.name: bistro MOHING
properties.description: Bistro MOHING, conveniently located a short walk from Nakseongdae Station, offers a sophisticated ambiance perfect for dates or special occasions. With its elegant chandeliers, soft lighting, and spacious seating, including private rooms, it’s an ideal spot for year-end parties and gatherings. The restaurant also provides thoughtful amenities like a coat check and clean restrooms, making for a comfortable dining experience for families and groups alike.

properties.name: 스시욘즈
properties.description: Sushi Yonzu offers an intimate and upscale dining experience, reminiscent of traditional Japanese kaiseki. The lunch omakase, priced at a reasonable 69,000 KRW, provides a generous 18 pieces of sushi. Guests praise the chef's attentive service and the pleasant pacing of the meal, making it an enjoyable culinary journey. The menu features a variety of fresh seafood, with the squid sushi (한치스시) infused with yuzu being a particular standout, celebrated for its unique texture and aroma.

properties.name: Sushi Ai
properties.description: Sushi Ai

properties.name: 김수사
properties.description:
Step into Kim Susa (김수사), a beloved sushi institution in Gangnam that has been serving up traditional Japanese cuisine for generations since 1986. The vibe here is classic and cozy, with a lively counter seating area where you can watch the chefs at work. Don't miss their signature welcome snack: crispy, savory house-made potato chips that perfectly whet your appetite before diving into their omakase (오마카세) courses.

Kim Susa offers a fantastic value, with lunch and dinner omakase sets featuring a variety of fresh, high-quality sushi and sashimi. From delicate white fish to rich tuna, each piece is expertly prepared. The menu also includes tempting options like sea urchin porridge (전복내장죽) and various maki rolls, ensuring a satisfying and authentic Japanese dining experience that honors tradition while remaining accessible.

properties.name: Sushi Go
properties.description: Located conveniently within Tokyo Station, Sushi-Go-Round Kantaro offers a lively and bustling atmosphere, perfect for refueling after a day of exploring. While it's known as a conveyor belt sushi spot, the reality is a modern system where fresh sushi is made to order right in front of you. Expect a diverse menu featuring various tuna cuts, seafood like herring (nishin, ニシン), and even fried options like chicken karaage (唐揚げ). It's a popular choice, so be prepared for a potential wait, especially during peak hours.

properties.name: BORNYON
properties.description:
Step into BORNYON, a stunning Korean wood-fired restaurant that offers a feast for the eyes as much as the palate. Housed in a former wedding hall, the expansive space boasts elegant decor, luxurious furnishings, and an incredible, panoramic terrace with breathtaking city views. The ambiance is sophisticated and chic, making it an ideal spot for special gatherings. While the terrace offers a minimum spend, the spacious interior and private rooms provide equally impressive settings for an elevated dining experience.

BORNYON's menu features creative Korean cuisine with a focus on wood-fired flavors. Guests can opt for exquisite tasting courses or à la carte dishes. Standout items include the unique fermented pear dressing with sea snail and the flavorful black barley risotto with bamboo shoots and shiitake mushrooms, a dish lauded as the most delicious by many. The restaurant's commitment to quality ingredients and artistic presentation ensures a memorable culinary journey.

properties.name: 면서울by윤
properties.description:
면서울 (Myeonsseoul) offers a refined yet casual Korean dining experience, perfect for both solo diners and small groups. The interior blends modern aesthetics with warm wood tones, creating an inviting atmosphere. This establishment, an offshoot of the Michelin-starred Yoonseoul, prides itself on its house-made noodles, crafted from organic Turkish whole wheat and roasted beans, and premium ingredients like locally sourced perilla oil and aged traditional doenjang (된장).

The signature dishes showcase meticulous attention to detail. The Hanwoo Onsu-yuk (한우 온수육), thinly sliced boiled Korean beef, is exceptionally tender and flavorful, served warm on a mini brazier. The Hanwoo Chanmyeon (한우 찬면) is a standout noodle dish with a broth that is subtly savory and rich with beef essence, reminiscent of a high-quality Pyeongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면).

properties.name: 달리아다이닝
properties.description: Nestled in the trendy Cheongdam area, Dalia Dining offers a sophisticated vegan dining experience curated by Chef Choi Hyun-seok. The restaurant boasts a chic, modern interior, making it a popular spot for both intimate gatherings and romantic dates. Even non-vegans rave about the delicious, plant-based creations, highlighting the restaurant's ability to impress a wide range of palates.

properties.name: 우주옥
properties.description: Emerging as a top contender in the Pyongyang naengmyeon (평양냉면) scene, WOOJUOK has expanded from its popular Mapo location to the upscale district of Cheongdam in Gangnam. The restaurant spans three floors, offering a modern dining experience with a focus on its signature cold noodle dishes. While the space isn't vast, it's efficiently utilized, featuring counter seating perfect for solo diners and a sleek ordering system via kiosks.

properties.name: 송하정스시
properties.description: Songhajung Sushi offers a high-quality sushi omakase experience at an accessible price point. This intimate, eight-seat counter is run by a solo chef and operates strictly by reservation. Diners can expect a thoughtful progression of dishes, starting with a delicate chawanmushi (차완무시) and a refreshing tomato salad, followed by seasonal delights like yellowtail (방어) and smoky grilled mackerel (삼치). The highlight is the expertly prepared sushi, featuring firm sea bream (참돔) and a flavorful tuna akami zuke (아카미 즈케) with a pleasant acidity, all complemented by perfectly seasoned rice.

properties.name: 려
properties.description: Nestled in a cozy, wood-toned interior, Sushi Ryeo Premium offers a serene dining experience perfect for special occasions. The omakase journey begins with delightful surprises like the chawanmushi (차완무시) with pumpkin and a rich abalone barley risotto, setting a high bar for the exquisite sushi to follow. Each piece is meticulously prepared, showcasing premium ingredients and thoughtful flavor combinations, making it a memorable culinary adventure.

properties.name: MUTAN
properties.description: MUTAN is a stylish Chinese dining spot near Apgujeong Station, perfect for year-end gatherings and company dinners. Its sleek interior, featuring a bright and sophisticated atmosphere, can accommodate various group sizes with private rooms available. The menu is impressively diverse, offering creative takes on classic Chinese dishes and seasonal specials, making it a popular choice among celebrities and food enthusiasts alike.

properties.name: TRATTORIA SAM KIM
properties.description: Step into Trattoria Samkim, a culinary haven inspired by the renowned Chef Sam Kim. The ambiance buzzes with an intimate energy, especially if you snag a seat at the bar, offering a captivating view of the chefs at work. From delicate amuse-bouches like the standout cod (대구) to the surprisingly delightful beet dish, each plate is a work of art. Don't miss the tender octopus (문어) or the fragrant shrimp risotto (딱새우 리조또), showcasing a masterful blend of textures and flavors.

properties.name: CROWN PIG
properties.description: Crown Pig, helmed by Chef Song Hoon, offers a premium pork experience with their signature 'Nan-chuk-mat-don' (난축맛돈), a crossbreed of Jeju black pork and Landrace. The star of the show is the 'Don-ma-ho-keu' (돈마호크), a unique cut of sirloin that's smoked in a dramatic tableside presentation. This trendy performance adds a layer of smoky aroma to the already impeccably clean-tasting pork, showcasing the chef's philosophy of delivering the best possible meat.

properties.name: 멘야미코
properties.description: This popular spot, featured on "Black & White Chef 2," is a must-visit before its TV appearance causes even longer waits. Located just a three-minute walk from Cheongdam Station, it's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, as tables fill up quickly. While the space is cozy, with around ten 2-person tables, the lively atmosphere means diners are happy to share tables. Be sure to pre-order your dishes as popular items like the Rosé Donkatsu (로제돈카츠) and cold soba sell out fast, even by opening time.

properties.name: JOWU
properties.description: JOWU

properties.name: CHUNGDAM NINE
properties.description: Chungdam Nine offers a sophisticated ambiance perfect for special occasions, with high ceilings creating an open and airy feel. The venue is renowned for its live jazz performances, providing a captivating soundtrack to your evening. Guests can enjoy a unique photo review event, where tagging the restaurant on social media earns a complimentary mini-dish, adding a fun, interactive element to the dining experience.

properties.name: 청담백송
properties.description: Step into Cheongdam Baeksong, a renowned beef restaurant steeped in tradition since the 1920s, recognized by Blue Ribbon and "Sikgaek" Huh Young-man's "Baengban Haengheng." This establishment boasts premium, dry-aged Hanwoo (한우) beef, with the signature 'Seodae-sal' (서대살) and the '240 Set' featuring prime cuts like tenderloin, sirloin, skirt steak, and short rib. The private rooms make it an ideal spot for celebrity gatherings and exclusive events, offering a sophisticated dining experience.

properties.name: 한우면개나리(압구정본점)
properties.description: Celebrate special occasions at Gaenari Hoegwan (개나리회관), a refined establishment in Apgujeong known for its exquisite Hanwoo (한우) beef and premium seafood. The 'Room Course' offers a generous spread, starting with sirloin (등심) and grilled cheese, followed by an impressive seafood platter featuring large scallops, geoduck, sea urchin (우니), abalone (전복), and more. The experience is elevated with attentive charcoal grilling service and the option to bring your own wine with a reasonable corkage fee.

properties.name: IRORIYAKI SAI
properties.description: IRORIYAKI SAI

properties.name: UUUM Eatery & Bakery
properties.description: Nestled in the charming cafe street near Maebong Station, UUUM Eatery & Bakery offers a serene escape with its beautiful plant-filled interior and inviting terrace ambiance. This spot is a haven for health-conscious diners, recognized by 'Taste of Seoul' for its commitment to delicious, wholesome food. They specialize in vegan and gluten-free options, making it a perfect recommendation for those seeking nutritious meals without compromising on flavor. The menu, which changes seasonally, clearly labels vegan, gluten-free, vegetarian, and organic choices, ensuring a delightful and transparent dining experience.

properties.name: 대삼식당
properties.description: Step into Daesamsikdang (대삼식당) for a retro vibe and top-tier frozen pork belly (냉동삼겹살). The star of the show is the incredibly flavorful frozen pork belly, perfectly complemented by a variety of classic banchan (반찬) like seasoned green onions (파무침) and stir-fried kimchi. Don't miss the unique 'seokkko jjigae' (섞어찌개), a hearty stew that expertly balances the richness of the pork, and the satisfying fried rice (볶음밥) to finish your meal.

properties.name: 더형제
properties.description: Step into The Hyungje (더형제), a premium seafood restaurant born from the renowned Hyungje Sanghoe (형제상회) of Noryangjin Fish Market. This establishment offers a luxurious dining experience, perfect for special occasions and group gatherings, with its elegant private rooms (룸식당). The star of the show is the stunning 12-dish assorted sashimi platter (모듬 회), a vibrant and visually impressive spread that promises a feast for both the eyes and the palate. With its convenient location near Gongdeok Station and ample parking, The Hyungje is an ideal choice for those seeking a sophisticated and memorable meal.

properties.name: ANSEOLI BAKING LAB
properties.description: Nestled in Cheongdam-dong, Anseoli Baking Lab is a highly sought-after bakery famous for its unique Pavlova cake. Due to its immense popularity, securing a purchase can be challenging, often requiring prior purchase history or a lucky reservation slot. The star of the show is the Pavlova, a dessert featuring a meringue base layered with cream and an abundance of fresh fruits. The meringue's crisp yet melt-in-your-mouth texture, combined with the slightly tangy yogurt-like cream and vibrant fruits, creates a refreshing and distinct dessert experience, setting it apart from traditional cakes.

properties.name: Royal Cake
properties.description: Step into a charming white house that feels like a fairytale, perfect for a cozy afternoon, especially on a rainy day. The interior is elegantly decorated with delicate tableware collections, creating a sophisticated yet welcoming ambiance. It's a delightful spot for enjoying a selection of exquisite cupcakes, with the Lemon Earl Grey being a standout favorite that might have you ordering a second helping.

properties.name: 영천영화(청담점)
properties.description:
This Cheongdam-dong spot is a popular destination, especially for international tourists seeking authentic Korean cuisine. The star of the show is the yukhoe bibimbap (육회비빔밥), a dish that many visitors from countries where raw beef is restricted make a point to try. While the reviewer's children raved about it, the reviewer themselves didn't try it, leaving the deliciousness to the imagination.

The restaurant offers a welcoming atmosphere and is known for its high-quality Korean beef dishes. It's a great place to experience a beloved Korean delicacy, making it a must-visit for food lovers exploring Seoul.

properties.name: monert
properties.description: Monert is a highly-regarded patisserie, recognized by the Blue Ribbon Survey for four consecutive years. This establishment prides itself on its premium, handcrafted cakes made daily by a pastry chef trained at the prestigious Nakamura Academy. The attention to detail is evident in every creation, from the perfect cream texture to the artful fruit arrangements, making each cake a masterpiece of both flavor and aesthetics. Popular items like the 'Ddujjonkku' (두쫀쿠) sell out quickly, so early visits are recommended.

properties.name: 츠키요와
properties.description: Step into Tsukiyowa for an exquisite kaiseki (가이세키) experience, helmed by a former head chef from Tokyo's Michelin-starred Nadaman. The intimate, open-kitchen setting at the counter allows for a focused appreciation of the chef's artistry. Seasonal ingredients, like the prized matsutake (송이) mushrooms, are transformed into delicate dishes that delight the senses. With an optional sake and whiskey free-flow, it's a perfect spot for a sophisticated evening or to impress esteemed guests.

properties.name: UZINGA
properties.description: UZINGA

properties.name: 툇마루밥상
properties.description: This long-standing hanjeongsik (한정식) restaurant, Ttoenmaru Bapsang, has relocated and continues to impress with its generous spread. Upon ordering by person, you'll be presented with a table laden with around 21 side dishes, including classics like japchae (잡채) and savory buchu-jeon (부추전), all served with a steaming pot of rice.
