The variety ensures there's something for everyone, making it an ideal spot for family gatherings or business meals, especially with their private room options.

properties.name: small talk
properties.description:
Small talk is deeply ingrained in Australian culture, acting as the social lubricant that keeps society running smoothly. It's characterized by friendly, informal conversations about everyday topics like the weather, sports, food, and hobbies. Australians are known for their openness, readily striking up conversations with strangers, offering help, and sharing details about their lives, sometimes to the point of TMI (Too Much Information).

While it might feel overwhelming at first, this culture of 'chatter' or 'suda' (수다) is an open invitation to connect. Unlike in some other cultures where such deep conversations might be reserved for close acquaintances, in Australia, it's a widespread practice across all ages and genders. Understanding that these interactions are generally lighthearted and non-controversial can help ease the pressure, allowing anyone to join in and participate in the friendly exchange.

properties.name: Texas de Brazil
properties.description:
Texas de Brazil offers a sophisticated dining experience near the Gangnam Express Bus Terminal, perfect for special occasions. The ambiance is dimly lit and luxurious, with festive holiday decorations adding to the charm. This churrasco (슈하스코) specialist is known for its all-you-can-eat grilled meats, expertly carved tableside by servers circulating with various cuts. The extensive salad bar complements the main event, featuring fresh vegetables, cured meats, and seafood.

Prepare for a feast as servers continuously offer a wide array of delicious, high-quality grilled meats. While the prices are on the higher side, the sheer variety and quality make it a memorable culinary adventure. It's an ideal spot for celebrations or impressive business dinners, with private rooms available for a more intimate gathering.

properties.name: Carols
properties.description: Step into Carols, a retro-themed family restaurant that evokes nostalgic memories of childhood birthday celebrations. The space is beautifully designed with warm brick, wooden tables, and vibrant stained glass, creating a cozy yet lively atmosphere reminiscent of classic American diners. It's an ideal spot for family gatherings or friendly meetups, offering a unique ambiance that feels like a movie set.

properties.name: 곱(문래점)
properties.description: Nestled in the vibrant Mullae Cultural Food Alley, 'Gop' (곱) is a highly recommended spot for those craving perfectly grilled intestines. This popular eatery, featured on TV, offers a lively atmosphere with a bustling crowd even before dinner hours. Their signature 'Modum Gui' (모듬구이), a platter of assorted grilled beef intestines like 'Gopchang' (곱창), 'Makchang' (막창), and 'Yeomtong' (염통), is cooked to order and served piping hot. The intestines are praised for their tender texture and rich flavor, especially when paired with the accompanying scallion kimchi (파김치) towards the end of the meal.

properties.name: 해월
properties.description: Nestled in Jungnang-gu, Haewol Shindang (해월신당) offers a deeply insightful and almost uncanny spiritual reading experience. Visitors often leave feeling a sense of calm and clarity, with many remarking on the uncanny accuracy of the predictions, particularly regarding personal finances and family matters. The atmosphere is conducive to serious reflection, making it a popular spot for those seeking guidance.

properties.name: 월화갈비
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Mullae, Wolhwa Galbi is a must-visit for date nights and gatherings, recognized by the Blue Ribbon Survey for its outstanding pork ribs. The industrial-chic interior, inspired by Mullae's blacksmith workshops, creates a unique dining atmosphere. Don't miss their signature Parkpo Galbi (박포갈비), a limited-offering dish prepared with a special triple-grilling process that infuses it with a deep charcoal flavor. Complement your meal with their signature garlic sauce and the complimentary spicy sundubu jjigae (순두부찌개).

properties.name: 히키니쿠토코메도산
properties.description: Step into a world of culinary delight at Hikinnikutokome Dosan, a hidden gem specializing in Japanese hamburg steak. The intimate counter seating offers a front-row view of chefs meticulously shaping and grilling the patties, filling the air with an irresistible aroma. Each order comes with up to three hamburg patties, unlimited rice, and a single egg, allowing for a personalized dining experience. Don't miss the chance to savor the rich, juicy patties, expertly grilled over charcoal for a smoky finish, and explore the array of accompanying sauces and condiments to find your perfect bite.

properties.name: HWACHOONOK CHEONGDAM
properties.description: Step into Hwachoonok Cheongdam, a legendary establishment that has been serving as the origin of Suwon galbi (수원 갈비) since 1945. This third-generation restaurant prides itself on a unique, soy-sauce-free marinade, a secret recipe passed down through decades, offering a taste of history with every bite. It's a place where tradition meets exquisite flavor, having been a favorite of dignitaries and locals alike for generations.

properties.name: 늘복집
properties.description: Nestled in a serene environment, Neulbokjip is a distinguished pufferfish (복어) specialty restaurant, recognized by a "Grand Master" for its deep and rich flavors. Its elegant exterior and spacious parking make it an ideal choice for formal occasions like family gatherings (상견례) or company dinners. The restaurant offers a sophisticated atmosphere with private rooms on the third floor, perfect for intimate celebrations and important meetings.

properties.name: 혜장국
properties.description: This Yeosu restaurant offers a warm and welcoming atmosphere, perfect for families. With spacious seating and a convenient table ordering system, it's easy to enjoy a hearty meal. The menu features a variety of comforting gukbap (국밥) and kimchi jjigae (김치찌개), with generous portions and a selection of delicious banchan (반찬) like the vibrant Dolsan gat-kimchi (돌산갓김치). Don't miss the complimentary fried eggs, a thoughtful touch that adds to the overall satisfying experience.

properties.name: 황푸차이나
properties.description: Conveniently located near the Gubeundari Station and Cheonho CGV, Hwangpu China offers a refined dining experience reminiscent of upscale establishments. The menu boasts elevated takes on classic Chinese dishes, with signature items like the Hwangpu Jajangmyeon (황푸짜장면) and Hwangpu Jjamppong (황푸짬뽕) showcasing the chef's confidence and skill. Don't miss the seasonal Hwangpu Naengyi Tangmyeon (황푸냉이탕면), a delicate noodle soup featuring fresh spring vegetables, or the perfectly crispy and chewy Heukcho Tangsuyuk (흑초탕수육), made with premium pork and a tangy black vinegar sauce.

properties.name: 히까리
properties.description: Step into Hikari, a long-standing sushi omakase institution that has proudly held its Blue Ribbon status for 11 consecutive years. Despite the slightly unpolished ambiance in some seating areas, the true magic unfolds at the counter. Chef's dedication shines through, offering a unique omakase experience that seamlessly blends sushi and sashimi, all while maintaining incredibly fair pricing that hasn't budged since opening. Prepare for a journey where traditional techniques meet thoughtful presentation, starting with a delicate crab meat soup and moving through expertly prepared sashimi like sea bream and amberjack, often best enjoyed with a touch of salt to truly appreciate their natural flavors.

properties.name: 히노야마
properties.description: Step into Hinoyama (히노야마) and discover a classic Japanese eatery with a warm, traditional interior. This spot prides itself on its handmade udon noodles, meticulously crafted with dough aged for 11 hours and served after a 15-minute boil. The rich broth, made with soy sauce aged for 360 hours, offers a deep, satisfying flavor. Don't miss their signature Napyak Udon (납작우동), a limited-quantity dish featuring thick, chewy noodles that are a true testament to the care and time invested in their preparation.

properties.name: 해남천일관
properties.description: For those seeking a refined dining experience in Jamsil, Hainam Cheonilgwan offers a tranquil atmosphere perfect for family gatherings or formal occasions. This esteemed restaurant, with a history spanning a century and recognized in the Michelin Guide, specializes in traditional Korean set meals (Hanjeongsik). Signature dishes include the savory Hanu Tteokgalbi (한우 떡갈비) and the flavorful Borigulbi (보리굴비), both presented with an array of meticulously prepared side dishes that highlight the essence of Korean cuisine.

properties.name: 해동복국(동여의도점)
properties.description: Step into a refined atmosphere at Haedong Bokguk, a 40-year veteran in the art of pufferfish cuisine. The spacious and elegantly decorated interior makes it an ideal spot for group gatherings. Start your meal with refreshing side dishes like the spicy pufferfish skin salad (bok kkeopjil muchim) and delicious kimchi, before diving into their signature dish. The Bokjiri (복지리) is a clear, refreshing soup brimming with generous portions of tender pufferfish, perfect for a satisfying meal or a potent hangover cure.

properties.name: FAIT MAISON
properties.description: FAIT MAISON, located on the 4th floor of the Sofitel Ambassador Seoul, offers an elegant dining experience. While the buffet is only available on weekends, weekdays feature a refined 5-course tasting menu. Guests can expect beautifully presented dishes, starting with an amuse-bouche that has a texture similar to bossam (편육). The meal progresses through delicate courses like snow crab with cauliflower royal (콜리플라워 로얄) and pan-seared sea bream with bouillabaisse sauce (부야베스 소스), all complemented by delicious bread and attentive service. The restaurant boasts a lovely view, which on this occasion, was enhanced by a surprise fireworks display.

properties.name: PUN JU
properties.description: PUNJU offers a contemporary Korean dining experience, blending traditional aesthetics with modern culinary techniques. The restaurant's name, meaning 'large, fine vessel,' is reflected in its beautifully crafted tableware and traditional Korean-inspired decor. Guests can enjoy a unique tasting menu, 'Matgim Charim' (맡김차림), which features a series of meticulously prepared dishes. The chefs' open kitchen allows for an engaging dining experience, with detailed explanations of each course and its ingredients.

properties.name: 한우리
properties.description: Hanuri, a venerable establishment in Apgujeong Rodeo, offers a refined dining experience with a focus on premium Korean beef. The restaurant is renowned for its Hanu Shabushabu (한우 샤브샤브), where the staff meticulously prepares and serves each portion, allowing guests to relax and enjoy. The set menu includes a delightful progression of dishes, starting with pumpkin porridge and a delicate Hanu Rosu Pyeonchae (한우 로스편채), before culminating in the star shabushabu, noodles, and dessert. The ambiance is sophisticated, making it an ideal spot for special occasions or entertaining guests.

properties.name: Francaise
properties.description: This is a vibrant hub for French language and culture, occupying a multi-story building that reflects its significant presence. It's a place where you can find French language classes, a media library (médiathèque) with resources ranging from children's levels to advanced studies, and a collection of French books and magazines, including timeless classics like 'The Little Prince' (Le Petit Prince). The atmosphere is one of shared learning and cultural appreciation, with a focus on both modern and historical aspects of French literature and language.

properties.name: 하모
properties.description: Nestled in Jangandong, Daemyeong Susan offers a charming dining experience, especially on pleasant days when you can enjoy fresh seafood outdoors. The restaurant boasts a clean and welcoming interior, with tanks full of seasonal catches, including the star of the show, ha mo (하모). Their menu features a variety of premium seafood options, but the highlight is undoubtedly the seasonal ha mo shabu-shabu (하모 샤브샤브), prepared with meticulous care. The restaurant also thoughtfully provides hot water to sanitize utensils and offers a selection of delicious side dishes like aged kimchi (묵은지) and mustard greens kimchi (갓김치) to complement your meal. They even allow corkage, so feel free to bring your favorite wine to pair with the exquisite seafood.

properties.name: 한성돈까스
properties.description: This popular spot, formerly a long-standing establishment, has relocated and now boasts a modern vibe with ample parking. It's a go-to for classic Korean comfort food, with the star being their signature donkatsu (돈까스). The pork cutlet is praised for its incredibly crispy batter and tender, juicy meat, making it a must-try. Don't miss the refreshing draft beer (생맥주) or the option to pair your meal with soju (소주) for the ultimate Korean dining experience.

properties.name: 평미가
properties.description: Pyeongmiga is a venerable Pyeongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면) restaurant, proudly carrying on a culinary legacy for three generations. The interior, though established in 2010, exudes a timeless, traditional ambiance, hinting at its deep roots tracing back to Pyeongyang. Beyond their signature cold noodles, the menu boasts a rich array of North Korean specialties like onban (온반), hand-made dumplings (손만둣국), and eobok-jang (어복쟁반), making it a destination for authentic Pyeongan cuisine.

properties.name: 피양콩할마니
properties.description: A long-standing favorite, Pyeongkong Halmeoni has earned a spot in the Michelin Guide Bib Gourmand for seven consecutive years, a testament to its dedication to quality. This bustling eatery, often packed even at lunchtime, specializes in traditional Korean bean dishes. The star of the show is their signature kongguksu (콩국수), a pure soybean noodle soup. Unlike other versions that might add nuts or seeds, their kongguksu is a celebration of 100% soybean flavor, offering a rich, smooth, and subtly sweet broth that feels like a comforting bowl of chilled soybean soup. It's a pure, unadulterated taste that truly highlights the essence of the soybean.

properties.name: Trouver
properties.description: Nestled in the stylish Sinsa-dong Garosu-gil, Trouver offers a refined Japanese-French fine dining experience. The intimate, bar-style seating with an open kitchen allows guests to witness the culinary artistry firsthand, all within a dimly lit, sophisticated ambiance perfect for quiet, elegant meals. Standout dishes include the exquisite uni (sea urchin) pasta, a perfectly cooked Korean beef tenderloin steak (hanu ansim steak), and the crispy yet tender grilled golden threadfin bream (geumtae gui), each showcasing a delicate balance of flavors and artful presentation.

properties.name: 키고
properties.description: Nestled a short walk from Cheongdam Station, Kigo offers an intimate Japanese dining experience with a set course menu for both lunch and dinner. The ambiance is refined, featuring a sleek bar counter and curated ceramic art. The culinary journey is helmed by a husband-and-wife chef team, whose synergy shines through in each meticulously prepared dish. Expect a delightful progression of flavors, from refreshing starters like the chilled soy milk with fig and tonburi to expertly cooked seafood and unique preparations like the steamed eel with glutinous rice, all served with an appreciation for beautiful tableware.

properties.name: 키라메키
properties.description: Nestled between Sinsa and Hakdong stations, Kirameki offers a refined kaiseki (가이세키) experience helmed by Chef Choi Jae-nyung, who trained in Japan. The lunch course, a "washoku" (와쇼쿠) full-course meal, is praised for its reasonable price point, making it an accessible splurge. Diners rave about the meticulously prepared 12-dish spread, highlighting the sesame-dressed eggplant (가지) as a surprisingly delightful standout, with its perfect balance and rich, nutty flavor.

properties.name: kushikawa
properties.description: Kushikawa is a Michelin-recognized gem, consistently earning its place in the guide for its exceptional kushiage (꼬치 튀김) omakase. The intimate, 12-seat bar counter sets the stage for a culinary journey where chefs prepare around 14 varieties of skewers, featuring a diverse array of fresh seafood, meats, and vegetables. Each bite is a testament to the art of deep-frying, served piping hot directly from the kitchen, allowing diners to savor the pure, unadulterated flavors and textures of the ingredients.

properties.name: KIHEI Coffee
properties.description: KIHEI Coffee, a beloved cafe in Seonjeongneung, has transitioned from curating top overseas roasters to roasting its own beans. The interior evokes a Hawaiian sunset, reflecting the owner's calm demeanor. They specialize in light-roast coffees bursting with fruit and floral notes, perfectly complemented by a selection of homemade cookies. Their commitment to quality is evident in their investment in a high-end Loring S7 roaster, ensuring a premium coffee experience.

properties.name: 팔화당
properties.description: Palhwadang (팔화당) is a popular spot near Seolleung Station, known for its spacious and comfortable atmosphere, making it ideal for solo diners. While famous for its high-quality Korean beef (hanwoo, 한우) and pork (handon, 한돈), the restaurant also shines with its hearty lunch menu. They recently launched new dishes like Galbitang (갈비탕), Hanwoo Yukgaejang (한우육개장), and Tueple Miyeokguk (투쁠미역국), with special Friday discounts and ongoing price reductions making these delicious meals incredibly accessible.

properties.name: 팔식당
properties.description: Step into Palsikdang (팔식당), a stylish Cheongdam-dong eatery that has quickly garnered a following, evident from the celebrity signatures adorning its clean, modern interior. This premium pork barbecue joint specializes in high-quality, grade-1 female pork, grilled to perfection over charcoal on a cast-iron plate. Don't miss their limited-quantity daily specials like galmaegisal (갈매기살 - pork skirt meat) and saenggalbi (생갈비 - raw pork ribs), which are expertly grilled tableside. Complement your meal with their popular yangnyeom gejang (양념게장 - marinated raw crab) for a spicy kick that perfectly cuts through the rich pork.

properties.name: patisserie sona
properties.description: Patisserie Sona is a European-inspired bakery in Magok, Seoul, helmed by a master patissier with national accolades. The bakery boasts a clean, modern exterior and an inviting interior with an open kitchen where you can see bakers at work. Each pastry is crafted with unique fermentation methods, resulting in distinct textures – from perfectly crisp to delightfully moist. The menu features a rotating selection of cakes and other baked goods, with samples available to help you choose.

properties.name: 태천옥
properties.description: This Blue Ribbon-certified spot in Gaebong-dong is a beloved local gem, perfect for a satisfying meal with family. While renowned for its signature Hamheung-style naengmyeon (함흥냉면), the restaurant also shines with hearty dishes like the spicy beef soup, eolkeun hanu gukbap (얼큰 한우 국밥), packed with generous chunks of tender beef and savory 우거지 (aged kimchi greens). For those seeking a milder option, the mushroom and beef bulgogi (버섯듬뿍불고기) offers a delightful mix of tender beef and chewy mushrooms in a sweet and savory broth.

properties.name: KUNSOMCHAI
properties.description: KUNSOMCHAI in Gyodae is a celebrated Thai restaurant, recognized by both Blue Ribbon and Michelin guides. The ambiance is cozy and intimate, with charming decor. A unique aspect is the availability of certain dishes only after 1 PM, and the option for Thai food courses, making it a distinctive dining experience. While the signature beef rib noodle soup (소갈비 쌀국수) offers a generous portion of tender meat and a surprisingly refreshing, slightly spicy broth with a hint of chili, the soft-shell crab curry (뿌빳풍 커리) presents a sweet and savory coconut-infused sauce that pairs well with rice, though the crab itself can be a bit oily.

properties.name: 타쿠미곤
properties.description: Experience the refined artistry of seasoned sushi at Takumi Gon (타쿠미곤), helmed by Master Kwon Oh-jun. While Chef Kwon may be in a private counter, Chef Kim Ki-nam delivers an omakase (오마카세) experience characterized by a calm and elegant composure. Delight in the exquisite textures and flavors of aged seafood, from the chewy octopus (문어) and firm sea bass (우럭) to the melt-in-your-mouth fatty tuna (쥬토로) and rich blackthroat seaperch (아카무츠). Each dish is a testament to meticulous preparation and a deep understanding of ingredients.

properties.name: KuMa
properties.description: KuMa offers a cozy and dimly lit ambiance, perfect for intimate conversations or a date night. The thoughtful inclusion of a coat rack adds to the comfort, especially during colder months. The menu is extensive, featuring a wide selection of sake and other spirits, with helpful pairing recommendations. Don't miss their top-tier dishes like the fresh 3-kind sashimi (삼종사시미), featuring thick-cut fish that's incredibly fresh, or the unique eggplant and beef stew (가지부채살구이) with a rich, slow-cooked sauce.

properties.name: Treeanon
properties.description: Treeanon is an antique-filled tea house that offers a tranquil escape, especially on a sunny morning. With a vast selection of teas, including Darjeeling Castleton (다즐링 캐슬틴) and Uva Mlesna (우바 뮬레즈나), the experience is about savoring the ritual of brewing and customizing your cup. Pair your tea with their delightful desserts like honey butter toast or chocolate banana for a unique afternoon treat.

properties.name: 파크루안
properties.description: This review describes a teacher networking event held in Seoul, not a restaurant. The event featured performances, lectures, and opportunities for educators to connect. Attendees shared their experiences and insights, with a focus on collaborative learning and professional development. The event aimed to foster a sense of community among teachers, encouraging them to share their knowledge and experiences through various platforms.

properties.name: PaPaho
properties.description: PaPaho is a long-standing Vietnamese restaurant that's a must-visit for those craving a comforting bowl of pho (쌀국수). The atmosphere is relaxed, making it a perfect spot for a leisurely meal. Don't miss their signature dishes like the generously portioned Goi Cuon (꼬이꾸온) with fresh rice paper rolls, the flavorful fried rice (볶음밥) packed with plump shrimp, and the perfectly stir-fried Pad Thai (팟타이) offering a delightful mix of crunchy bean sprouts and soft noodles.

properties.name: CHIBO
properties.description: Step into CHIBO (치보), a vibrant Osaka-style teppanyaki specialist that brings 50 years of tradition to Seoul's Garosu-gil. The atmosphere buzzes with the energetic greetings of Japanese restaurants, making it a perfect spot to feel like you've hopped over to Japan. Enjoy a wide array of expertly prepared dishes, served on your personal teppan grill, and pair them with a refreshing draft beer for the ultimate experience.

properties.name: 코끼리베이글
properties.description: This popular bakery is a must-visit for its unique, chewy bagels, baked in a brick oven for a distinct texture. Beyond the wide variety of flavorful bagels, like garlic and cream cheese options, they also offer delicious sandwiches and hearty stew sets. The Hannam branch boasts a spacious, multi-level interior with comfortable seating and even offers Han River (한강) views from the upper floors, making it a great spot to relax and enjoy a treat.

properties.name: BLUE ROOF TOWN
properties.description: Step into Blue Roof Town (청기와타운) and experience a unique vibe inspired by the nostalgic Korean enclaves of Los Angeles and New York. This eatery captures the essence of a bygone era, offering a distinct retro atmosphere that transports you back in time. It's a popular franchise, so you can find this distinctive ambiance at various locations across the city, perfect for a casual meal or a gathering in their private rooms.

properties.name: 채윤희
properties.description: Nestled in the artistic alleys of Mullae, Chae Yoon Hee offers a vibrant, atmospheric escape perfect for a night out with friends. The real magic happens on the third-floor rooftop, which transforms into a lively urban pocha (포차) with twinkling lights and a buzzing ambiance. While it can get a bit noisy, the energetic vibe is infectious and ideal for laughter and conversation. Be sure to use the Tabeling app to snag a coveted rooftop spot, especially on a pleasant evening.

properties.name: chai lyn
properties.description: Chai Lyn offers a sophisticated dining experience with its high ceilings and elegant ambiance, perfect for special occasions. The menu boasts a variety of expertly prepared Chinese dishes, with standouts including the tender and flavorful Dongpo Pork (동파육), which falls apart at the touch of chopsticks, and the refreshing seafood dishes like the Sea Cucumber with Shrimp Paste (오룡해삼). The fried rice is also a must-try, perfectly cooked and seasoned.

properties.name: 춘천옥
properties.description: Escape the summer heat at Chuncheonok (춘천옥), a clean and accessible jjimjilbang (찜질방) in the heart of Jungnang-gu. This 24-hour oasis offers a refreshing retreat with well-regulated temperatures, featuring both hot jjimjil rooms and a cooling ice room for the ultimate relaxation. Its convenient location and affordable entrance fee make it an ideal spot to unwind and recharge, especially during sweltering nights when other cafes close.

properties.name: 초롱이고모부대찌개
properties.description:
Nestled within the bustling Amsa Market, Chorongi Gomobu Dae Jjigae is a beloved local institution, recognized with Blue Ribbon accolades. The vibe here is classic 'no-po' (old-school eatery) charm, offering a cozy and authentic market atmosphere. It's a place where families flock for a comforting meal, and the clean, well-maintained space complements the delicious food.

The star of the show is their Budae-jjigae (부대찌개), a hearty stew brimming with an generous amount of ham and sausage. For those seeking an even richer experience, the Budae-jeongol (부대전골) offers an elevated version with a wider variety of meats. The broth is surprisingly clean and refreshing, not overly spicy, making it a hit even with younger diners. Don't forget to save room for the 'Korean dessert' – the savory fried rice (bokkeumbap) made from the leftover broth of the Budae-jeongol is a must-try.

properties.name: 청담골
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Gangnam, Cheongdamgol offers a traditional Korean dining experience that feels like a warm embrace. This spot is renowned for its 'baekban' (백반), a set meal featuring a generous spread of side dishes, often exceeding ten varieties, served with your choice of rice or 'nurungji' (누룽지 - scorched rice). It's a fantastic value, especially considering its prime location, making it a go-to for those seeking authentic Korean home cooking without breaking the bank. The atmosphere is bustling yet comforting, perfect for family meals or a satisfying solo dining experience, though be aware of potential lunch hour restrictions for single diners.

properties.name: 참나무숯불에닭이만나는순간
properties.description: Step into a 30-year-old institution near Yangjae Citizen's Forest, a true testament to dedication since 1995. This cozy spot, run by the same family for decades, specializes in a single, star dish: charcoal-grilled dakgalbi (닭갈비). The chicken is marinated for three days and then expertly grilled over oak charcoal, resulting in incredibly tender, juicy, and slightly chewy morsels infused with a subtle smoky aroma. The mild, sweet-and-spicy sauce is a perfect complement, and the owners' passion for their craft is evident in every detail.

properties.name: Cakehouse Wien
properties.description: Step into Cake House Wien, a beloved institution in Cheongdam-dong with a rich 40-year history. This pioneering bakery is credited with introducing Korea's first fresh cream cake, a testament to its legacy as a first-generation patisserie. The atmosphere buzzes with locals from early morning, offering a cozy spot to enjoy their wide array of baked goods. While some might find the cake designs classic, they evoke a sense of nostalgia, much like their signature marble castella, which is praised for its simple, clean sweetness. Don't miss out on their highly recommended danpat-ppang (단팥빵) and soboro-ppang (소보로빵) for a true taste of tradition.

properties.name: 진진만두
properties.description:
A beloved institution for Yeouido office workers, Jinjin Mandu (진진만두) has opened a new branch near City Hall Station, offering a welcome respite with its comforting, hearty fare. The interior blends traditional Korean aesthetics with modern touches, creating a clean and sophisticated dining space perfect for both casual lunches and business meetings. Its prime location near Seoul Plaza makes it an ideal spot to warm up after ice skating.

The star of the show is the signature Son Mandu Sulguk (손만두 술국), a spicy, flavorful broth made with a rich Hanwoo (한우) beef bone base, first introduced by the restaurant in 1998. The handmade dumplings are delicate yet chewy, bursting with savory meat and vegetable filling. For a milder option, the Suyuk Gomtang (수육곰탕) boasts a clear, deeply savory broth infused with the aroma of premium beef, showcasing the restaurant's commitment to quality ingredients and traditional flavors.

properties.name: 조리인
properties.description: Nestled on the third floor in Apgujeong, Jorrin offers an intimate sushi omakase experience with its all-counter seating and private rooms. Regulars return for the chef's warm welcome and the generous 'Sushi Course' (99,000 KRW per person). The meal begins with refreshing starters like sea cucumber salad and a rich, savory flounder liver, leading into a meticulously prepared sushi selection. Standouts include the fatty tuna (otoro) with spicy salt and the sweet-and-salty monkfish liver (ankimo), each expertly seasoned and ready to be savored.

properties.name: 진가와(한국본점)
properties.description: Step into Jinagawa (진가와), a renowned Japanese noodle specialist that has earned a spot on the Korean Michelin Guide's 'Bib Gourmand' list for five consecutive years. With a legacy spanning 12 generations and over 360 years, Jinagawa prides itself on its meticulously crafted udon and somen noodles, imported directly from Kagawa Prefecture, Japan. The result is an unparalleled chewy and smooth texture that forms the heart of their exquisite dishes. It's the perfect spot for a solo lunch, offering a moment of peaceful indulgence.

properties.name: 지인고기
properties.description: This Yeonnam-dong gem is a perfect spot for gatherings, conveniently located near Hongik University Station. The restaurant offers a modern dining experience with T-order kiosks supporting multiple languages, catering to a diverse clientele. It's even been featured on Japanese TV, with K-pop group NCT WISH having visited. The highlight is their 'Hanpan Set' (한판세트), featuring both thinly sliced beef brisket (usambgyeop, 우삼겹) and pork belly (samgyeopsal, 삼겹살). The staff expertly grill everything for you, allowing you to relax and enjoy the company and delicious food.

properties.name: 일일향
properties.description: Ililhyang in Songpa is a spacious Chinese restaurant perfect for group gatherings and company dinners, capable of hosting up to 70 people. The ambiance is clean and well-suited for special occasions. While the menu is extensive, standout dishes include the unique Lemon Shrimp (레몬중새우) and the slightly spicy, tender La-Jo-Gi (라조기). Both the Jjajangmyeon (짜장면) and Jjamppong (짬뽕) are highly recommended for those seeking a satisfying meal.

properties.name: ildossi dakgalbi
properties.description: Step into Ildossi Dakgalbi (일도씨닭갈비) in Mokdong for a refined take on the beloved Korean stir-fried chicken dish. The restaurant boasts a sophisticated, calming ambiance with warm wooden interiors, making it a surprisingly tranquil spot for a meal. Their signature 'Chi-Chi-Cheese Dakgalbi' (치-치-치즈 닭갈비) is a must-try, featuring double the meat and triple the cheese, perfect for those who crave a rich, indulgent experience. Complement your meal with their house-made coleslaw and enjoy the unique, addictive flavor profile derived from their special sauce made with domestic ingredients.

properties.name: 자맛풍
properties.description: Nestled near Yangjae Station, Jamatpung2 offers a unique Korean omakase (오마카세) experience where the menu changes daily based on the chef's whim and seasonal ingredients. Reservations are highly recommended, often needing to be made weeks in advance. The vibe is lively, with patrons enjoying a wide selection of traditional liquors, wine, and whiskey. Expect a multi-course meal that starts with appetizers like ricotta cheese salad and jeon (전), followed by a seafood platter featuring seasonal catches. A standout is their signature self-made gimbap (김밥), made with pickled melon, offering a delightful sweet and savory bite.

properties.name: gentil
properties.description: Gentil is a cozy bistro and wine bar that requires reservations for dinner and a drink order. While the signage is subtle, the interior offers a charming atmosphere with around 8 seats and a diverse selection of glassware. The menu features French-inspired dishes, with the pan-seared sea bream (옥돔스테이크) standing out as a highlight for its flaky skin and tender flesh, complemented by a bright lemon beurre blanc sauce. Complimentary brioche and a rich duck liver pâté (오리간 빠떼) with pistachios are also delightful touches.

properties.name: 일미락
properties.description: Ilmiyak is a premier destination in Mok-dong for an elevated Korean BBQ experience, specializing in pork. Their unique "nuruk-fermented" (누룩 발효) pork, particularly the neck (moksal - 목살) and skirt meat (gabrisal - 가브리살), boasts a deep, rich flavor profile that redefines the pork experience. Complement your meal with their popular grilled deodeok (더덕구이) and hearty sulbap (술밥) for a truly authentic taste of Mok-dong's culinary scene.

properties.name: 진사향
properties.description: Located just a 3-minute walk from Guro Station, Jinsahyang is a Chinese restaurant that offers excellent value. They boast affordable prices, with a special event price for Jajangmyeon (짜장면) at 5,000 won and mini Tangsuyuk (탕수육) at 12,000 won. Their lunch course menus start at a very reasonable 15,900 won, making it a great option for a satisfying Chinese meal without breaking the bank. The restaurant provides a spacious dining area and private rooms, ideal for gatherings and company dinners.

properties.name: J'ANTIQUE
properties.description: J'ANTIQUE is a beloved neighborhood bakery in Seocho-gu, so popular that its signature breads sell out within hours of opening. It's recommended to visit early or utilize their convenient Naver reservation system to secure your favorites. This bakery is recognized by Blue Ribbon Survey and featured on "The Master of Living," emphasizing their commitment to using naturally leavened sourdough and healthy ingredients, with a philosophy of same-day production and sales.

properties.name: 조양관
properties.description: Steeped in over 70 years of history, Joyanggwan offers a refined Hanjeongsik (한정식) experience in Gangnam. Each course is a work of art, showcasing fresh, seasonal ingredients prepared with a focus on their natural flavors. The subtle seasoning allows the quality of the premium ingredients to shine, resulting in dishes that are both delicious and comforting. With its private rooms and attentive service, Joyanggwan is an ideal choice for important gatherings like business dinners, family celebrations, or formal meetings with elders.

properties.name: Juilette
properties.description: Juilette offers a vibrant and lively atmosphere, perfect for a night out with friends. The space is known for its energetic vibe, making it a popular spot for those looking to unwind and enjoy good company. It's a place where the buzz of conversation and the clinking of glasses create a memorable experience.

properties.name: Giani's Napoli
properties.description: Step into Giani's Napoli in Cheongdam and be greeted by a warm, inviting ambiance, enhanced by soft yellow lighting and charming wall art that hints at the authentic Neapolitan pizza experience. The open kitchen, complete with a visible wood-fired oven, adds to the vibrant atmosphere. Don't miss their signature Gambari Crema (감베리크레마), a creamy, spicy pasta dish loaded with plump shrimp and mozzarella, which many patrons find themselves ordering repeatedly. The chewy, delightful pizza dough, a hallmark of their traditional Neapolitan style, is also a must-try, whether as a starter or on their pizzas like the fresh and flavorful Rucola Pesto (루꼴라패스토).

properties.name: 장사랑
properties.description: Nestled within I'm Park Mall, Jang Sarang is a spacious Korean restaurant perfect for family gatherings or a healthy meal. While the price point might be a bit higher, the quality and thoughtful preparation of dishes like the signature Dolsot Jeongseon Gondeure Bibimbap (돌솥 정선 곤드레 비빔밥) make it a worthwhile experience. The bibimbap, served piping hot in a stone pot, is flavorful without being overly spicy, and the accompanying banchan (side dishes) are perfectly balanced.

properties.name: 주
properties.description:
Spectrum at the Fairmont Ambassador Seoul offers a luxurious buffet experience with a wide array of meticulously prepared dishes. The carving station is a highlight, featuring succulent Peking duck (북경오리), crispy samgyeopsal (삼겹살), and tender chashu (차슈), all expertly sliced and served with a variety of condiments. Fresh sashimi (회), grilled lobster, and eel (장어구이) are also standout options, alongside a well-rounded Korean section with namul (나물) and various banchan (반찬).

The ambiance is sophisticated and ideal for special occasions or a lavish meal. Diners can expect attentive service, with staff readily available to assist with carving and serving. The sheer variety ensures something for every palate, from rich grilled meats like lamb ribs (양갈비구이) and beef sirloin (채끝등심구이) to comforting Korean staples and international favorites. It's a feast for the senses, promising a memorable culinary journey.

properties.name: 임진강민물장어
properties.description: A long-standing institution since 2012, Imjingang Minmul Jang-eo (임진강민물장어) is a beloved spot for grilled freshwater eel (minmul jang-eo, 민물장어). The restaurant boasts a 40-year tradition, offering a focused menu where the salt-grilled eel shines. The atmosphere is welcoming and spacious, with many families and elders frequenting the establishment, signaling its status as a true local gem. Expect attentive service, with staff expertly grilling the eel to perfection right at your table.

properties.name: yido cheongdam
properties.description: Yido Cheongdam is a hidden gem in Seoul's upscale Cheongdam district, offering a dining experience that blends culinary art with exquisite ceramics. The restaurant's ambiance is sophisticated and romantic, enhanced by beautiful pottery displays and soft lighting, making it an ideal spot for special occasions and memorable dates. Guests can explore a curated collection of pottery before their meal, setting the stage for a visually and gastronomically delightful experience.

properties.name: 이봉
properties.description: Step back in time at this historically significant site, once the resting place of Crown Prince Sado (사도세자) under the name Yeong-u-won (영우원). While the original location is now occupied by Samyuk Seoul Hospital, a marker on Baebongsan Mountain (배봉산) hints at its former presence. This spot offers a unique glimpse into the evolving nomenclature of royal tombs, a fascinating aspect of Korean history.

properties.name: IKIKI
properties.description: IKIKI is a vibrant pop-up event space that draws crowds eager for unique camping gear and design collaborations. While the main draw for many was the limited Natal Design collection, the event also featured a curated selection of other brands, including Helinox and In Out. The atmosphere buzzed with excitement, despite the summer heat, as attendees navigated the popular items and sought out exclusive finds like the stylish flag, bags, and shades that IKIKI is known for.

properties.name: wootender
properties.description: Wootender is a high-end Hanwoo (한우) destination in Apgujeong, perfect for special occasions. While the service can be a bit hands-off, the star of the show is undoubtedly the premium beef. The special tenderloin (특안심) is a must-try, cooked to perfection by the staff for an additional fee. Don't miss the incredible katsusando (카츠산도), a dish so delicious it warrants a return visit on its own.

properties.name: Bakery YUL
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Gangdong-gu, YUL Bakery (율베이커리) is a charming neighborhood gem recognized by the Blue Ribbon Survey. Its unique exterior, featuring an interior design inspired by the bakery's initial 'Y', draws you in. Inside, you'll find a delightful array of breads, from the hearty Fig Rye (무화과 호밀) packed with figs, cranberries, and walnuts, to the popular Cranberry Tiger (크랜베리 타이거), a Dutch-style bread made with rice flour and loaded with cranberries and walnuts. Don't miss their signature Baguette Sandwich (바게트 샌드위치), a flavorful creation with salami and a hint of spice, or the perfectly textured Ciabatta (치아바타), ideal for dipping or as a sandwich base.

properties.name: 이도곰탕
properties.description: Nestled near the GS Art Center, this restaurant offers a comforting and traditional dining experience, perfect for a warm meal before or after a show. The ambiance is serene, with a touch of analog charm from its wooden staircase, making it a welcoming spot even for solo diners. The establishment prides itself on a legacy of culinary tradition, passed down through three generations from a recipe by a Korean medicine doctor ancestor.

properties.name: 이조보쌈
properties.description: This beloved spot in Dangsan is a must-visit for anyone craving perfectly tender and moist bossam (보쌈). The star of the show is the succulent pork, complemented by a spicy and crunchy radish kimchi (무말랭이) that strikes a delightful balance. Served with a flavorful doenjang jjigae (된장찌개) or cheonggukjang (청국장), it's a comforting and satisfying meal that appeals to all palates, even those who prefer lean meat.

properties.name: 운봉산장
properties.description: Step into Yangdaesanmaek, a hip spot with a warm, inviting ambiance reminiscent of a stylish cafe. This restaurant is making waves for its exceptional lamb rib suyuk (양갈비 수육), a dish praised for its incredibly tender and melt-in-your-mouth texture. The accompanying side dishes, like the Yeosu-style gat-kimchi (갓김치) and house-made pickled garlic scapes, perfectly complement the rich flavors of the suyuk, creating a truly memorable dining experience. Don't miss out on their unique dipping sauce, a savory blend that elevates every bite.

properties.name: 원조호수삼계탕
properties.description: Step into a unique dining space that feels like a collection of interconnected rooms, offering ample seating. A standout feature is the self-serve bar stocked with whole cucumbers, spicy green peppers (cheongyang-gochu, 청양고추), and their signature gochujang (고추장). This special gochujang is a must-try, boasting a perfectly balanced sweet and spicy flavor with a delightful texture, ideal for dipping the fresh vegetables. The restaurant's specialty, Samgyetang (삼계탕), is a rich, thick soup made with perilla seeds (deulkkae, 들깨) and glutinous rice flour, creating a uniquely hearty and creamy broth. The chicken itself is incredibly tender, falling apart with the slightest touch, and is filled with savory glutinous rice.

properties.name: 원강
properties.description: This cozy spot near Nonhyeon Station is a local favorite, especially for lunch. While known for its grilled meats, the real stars are the hearty rice dishes. The signature mu-bap (무밥), a radish rice bowl, is a must-try, featuring tender radish and savory bits of meat, served with delicious side dishes like tofu and radish greens. It's a comforting and satisfying meal that leaves you feeling full but not heavy.

properties.name: Washoku Yein
properties.description: Washoku Yein

properties.name: 온유월식당
properties.description:
Located conveniently near Express Bus Terminal Station, On-yu-wol Sikdang is a must-visit for pork aficionados. This eatery proudly serves pork that has received the prestigious Presidential Award, a testament to its exceptional quality. The Duroc pork, aged using a special low-temperature technique and HACCP certified, boasts a rich, savory flavor that sets it apart from ordinary samgyeopsal (삼겹살). The thick-cut pork belly, or ogyeopsal (오겹살), is a visual and culinary delight, with distinct layers of fat and meat that grill to perfection, offering a deeply satisfying, non-greasy taste.

Beyond the star pork, the restaurant offers unique side dishes. Don't miss the Muk-eunji Yukhoe Guksu (묵은지 육회 국수), a refreshing cold noodle dish with aged kimchi and beef tartare. While the self-serve seasonal greens like spring cabbage (bomdong, 봄동) are a nice touch, be sure to check the self-bar as instructions aren't always explicit. The clean, straightforward interior provides a comfortable setting to savor these high-quality dishes, making it a standout dining experience.

properties.name: Onyva
properties.description: Nestled in the alleys of Apgujeong Rodeo, Onyva offers a refined dining experience helmed by Chef Park Jin-yong, known for his collaborations with Chef Lee Ha-seong of "Black & White Chefs 2." The tasting menu is a journey of delicate flavors and textures, starting with a standout Tofu Puree with Yam, Sweet Shrimp, Lotus Root, and Kelp Powder, praised for its exquisite balance and contrasting textures. Another highlight is the perfectly cooked Tilefish (Okdom, 옥돔) served with a unique Seaweed (Maesaengi, 매생이) oil sauce, offering a fresh take on seafood dishes.

properties.name: Oak Restaurant & Bar
properties.description: Oak Room at the Millennium Hilton Seoul offers a sophisticated buffet experience, perfect for year-end gatherings. The ambiance is elevated, setting it apart from typical buffet restaurants, with a central bar ideal for evening drinks. Guests can enjoy a wide array of dishes, from comforting pumpkin porridge (호박죽) and fresh salads with smoked salmon to made-to-order pasta and a selection of grilled meats like roast beef (로스트 비프) and lamb.

properties.name: 우정양곱창
properties.description: Nestled near Hakdong Station, Woojung Yang Gopchang offers a refined take on Korean barbecue, standing out with its clean and upscale ambiance unlike typical eateries. It's a popular spot, often bustling with patrons, so arriving early or during off-peak hours is recommended to snag a table. The restaurant is recognized for its quality, consistently earning a Blue Ribbon award since 2023, and features a comfortable, well-maintained interior with square tables perfect for dining.

properties.name: 우가
properties.description: Step into W.GaNa, a premier Hanwoo Omakase experience often lauded as one of Seoul's top three. This intimate, reservation-only establishment offers a sophisticated ambiance with soft music, perfect for a refined evening. While the menu features a delicate dance of premium Korean beef (Hanwoo) and seasonal seafood, the star of the show is undoubtedly the exquisite Hanwoo, particularly the tenderloin (Ansim, 안심) and ribeye (Kkotdeungsim, 꽃등심), often elevated with luxurious toppings like caviar and paired with aromatic wild pine mushrooms (Jayeonsan Songi, 자연산 송이).

properties.name: The Original Pancake House
properties.description:
Step into The Original Pancake House, a beloved American diner that has been serving classic breakfast and dinner since 1953. Located near Seoul's vibrant Gwanghwamun and Cheonggyecheon areas, it's a perfect spot for a leisurely brunch with friends. The ambiance is cozy, filled with charming decor, and the menu boasts a wide array of options beyond their famous pancakes, including crepes, omelets, and waffles.

Don't miss their signature Apple Pancakes (애플 팬케이크), generously topped with caramelized apples and a hint of cinnamon, or the rich Egg Benedict with Gouda Cheese Pancakes (에그 베네딕트 고다 치즈 팬케이크). The Taco Salad (타코 샐러드) is also a popular choice, though be mindful of the cilantro in the dressing. With free coffee refills, it's an ideal place to linger and catch up.

properties.name: Oasis
properties.description: Nestled within the Seoul Botanic Park, Oasis is a tranquil haven perfect for a refreshing break after exploring the grounds. The cafe's interior seamlessly blends with the natural surroundings, featuring lush greenery that creates a calm and organized atmosphere. With ample seating, including long communal tables ideal for families or groups, and a convenient kiosk ordering system, Oasis offers a comfortable and efficient experience. Don't miss their signature Strawberry Latte (딸기라떼), a smooth and pleasantly sweet drink, or their delicious cookies, perfect for pairing with a balanced Americano (아메리카노) to reset your palate.

properties.name: 영의정
properties.description: Nestled in Yeouido, Yeonguijeong is a hidden gem offering a refined Hanjeongsik (한정식) experience. This restaurant is perfectly suited for business meetings or company dinners, thanks to its private room (룸) seating. The ambiance is clean and spacious, with comfortable floor seating that allows for extended stays without discomfort. The service is prompt and efficient, ensuring a smooth dining experience from start to finish.

properties.name: 와라쿠
properties.description: Waraku is a distinguished kushiyaki (꼬치구이) specialist, recognized for its authentic Japanese skewers crafted with high-quality ingredients. This spacious establishment offers a refined yet comfortable atmosphere, perfect for enjoying a variety of grilled delights alongside drinks. The menu features expertly prepared yakitori (야키토리) and tsukune (츠쿠네), showcasing a delightful balance of seasoning and tender textures that make for a memorable dining experience.

properties.name: Obaltan
properties.description: Obaltan offers a clean and satisfying grilled beef tripe (yang-gopchang, 양곱창) experience. The star of the show is the signature grilled beef intestines (daechang, 대창), praised for its chewy exterior and tender, melt-in-your-mouth interior. While the portions might seem a bit small for the price, the exceptional texture and flavor of the grilled tripe and intestines, often served with grilled diaphragm (yeomtong, 염통), make it a memorable meal. The restaurant also serves refreshing cold buckwheat noodles (hoenaengmyeon, 회냉면) and a savory soybean paste stew (doenjang jjigae, 된장찌개) to round off your meal.

properties.name: ENTIQUE PATISSERIE
properties.description: Stumble upon Entique Pâtisserie, a charming cafe born from the concept of a small shop selling luxurious gifts. This spot is a delightful discovery, offering a curated selection of meticulously crafted baked goods and desserts. From classic madeleines and financiers to more elaborate creations like the pistachio tart, each item is presented with an elegant simplicity that matches the cafe's clean and inviting interior.

properties.name: A.N.OTHER
properties.description: Nestled near Gangnam-gu Office Station, A.N.OTHER is a stylish cafe with an open, airy feel, thanks to its large windows that connect the interior with the bustling street. It's a great spot to enjoy a coffee while people-watching. The cafe offers a modern ordering experience via a kiosk and features a chic bar seating area alongside a few indoor tables.

properties.name: 안남
properties.description: Nestled near Seoul Samsung Hospital, Annnam offers a bustling Vietnamese dining experience, popular with local office workers during lunch. While the entrance might be a bit tricky to find, the well-organized system with a dedicated host ensures a smooth seating process, even during peak hours. The menu features clear photos, making it easy to choose from a variety of pho, fried rice, and noodle dishes, with set menus also available.

properties.name: Epanoui
properties.description: Epanoui offers a bright and airy French dining experience, perfect for special occasions. The space is bathed in natural light, with art adorning the walls, creating a cheerful ambiance. While the menu is set, guests can choose to add a signature main. The wine list is extensive, featuring a wide selection of French wines, with options for glasses or carafes of house wine. Standout dishes include the scallop quenelle, a flavorful gambas crustacéen, and the crispy-crusted salmon. The highly recommended lobster main, though noted as potentially salty, pairs wonderfully with wine, and the 72-hour slow-cooked beef bourguignon is a rich, satisfying choice.

properties.name: ENSEMBLE
properties.description:
ENSEMBLE is a captivating exhibition that explores the coexistence of diverse selves through the works of four artists with distinct artistic languages and cultural backgrounds. The show delves into the intricate processes of self-creation and deconstruction, weaving together sensations, memories, spaces, images, relationships, and imagination. The intentional arrangement of artworks creates a harmonious yet subtly tense dialogue, inviting viewers to contemplate the dynamic interplay between different entities.

The exhibition features Antoine Carbonne's innovative approach to painting, where his frescoes and site-specific works transcend traditional mediums by occupying entire spaces and telling stories. He experiments with imbuing traditional still lifes, inspired by Joseon Dynasty paintings, with a sense of movement, creating a disorienting yet engaging experience. Sophie Varin's intimate canvases meticulously build narratives that capture the complex emotions and potential tensions within social relationships, leaving viewers in a state of thoughtful ambiguity.

properties.name: 어성일식
properties.description: Securing a reservation at Eoseong Ilsik (어성일식) is a challenge, but the experience is well worth the effort. This popular omakase spot buzzes with activity, operating on a timed reservation system, so arriving early is recommended. While the counter seats offer a dynamic dining experience, the private rooms provide a more intimate and undisturbed meal, perfect for family gatherings. The sashimi omakase, served as a beautifully plated course, showcases incredibly fresh and high-quality fish, with even those who don't typically favor tuna impressed by its vibrant color and taste. Each dish is a delightful surprise, consistently exceeding expectations and leaving diners thoroughly satisfied.

properties.name: OUR bakery
properties.description: This cozy bakery near Bangbae Station is a delightful spot for a sweet treat. The interior is intimate, offering a warm atmosphere perfect for enjoying a pastry. Don't miss their signature "Dirty Choco" (더티초코), a rich chocolate pastry with a moist and soft interior, and the popular "Double Cheese" (더블치즈). They also offer a variety of other tempting baked goods and cakes, making it a great place to grab a bite or a takeaway treat.

properties.name: 암사민물장어
properties.description: This long-standing eel restaurant, operating since 1990, offers a taste of tradition and quality. Conveniently located near Amsa Station and easily accessible by car with complimentary valet parking, it's a perfect spot for family outings. The atmosphere is lively yet comfortable, especially by the windows offering a sense of openness. They pride themselves on using only fresh, domestic freshwater eels, offering three flavors: salt-grilled, soy-glazed, and spicy gochujang.

properties.name: Unknown diner
properties.description: Tucked away in a quiet residential area near Maebong Station in Dogok-dong, Unknown Diner is a hidden gem that lives up to its name. Don't let its unassuming exterior fool you; this Italian restaurant surprises with its exceptional food, ambiance, and wine list. The owner-chef's dedication to fundamental flavors shines through, with dishes that prioritize quality ingredients and well-executed recipes over flashy presentations. The basil pesto pasta with macadamia nuts is a standout, perfectly cooked and harmoniously balanced.

properties.name: 여의도따로국밥
properties.description:
This Yeouido institution offers a nostalgic dining experience, with its interior showing decades of history. It's a no-frills spot, often frequented by taxi drivers, serving up a signature 'ttaro gukbap' (따로국밥) that arrives piping hot and fast. The broth is clear and refreshing, perfect for a hearty meal, though the beef can be a bit on the tougher side, which some find part of its unique charm. 

While the menu has simplified over the years, focusing on its core offerings like the 'ttaro gukbap' and 'dolpan suyuk' (돌판수육), the restaurant maintains a loyal following. It's a place that evokes childhood memories for some and offers a comforting, familiar taste amidst the bustling Yeouido district.

properties.name: 여수오동도
properties.description: Oedongdo Island offers a refreshing escape with its scenic walking paths and stunning ocean views. Visitors can choose to stroll along the picturesque breakwater, rent a bicycle for a leisurely ride, or hop on the charming Dongbaek train (동백열차) for a convenient journey. The island is a popular spot for families and couples, providing a relaxing atmosphere perfect for enjoying the natural beauty and even catching a beautiful sunrise (해돋이).

properties.name: 양마니
properties.description: Yangmani Kalguksu is a cozy, welcoming spot perfect for a warm bowl of noodles, especially on a chilly day. This popular eatery is known for its handmade noodles, with a generous portion of delicious perilla seed kalguksu (들깨 칼국수) that's rich and nutty. Don't miss out on their tender and flavorful suyuk (수육), which is a must-try alongside their hearty noodle dishes. The friendly service and the complimentary boribap (보리밥) add to the comforting, home-style dining experience.

properties.name: 스시스즈메
properties.description: Sushi Suzume, helmed by Chef Choi Sang-hoon of Sushi Uto fame, offers an exquisite omakase experience in Apgujeong. The intimate, 8-seat counter provides a cozy atmosphere perfect for special occasions. Diners rave about the exceptional quality of ingredients, from pristine akami and fatty tuna to expertly prepared appetizers like sea eel (unagi, 장어) and a rich monkfish liver (ankimo, 아귀간) that melts in your mouth. The generous portions and the chef's skillful, attentive service make for a truly memorable meal.

properties.name: 스시사
properties.description:
SUSHISA offers a unique omakase experience, with a surprisingly comfortable atmosphere that deviates from the typical hinoki wood counter. The attentive staff immediately noticed and accommodated a left-handed diner's seating preference, showcasing exceptional service. The meal began with an impressive array of otsumami (appetizers), including abalone, sea bass, and a standout tuna belly cut called sunazuri (스나즈리). The sushi course continued with exquisite pieces like red seabream (붉바리), scallop, and a rich uni (우니) from Hokkaido, culminating in a celebratory sea urchin dish prepared as miyeokguk (미역국) for a birthday.

The dining journey at SUSHISA is described as a continuous flow of deliciousness, leaving guests feeling delighted and deeply satisfied. The presentation is artistic, with even the restroom featuring striking decor. The meal is punctuated by creative dishes like a high-end corn chip and a refreshing seaweed salad. The experience is rounded off with a beautiful, edible cake and a palate-cleansing watermelon sorbet, making for a truly memorable and happy occasion.

properties.name: 시라카와
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Apgujeong Rodeo, Shirakawa Tempura offers a casual izakaya (이자카야) experience with a focus on value-for-money tempura (텐푸라). While not strictly an omakase, their course menu provides a delightful array of dishes, including seven types of tempura, alongside other options like salads, sashimi, and udon (우동). The relaxed atmosphere makes it a great spot to enjoy a variety of Japanese-inspired small plates and expertly fried tempura.

properties.name: 스시쇼우
properties.description: Sushi Shouya offers an omakase-level experience with their 'pan sushi' (판초밥), a welcome addition to the Bangbae-dong dining scene. The intimate bar seating and attentive chef create a personalized atmosphere, reminiscent of a high-end sushi counter. They focus on using quality ingredients, with a special emphasis on aged fish and red vinegar rice (적초샤리), aiming for deep, savory flavors in every piece. The set menu includes a delightful chawanmushi (계란찜) with truffle oil and grated yam, followed by 14 pieces of expertly crafted sushi.

properties.name: SURIN
properties.description: SURIN Han Nam offers an elevated Korean beef (hanwoo, 한우) dining experience, perfect for special occasions and private gatherings. The restaurant boasts a sophisticated ambiance with private rooms, dim lighting, and luxurious interiors, making it an ideal spot for meetings, anniversaries, or intimate celebrations. The highlight is their premium 1++ Korean beef, meticulously prepared through a multi-course dinner that showcases the quality and flavor of each cut, from delicate yukhoe (육회) to a rich truffle sirloin rice (트러플 안심 솥밥).

properties.name: Scopa The Chef
properties.description:
Nestled on a quiet side street in Cheongdam-dong, Scopa The Chef has been a steadfast Italian dining destination since 2015. The restaurant offers a surprisingly open and airy ambiance despite its modest size, enhanced by elegant white tablecloths and fresh flowers – a touch often reserved for fine dining. It's an ideal spot for enjoying Italian cuisine casually, whether for a wine gathering or a relaxed meal with friends. Their unique corkage policy, offering free corkage for bottles ordered from their own wine list, makes it a favorite for wine enthusiasts.

The menu features a delightful array of Italian dishes, with a particular reputation for their pasta. Diners can expect expertly prepared plates, from a refreshing fig, apple, and green grape salad to a rich octopus and shrimp dish over potato puree. The grilled chicken leg is a standout, and the Italian-style beef tartare offers a unique twist. The experience is rounded out with perfectly cooked steaks, making Scopa The Chef a well-rounded choice for a memorable Italian dining experience.

properties.name: SWEETS PLANET
properties.description: This charming cafe, featured on the popular TV show 'Wednesday Food Talk' (수요미식회), offers a relaxed atmosphere perfect for a leisurely afternoon. While not overly crowded, it's a great spot to unwind. The menu highlights their signature roll cakes and éclairs, with popular items like the Earl Grey roll cake (홍차 롤) available at reasonable prices.

properties.name: 아나고야
properties.description: Step into a piece of history at Anagoya, the pioneer that sparked the beloved Anago Alley in Bisan-dong. This spot is renowned for its fresh, wild-caught sea eel (anago, 아나고), a delicacy that's never farmed. Whether you opt for the expertly prepared frozen cuts or the whole, live anago, the experience is a true taste of tradition. The grilled anago, perfectly cooked to retain its moisture, offers a tender and savory bite, with the innards providing a delightful chewy texture. Don't miss the chance to pair your meal with a refreshing glass of Dokdo Soju (독도 소주) and soak in the nostalgic atmosphere.

properties.name: 스시선수
properties.description: Nestled in the upscale Gangnam district, Sushi Seonsu (스시선수) offers a refined omakase experience helmed by Chef Choi Ji-hoon. Known for its appearance on "Wednesday Food Relay" and Michelin Guide recommendations, the restaurant excels at highlighting the natural flavors of premium ingredients. The lunch service, priced around ₩110,000-₩120,000, is particularly praised for its value, featuring expertly crafted sushi like flounder (광어), otoro (오도로), shima-aji (시마아지), and kinmedai (금태) served directly from the counter. The atmosphere is spacious and elegant, with valet parking available, making it a sophisticated choice for sushi aficionados.

properties.name: 신기루황소곱창구이
properties.description:
This popular spot near Seoul National University Station is a must-visit for its incredible value and lively atmosphere. Be prepared to queue, as word has gotten out about this gem! The signature modum gui (모듬구이), a generous platter of assorted grilled intestines, is a visual feast and a true testament to their commitment to quality ingredients. The complimentary sunji haejang-guk (선지해장국) or miyeok-guk (미역국) is a delicious bonus, and the banchan (반찬), especially the seasoned chives, are perfect for complementing the rich flavors of the grilled meats. 

Despite the bustling environment and occasional wait, the friendly service and the sheer deliciousness of the food make it all worthwhile. It's a place where you can truly savor authentic Korean grilled intestines without breaking the bank. Come early, grab a seat, and get ready for a memorable meal!

properties.name: 신사면옥
properties.description: Nestled between Apgujeong Rodeo and Gangnam-gu Office stations, Sinsa Myeonok is a well-established spot that feels like a hidden gem. Despite its unassuming menu, the restaurant boasts a surprisingly diverse lunch selection, making it a fantastic choice for a midday meal. The spicy pork bulgogi (매운 제육 뚝배기) is a standout, packing a flavorful punch, while the unique cheddar cheese pork cutlet (치즈 돈까스 정식) offers a delightful twist on a classic. Don't miss the dumplings (만두) either – they're a perfect accompaniment to the hearty main dishes.

properties.name: 수정
properties.description: Seoul Sujeong Church in Yeongdeungpo is a place of profound spiritual reflection and community support. Visitors often find solace and renewed purpose through the powerful sermons, like the one emphasizing the "go the extra mile" (5리를 가라 하면 10리를 가라) principle. It's a welcoming space for those seeking comfort, strength, and a deeper connection to faith, especially during challenging times.

properties.name: CHACONNE
properties.description: CHACONNE is a highly anticipated new restaurant helmed by Chef Shim Jeong-taek, who honed his skills at Michelin-starred establishments like Quintessence in Tokyo and Mosu Hong Kong. The tasting menu offers a journey through meticulously crafted dishes, starting with a warm, crispy-yet-fluffy fermented shiitake mushroom tart (pyogo tart, 표고 타르트) bursting with savory umami. Subsequent courses showcase a delightful interplay of textures and flavors, from a creamy tomato-watermelon gazpacho with grilled eggplant and sweet shrimp to tender scallops with green peas and a vibrant Timur pepper sauce. The culinary artistry continues with a sweetbread dish featuring mango salad and crispy cornflakes, followed by grilled hair-tail (gaetjangeo, 갯장어) with asparagus and a nuanced Parmigiano cheese sauce.

properties.name: SAGE & BUTTER
properties.description:
Tucked away on a quiet side street in Gangnam, Sage & Butter offers a serene and intimate dining experience. The restaurant's ambiance is thoughtfully curated with soft, ambient lighting and elegant floral arrangements, creating a sophisticated yet cozy atmosphere. Each dish is presented like a work of art, with meticulous attention to color and plating, making for a visually stunning and delightful meal. 

The signature Lemon Shrimp Pasta (레몬 쉬림프 파스타) is a must-try, boasting a rich flavor profile centered around butter and herbs. The Fregola (프레골라), a unique pasta resembling risotto, is served with seasonal ingredients, offering a delightful textural contrast. Complement your meal with their seasonal salads and a selection of wines for a truly memorable culinary journey.

properties.name: 상남스시
properties.description:
Sangnam Sushi is a highly sought-after omakase spot, renowned for its exceptional value and consistently delivering a top-tier dining experience. It's a place that draws dedicated regulars, evidenced by the constant 'reservation wars' to secure a table. Despite long intervals between visits, the quality and overall experience remain unchanged, making it a reliable choice for a memorable meal.

The culinary journey begins with delicate tsumami (츠마미), including comforting abalone porridge (전복죽) and perfectly crisp shrimp balls (새우볼튀김). The nigiri (니기리) selection is where the magic truly happens, with standouts like the incredibly chewy rockfish (능성어), the refreshing sea bream (참돔) with a hint of yuzu, and the rich, melt-in-your-mouth mackerel (고등어). Each piece is a testament to the chef's skill, offering a balanced and flavorful progression that pairs wonderfully with sake.

properties.name: Sunho Coffee
properties.description: Sunho Coffee offers a unique and engaging coffee experience, especially during their special pop-up events. The atmosphere transforms into a captivating performance as baristas meticulously brew drip coffee (드립 커피), turning each cup into a show. Whether you're a regular or a first-time visitor, you'll appreciate the nuanced flavors of their carefully selected beans and the artistry behind each pour. It's a place where the simple act of enjoying coffee becomes a memorable event.

properties.name: 서평면옥
properties.description: Seopyeon Myonok offers a refined dining experience, conveniently located near Boram-dong Hospital Station thanks to the new Sillim Line. The restaurant boasts a diverse menu, featuring both main dishes and an impressive array of side dishes (곁들임). A standout feature is the option to order side dishes in half portions, making it ideal for couples or smaller groups to sample a variety of flavors without overindulging. The subtle, refreshing kimchi and pickled radish are perfect accompaniments to their signature dishes.

properties.name: Sowoori Butcher Restaurant
properties.description: Sowoori Butcher Restaurant

properties.name: 서래오뎅
properties.description: This cozy spot is a must-visit, especially as the weather cools. While it boasts an impressive odeng bar (오뎅바), don't overlook the other delicious dishes that make it a place you'll find yourself craving. The flat odeng (납작한 어묵) is highly recommended, and for a truly satisfying meal, the chicken skin skewers (닭껍질꼬치) and stir-fried chicken feet (닭발볶음) are absolute must-orders. Round off your experience with the seafood tteokbokki (해물떡볶이) or okonomiyaki (오코노미야끼), and finish with the bacon skewers (베이컨꼬치) that come with tomatoes, a surprisingly delightful way to end the meal.

properties.name: Sulnoon
properties.description: Sulnoon (설눈) offers a taste of authentic Pyongyang-style naengmyeon (냉면), a dish reminiscent of the flavors found at Pyongyang's Koryo Hotel. The restaurant is run by a mother-daughter duo, with the mother drawing on her experience at the Koryo Hotel to recreate the traditional recipes. Their signature naengmyeon features noodles made from buckwheat flour from Bongpyeong, mixed with potato starch, and a rich, clear broth simmered for 8 hours with beef, pork, and chicken, then aged for a day. The dish is generously topped with over ten ingredients, including egg garnish, beef, pork, chicken, pickled radish, and cucumber, creating a deep flavor and dark noodle color.

properties.name: 서백자간장게장
properties.description: Nestled near Samseong Station, Seobaekja Ganjang Gejang is a celebrated spot for those seeking the finest soy-marinated crabs (ganjang gejang - 간장게장). This restaurant, recognized in the Blue Ribbon Survey, is known for its incredibly fresh, plump crabs brimming with roe, offering a savory and non-fishy taste that even seasoned connoisseurs rave about. The generous portions, with pre-scored crab legs for easy eating and a treasure trove of roe in the shells, make for a truly satisfying experience. It's a perfect destination for a hearty meal, especially during the autumn crab season, and its elegant ambiance with private rooms makes it ideal for business dinners and gatherings.

properties.name: 삼일정육식당
properties.description: Nestled near Yangjaecheon Stream, Samil Meat Restaurant (삼일정육식당) is a popular spot for both high-quality Korean beef (hanwoo, 한우) and flavorful grilled pork bulgogi (돼지불백). The spacious interior makes it an ideal location for group dinners and company gatherings, with an open kitchen ensuring transparency and a self-serve bar offering unlimited fresh greens. Expect a premium dining experience with excellent cuts like sirloin (deungsim, 등심) and beef brisket (chadolbagi, 차돌박이), known for their superior quality and taste.

properties.name: 삼성원조양곱창
properties.description: A celebrity hotspot in Cheongdam-dong, Samsung Wonjo YangGopchang (삼성원조양곱창) is famous for its grilled intestines, with patrons often spotting famous faces. The space offers a comfortable dining experience with ample table spacing, and the pre-grilled, crispy yet chewy yanggopchang (양곱창) is a must-try. Don't miss the special pickled onion sauce, which perfectly complements the rich flavor of the intestines. For those looking for a hearty meal to go, the beef tripe stew (곱창전골) is available for takeout and is said to be incredibly delicious when prepared at home.

properties.name: 샘밭막국수
properties.description: Nestled in Chuncheon, Sam Bat Makguksu offers a spacious and clean dining experience, with a charming outdoor seating area perfect for enjoying good weather. The menu boasts a variety of options, with all ingredients sourced domestically. Signature dishes include the pure buckwheat makguksu (순메밀 막국수), a subtly bitter noodle dish that's surprisingly smooth, and a delicious half-and-half jeon (전) platter featuring a chewy potato pancake and a crispy mung bean pancake. The bossam (보쌈) is also a highlight, with a perfect balance of tender meat and just the right amount of fat, free from any gamey odors.

properties.name: VINOLOGY
properties.description: Step into Vinology, a chic and sophisticated wine bar perfect for a special evening out. The modern, monochrome interior sets a stylish mood, complemented by a curated playlist that enhances the overall ambiance. It's an ideal spot for date nights or celebrating milestones, offering a refined yet relaxed atmosphere for enjoying good company and conversation.

properties.name: Salon de Noir
properties.description: Salon de Noir is a Mexican bistro offering a diverse menu from tacos to burritos and quesadillas, alongside a remarkable selection of beverages including beer, traditional Korean liquors (traditionally, 전통주), tequila, and wine. Despite its name not immediately suggesting Mexican cuisine, the establishment is helmed by someone with a background in media and extensive experience in wine import and retail, giving it a unique origin story. Known for its generous portions, this spot is perfect for enjoying a casual afternoon beer (낮맥) or a full meal, with prices that are easy on the wallet and an impressive variety of drinks that make it a place worth visiting, even solo.

properties.name: BEFLIQUE
properties.description: BEFLIQUE is a premier Hanwoo (한우) omakase destination in Gangnam, perfect for those seeking a special dining experience. The restaurant boasts a refined ambiance with private rooms, making it ideal for intimate dates, family gatherings, or business meetings. Guests can enjoy a meticulously crafted course menu featuring premium aged Hanwoo, expertly grilled over charcoal by the chefs. The experience is elevated by a curated wine list and the option to bring your own bottle, enhancing the celebratory atmosphere.

properties.name: Villa Guerrero
properties.description: Step into Villa Guerrero, a no-frills taquería that transports you straight to the streets of Mexico. This hidden gem, founded by someone who fell in love with authentic Mexican flavors, focuses solely on delivering genuine taste and value. The vibe is unpretentious, with simple decor that lets the food take center stage. Be prepared for a strict adherence to their seating policy, but the wait is well worth it for a true taste of 'taco' (따꼬).

properties.name: bistro POLPO
properties.description: Nestled in the burgeoning Bangbae Sgil area, known for its artistic vibe and independent shops, Bistro Polpo is a relatively new addition to the neighborhood. This charming bistro is helmed by a duo with a keen eye for ingredients, particularly those from East Asia. The menu offers a delightful fusion, with standout dishes like the intensely flavored truffle oil steamed egg (트뤼플 오일 계란찜) and the refreshing coriander salad (고수 샐러드) that proved so popular it caused a friendly dispute. For pasta lovers, the al dente tagliatelle bolognese (볼로네제 딸리아텔레) and the fresh anchovy spaghetti (생멸치 스파게티) are highly recommended.

properties.name: Brick Oven
properties.description: Brick Oven is a long-standing New York-style pizza joint in the heart of Gangnam, serving up authentic pies since 2009. They boast a thin, crispy crust baked in a 550-degree Fahrenheit brick oven, topped with a generous array of fresh ingredients. The "New Yorker" pizza offers a classic combination of beef, sausage, pepperoni, and vegetables, while the "Spicy Chicken" provides a flavorful kick with spicy tomato sauce and jalapeños. The ability to order half-and-half pizzas makes it easy to sample their diverse offerings.

properties.name: 산호
properties.description: A popular ramen spot near Boramdae Station, Sanho Ki is known for its signature mazesoba (마제소바). Be prepared for a wait, as the cozy interior often leads to queues, especially during peak hours. The menu also features tonkotsu ramen (돈코츠 라멘) and a unique basil ramen, with clear explanations available at the kiosk for easy ordering. While the wait for your food might be a bit long due to the solo operation, the delicious mazesoba is worth the anticipation.

properties.name: Bread Fit
properties.description:
Nestled in Yeouido, Bread Fit is a beloved local bakery that has won over the hearts of the financial district's busy professionals. This charming spot offers a delightful array of meticulously crafted pastries alongside expertly brewed specialty coffee. Despite a lingering notion that men prefer rice over bread, the patrons here, often seen enjoying bags of their signature milk cream bread (우유크림빵), are proving this stereotype wrong. Even with a slight grumble about prices, the consistent quality and delicious offerings keep them coming back for their daily dose of bread and coffee.

Bread Fit prides itself on using only the finest ingredients, with imported flours and premium domestic produce, olives, chocolate, and butter. Their commitment to freshness is evident in their daily baked goods, made without preservatives or artificial additives. The bakery also shines with its in-house roasted specialty coffee, featuring a unique daily selection of single-origin beans. The owner's passion extends to sharing the stories behind each coffee and pastry, creating a truly engaging experience enhanced by warm lighting and a welcoming atmosphere.

properties.name: 부산양곱창
properties.description: This beloved spot, Busan Yang Gopchang (부산양곱창), is a long-standing favorite for its exceptional beef intestines.
